namespace Chapter9

open Set

/-- Proposition 9.8.3 / Exercise 9.8.4 -/
theorem MonotoneOn.exist_inverse {a b:ℝ} (h: a < b) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (hcont: ContinuousOn f (.Icc a b)) (hmono: StrictMonoOn f (.Icc a b)) :
  f '' (.Icc a b) = .Icc (f a) (f b) ∧
  ∃ finv: ℝ → ℝ, ContinuousOn finv (.Icc (f a) (f b)) ∧ StrictMonoOn finv (.Icc (f a) (f b)) ∧
  finv '' (.Icc (f a) (f b)) = .Icc a b ∧
  (∀ x ∈ Set.Icc a b, finv (f x) = x) ∧
  ∀ y ∈ Set.Icc (f a) (f b), f (finv y) = y
   := by
  sorry

end Chapter9

/-- Lemma 7.1.4(a) / Exercise 7.1.1 -/
theorem concat_finite_series {m n p:ℤ} (hmn: m ≤ n+1) (hpn : n ≤ p) (a: ℤ → ℝ) :
  ∑ i ∈ Icc m n, a i + ∑ i ∈ Icc (n+1) p, a i = ∑ i ∈ Icc m p, a i := by sorry

end Finset

/-!
Self-contained extraction for Chapter 11.4 target theorem IntegrableOn.add
Definitions are copied verbatim from earlier sections as needed.
-/

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter11
open BoundedInterval

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ' {f:ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval} (_:PiecewiseConstantOn f I) := integ f I

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter9
/-- Definition 9.6.1 -/
abbrev BddAboveOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, f x ≤ M

abbrev BddBelowOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, -M ≤ f x

abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M
end Chapter9

namespace Chapter11
open BoundedInterval Chapter9

/-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

/-- Definition 11.3.2 (Uppper and lower Riemann integrals )-/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Definition 11.3.4 (Riemann integral)
As we permit junk values, the simplest definition for the Riemann integral is the upper integral.-/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter11
open Chapter9

/-- Theorem 11.4.1(a) / Exercise 11.4.1 -/
theorem IntegrableOn.add {I: BoundedInterval} {f g:ℝ → ℝ} (hf: IntegrableOn f I) (hg: IntegrableOn g I) :
  IntegrableOn (f + g) I ∧ integ (f + g) I = integ f I + integ g I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := union_pair

instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := {x} ∪ X

/-- Exercise 3.1.1 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.pair_eq_pair {a b c d:Object} (h: ({a,b}:Set) = {c,d}) :
    a = c ∧ b = d ∨ a = d ∧ b = c := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic

/--
  This definition needs to be made outside of the Section 4.3 namespace for technical reasons.
-/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε


namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

/--
If `a : ℕ → ℚ` is used in a context where a `Sequence` is expected, automatically coerce `a` to `Sequence.ofNatFun a` (which will be pretty-printed as `↑a`)
-/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

/-- Auxiliary lemma to help aesop discharge `vanish` in `CauchySequence.mk'`. -/
lemma Aux_vanish_ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : ∀ n, n < 0 → ((a : Sequence).seq n) = 0 := by
  intro n h
  change (Sequence.ofNatFun a).seq n = 0
  simp [Sequence.ofNatFun, not_le.mpr h]

attribute [aesop safe apply] Aux_vanish_ofNatFun

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.1.3 - definition of ε-steadiness for a sequence starting at 0 (generalized to any n₀) -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

/-
Equivalent/Eventually close sequences (Section 5.2)
-/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

/-- A sequence starting at zero that is Cauchy, can be viewed as a Cauchy sequence.-/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy -/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/--
  It is convenient in Lean to assign the "dummy" value of 0 to `LIM a` when `a` is not Cauchy.
  This requires Classical logic, because the property of being Cauchy is not computable or
  decidable.
-/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

/--
  Definition 5.4.1 (sequences bounded away from zero with sign). Sequences are indexed to start
  from zero as this is more convenient for Mathlib purposes.
-/
abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

/-- Definition 5.4.1 (sequences bounded away from zero with sign). -/
abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Proposition 5.4.4 (basic properties of positive reals) / Exercise 5.4.1 -/
theorem Real.trichotomous (x:Real) : x = 0 ∨ x.IsPos ∨ x.IsNeg := by sorry

end Chapter5

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 9.3, containing the core definitions used
by the target theorem and the target theorem itself.
-/

/-- Definition 9.1.5.  Note that a slightly different `Real.adherent` was defined in Chapter 6.4 -/
abbrev Real.adherent' (ε:ℝ) (x:ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) := ∃ y ∈ X, |x - y| ≤ ε

/-- Definition 9.3.1 -/
abbrev Real.CloseFn (ε:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ X, |f x - L| < ε

/-- Definition 9.3.3 -/
abbrev Real.CloseNear (ε:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ δ > 0, ε.CloseFn (X ∩ .Ioo (x₀-δ) (x₀+δ)) f L

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.1.-/
abbrev AdherentPt (x:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) := ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.adherent' x X

/-- Definition 9.3.6 (Convergence of functions at a point)-/
abbrev Convergesto (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.CloseNear X f L x₀

/-- Proposition 9.3.14 (Limit laws for functions) / Exercise 9.3.2 -/
theorem Convergesto.max {E:Set ℝ} {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {L M:ℝ} {x₀:ℝ} (h: AdherentPt x₀ E)
  (hf: Convergesto E f L x₀) (hg: Convergesto E g M x₀) :
  Convergesto E (max f g) (max L M) x₀ := by
    sorry

end Chapter9

import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Set.Intervals.Basic
import Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Basic

open scoped BigOperators

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals (Ioo/Icc/Ioc/Ico) -/ 
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- Coercion to sets of real numbers -/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

/-- Membership: `x ∈ I` means `x` in the coerced set of `I`. -/
instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I : Set ℝ)

/-- Inclusion relation between bounded intervals (as sets). -/
instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

/-- Endpoints of a bounded interval -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

/-- Length of a bounded interval -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

/-- Partitions of bounded intervals -/
@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Constant functions on types -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

/-- Constant-on for functions on subsets of ℝ -/
abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Piecewise-constant relative to a fixed partition -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Piecewise-constant on a bounded interval -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Integral of a piecewise constant function with respect to a partition -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Integral of a piecewise-constant function on a bounded interval -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

open PiecewiseConstantOn

/-- Theorem 11.2.16 (d) (Laws of integration) / Exercise 11.2.4 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.integ_of_nonneg {f: ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval} (h: ∀ x ∈ I, 0 ≤ f x)
  (hf: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) :
  0 ≤ integ f I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-! Self-contained slice for Chapter 3 Section 3.5 target theorem -/

namespace Chapter3

/- Auxiliary: provide a simple `grind` tactic aliasing to `aesop` to match the textbook usage. -/
macro "grind" : tactic => `(tactic| aesop)
macro "grind" "[" args:sepBy(tactic, ",") "]" : tactic => `(tactic| aesop)

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Axiom 3.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (sets are objects) -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Coerce a `Set` to a type via its subtype of elements. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x : Object ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

/-- Provide union notation for our `Set`. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

/-- Provide singleton and insert notation for our `Set`. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where singleton := SetTheory.singleton
instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where insert x X := {x} ∪ X

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair). -/
@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst: Object
  snd: Object

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair) -/
@[simp]
theorem OrderedPair.eq (x y x' y' : Object) :
    (⟨ x, y ⟩ : OrderedPair) = (⟨ x', y' ⟩ : OrderedPair) ↔ x = x' ∧ y = y' := by aesop

/-- Helper lemma for Exercise 3.5.1 -/
lemma SetTheory.Set.pair_eq_singleton_iff {a b c: Object} : {a, b} = ({c}: Set) ↔
    a = c ∧ b = c := by
  sorry

/-- Exercise 3.5.1, first part -/
def OrderedPair.toObject : OrderedPair ↪ Object where
  toFun p := ({ (({p.fst}:Set):Object), (({p.fst, p.snd}:Set):Object) }:Set)
  inj' := by sorry

instance OrderedPair.inst_coeObject : Coe OrderedPair Object where
  coe := OrderedPair.toObject

/-- A convenience abbreviation mirroring the textbook's `replace`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A : Set) {P : A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

/-- A technical operation used to define the Cartesian product. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.slice (x:Object) (Y:Set) : Set :=
  Y.replace (P := fun y z ↦ z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) (by grind)

/-- Definition 3.5.4 (Cartesian product) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.cartesian (X Y:Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (X.replace (P := fun x z ↦ z = slice x Y) (by intro _ _ _ ⟨h1, h2⟩; exact h1.trans h2.symm))

-- Provide the ×ˢ notation via SProd as in the source.
instance SetTheory.Set.inst_SProd : SProd Set Set Set where
  sprod := SetTheory.Set.cartesian

open SetTheory.Set

/-- Exercise 3.5.4 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.union_prod (A B C:Set) : (A ∪ B) ×ˢ C = (A ×ˢ C) ∪ (B ×ˢ C) := by sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic

/--
  This definition needs to be made outside of the Section 4.3 namespace for technical reasons.
-/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

/--
If `a : ℕ → ℚ` is used in a context where a `Sequence` is expected, automatically coerce `a` to `Sequence.ofNatFun a` (which will be pretty-printed as `↑a`)
-/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

/--
A slight generalization of Definition 5.1.3 - definition of ε-steadiness for a sequence with an
arbitrary starting point n₀
-/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- A notion of Cauchy sequences (from Section 5.1) -/
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

/-- Definition 9.3.1 -/
abbrev Real.CloseFn (ε:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ X, |f x - L| < ε

/-- Definition 9.3.3 -/
abbrev Real.CloseNear (ε:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ δ > 0, ε.CloseFn (X ∩ Set.Ioo (x₀-δ) (x₀+δ)) f L

/-- Definition 9.1.5.  Note that a slightly different `Real.adherent` was defined in Chapter 6.4 -/
abbrev Real.adherent' (ε:ℝ) (x:ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) := ∃ y ∈ X, |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.1.-/
abbrev AdherentPt (x:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) := ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.adherent' x X

/-- Definition 9.3.6 (Convergence of functions at a point)-/
abbrev Convergesto (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.CloseNear X f L x₀

/-- Proposition 9.3.14 (Limit laws for functions) / Exercise 9.3.2 -/
theorem Convergesto.min {E:Set ℝ} {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {L M:ℝ} {x₀:ℝ} (h: AdherentPt x₀ E)
  (hf: Convergesto E f L x₀) (hg: Convergesto E g M x₀) :
  Convergesto E (min f g) (min L M) x₀ := by
    sorry

end Chapter9

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

namespace SetTheory.Set

/-- Provide `{x}` notation for singleton sets. -/
instance instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

/-- Provide `X ∪ Y` notation for union of two sets. -/
instance instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

/-- Provide `insert x X` to build finite sets `{x} ∪ X`. -/
instance instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := {x} ∪ X

/-- Subtype of elements of a set (used heavily). -/
abbrev toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

/-- Allow treating a set as a type of its elements. -/
instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := toSubtype A

/-- A convenient abbreviation for the specification axiom. -/
abbrev specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set :=
  SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Set difference `X \ Y` via specification. -/
instance instSDiff : SDiff Set where
  sdiff X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∉ Y)

/-- Replacement packaged for the `Set` namespace. -/
abbrev replace (A : Set) {P : A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

@[simp]
lemma replacement_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') (y:Object) :
    y ∈ A.replace hP ↔ ∃ x, P x y := SetTheory.replacement_axiom A P hP y

/-- Axiom 3.12 (Union) -/
@[simp]
lemma union_axiom (A: Set) (x:Object) :
    x ∈ SetTheory.union A ↔ ∃ (S:Set), x ∈ S ∧ (S:Object) ∈ A := SetTheory.union_axiom A x

end SetTheory.Set

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair). -/
@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst : Object
  snd : Object

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair) -/
@[simp]
theorem OrderedPair.eq (x y x' y' : Object) :
    (⟨ x, y ⟩ : OrderedPair) = (⟨ x', y' ⟩ : OrderedPair) ↔ x = x' ∧ y = y' := by
  sorry

/-- Exercise 3.5.1, first part -/
def OrderedPair.toObject : OrderedPair ↪ Object where
  toFun p := ({ (({p.fst}:Set):Object), (({p.fst, p.snd}:Set):Object) }:Set)
  inj' := by sorry

instance OrderedPair.inst_coeObject : Coe OrderedPair Object where
  coe := OrderedPair.toObject

-- A very small "grind" tactic stub to support uses like `by grind` in uniqueness proofs.
open Lean Elab Tactic in
elab "grind" : tactic => do
  evalTactic (← `(tactic| first | intros; aesop | aesop))

namespace SetTheory.Set

/-- A technical operation, turning an object `x` and a set `Y` to a set `{x} × Y`. -/
abbrev slice (x:Object) (Y:Set) : Set :=
  Y.replace (P := fun y z ↦ z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) (by grind)

@[simp]
theorem mem_slice (x z:Object) (Y:Set) :
    z ∈ (slice x Y) ↔ ∃ y:Y, z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair) := replacement_axiom _ _

/-- Definition 3.5.4 (Cartesian product) -/
abbrev cartesian (X Y:Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (X.replace (P := fun x z ↦ z = slice x Y) (by intro _ _ _ ⟨h1, h2⟩; exact h1.trans h2.symm))

end SetTheory.Set

-- Provide the ×ˢ notation for our cartesian product.
infixl:70 " ×ˢ " => Chapter3.SetTheory.Set.cartesian

namespace Chapter3
open SetTheory SetTheory.Set

/-- Exercise 3.5.4 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.prod_diff (A B C:Set) : A ×ˢ (B \ C) = (A ×ˢ B) \ (A ×ˢ C) := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]

/--

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where

  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

abbrev Sequence.BoundedBy (a:Sequence) (M:ℚ) : Prop := ∀ n, |a n| ≤ M

/-- Definition 5.1.12 (bounded sequences) -/
lemma Sequence.boundedBy_def (a:Sequence) (M:ℚ) : a.BoundedBy M ↔ ∀ n, |a n| ≤ M := by rfl

abbrev Sequence.IsBounded (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∃ M ≥ 0, a.BoundedBy M

/-- Definition 5.1.12 (bounded sequences) -/
lemma Sequence.isBounded_def (a:Sequence) : a.IsBounded ↔ ∃ M ≥ 0, a.BoundedBy M := by rfl

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

    (a:Sequence).IsBounded ↔ (b:Sequence).IsBounded := by sorry

/--
  Definition 7.2.1 (Formal infinite series). This is similar to Chapter 6 sequence, but is

-/
@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Definition 7.2.2 (Convergence of series) -/
abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop := ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

abbrev Series.diverges (s : Series) : Prop := ¬s.converges

Self-contained extraction for Chapter 6 sequences and the target theorem from Section 6.6.
-/

/- Definition 6.1.1 (Distance / ε-close on ℝ). -/
abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

/-- Definition 6.1.2 (ε-close). -/
theorem Real.close_def (ε x y : ℝ) : ε.Close x y ↔ dist x y ≤ ε := by rfl

namespace Chapter6

/--
  Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). This is similar to the Chapter 5 sequence, except that now the
  sequence is real-valued. As with Chapter 5, we start sequences from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h: n ≥ m) :
    (Sequence.mk' m a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℝ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by simp

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {m₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ m₁) :
  (a.from m₁) n = a n := by
  simp [hn]; intros; symm; solve_by_elim [a.vanish]

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
abbrev Real.CloseSeq (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
theorem Real.closeSeq_def (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  ε.CloseSeq a L ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.m, dist (a n) L ≤ ε := by rfl

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
abbrev Real.EventuallyClose (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
theorem Real.eventuallyClose_def (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  ε.EventuallyClose a L ↔ ∃ N, (N ≥ a.m) ∧ ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L := by rfl

namespace Chapter6

abbrev Sequence.TendsTo (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

theorem Sequence.tendsTo_def (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  a.TendsTo L ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L := by rfl

end Chapter6

namespace Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.6.1 -/
abbrev Sequence.subseq (a b: ℕ → ℝ) : Prop := ∃ f : ℕ → ℕ, StrictMono f ∧ ∀ n, b n = a (f n)

/-- Proposition 6.6.5 / Exercise 6.6.4 -/
theorem Sequence.convergent_iff_subseq (a:ℕ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) :
    (a:Sequence).TendsTo L ↔ ∀ b:ℕ → ℝ, Sequence.subseq a b → (b:Sequence).TendsTo L := by
  sorry

end Chapter6

namespace Chapter7

open BigOperators

@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as series. -/
instance Series.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Series where
  coe := fun a ↦ {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind
  }

@[simp]
theorem Series.eval_coe (a: ℕ → ℝ) (n: ℕ) : (a: Series).seq n = a n := by simp

abbrev Series.mk' {m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Series where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/-- Definition 7.2.2 (Convergence of series) -/
abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop := ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

abbrev Series.diverges (s : Series) : Prop := ¬s.converges

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev Series.sum (s : Series) : ℝ := if h : s.converges then h.choose else 0

/-- Absolute value of a series (termwise). -/
abbrev Series.abs (s:Series) : Series := mk' (m:=s.m) (fun n ↦ |s.seq n|)

abbrev Series.absConverges (s:Series) : Prop := s.abs.converges

/-- Exercise 7.4.2 : reprove Proposition 7.4.3 using Proposition 7.41, Proposition 7.2.14,
    and expressing `a n` as the difference of `a n + |a n|` and `|a n|`. -/
 theorem Series.absConverges_of_permute' {a:ℕ → ℝ} (ha : (a:Series).absConverges)
  {f: ℕ → ℕ} (hf: Function.Bijective f) :
    (fun n ↦ a (f n):Series).absConverges  ∧ (a:Series).sum = (fun n ↦ a (f n):Series).sum := by
  sorry

end Chapter7

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

instance SetTheory.Set.instIntersection : Inter Set where
  inter X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∈ Y)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') (y:Object) :
    y ∈ A.replace hP ↔ ∃ x, P x y := SetTheory.replacement_axiom A P hP y

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := {x} ∪ X

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair). -/
@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst: Object
  snd: Object

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair) -/
@[simp]
theorem OrderedPair.eq (x y x' y' : Object) :
    (⟨ x, y ⟩ : OrderedPair) = (⟨ x', y' ⟩ : OrderedPair) ↔ x = x' ∧ y = y' := by aesop

/-- Exercise 3.5.1, first part -/
def OrderedPair.toObject : OrderedPair ↪ Object where
  toFun p := ({ (({p.fst}:Set):Object), (({p.fst, p.snd}:Set):Object) }:Set)
  inj' := by sorry

instance OrderedPair.inst_coeObject : Coe OrderedPair Object where
  coe := OrderedPair.toObject

abbrev SetTheory.Set.slice (x:Object) (Y:Set) : Set :=
  Y.replace (P := fun y z ↦ z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) (by grind)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.cartesian (X Y:Set) : Set :=
  union (X.replace (P := fun x z ↦ z = slice x Y) (by intro _ _ _ ⟨h1, h2⟩; exact h1.trans h2.symm))

scoped infixr:67 " ×ˢ " => SetTheory.Set.cartesian

/-- Exercise 3.5.4 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.prod_inter (A B C:Set) : A ×ˢ (B ∩ C) = (A ×ˢ B) ∩ (A ×ˢ C) := by sorry

end Chapter3

/-- Lemma 7.5.2 / Exercise 7.5.1 -/
theorem Series.ratio_ineq {c:ℤ → ℝ} (m:ℤ) (hpos: ∀ n ≥ m, c n > 0) :
  atTop.liminf (fun n ↦ ((c (n+1) / c n:ℝ):EReal)) ≤
    atTop.liminf (fun n ↦ ↑((c n)^(1/(n:ℝ)):ℝ))
  ∧ atTop.liminf (fun n ↦ (((c n)^(1/(n:ℝ)):ℝ):EReal)) ≤
    atTop.limsup (fun n ↦ ↑((c n)^(1/(n:ℝ)):ℝ))
  ∧ atTop.limsup (fun n ↦ (((c n)^(1/(n:ℝ)):ℝ):EReal)) ≤
    atTop.limsup (fun n ↦ ↑(c (n+1) / c n:ℝ))
    := by
  sorry

theorem _root_.HasDerivWithinAt.of_div {X: Set ℝ} {x₀ f'x₀ g'x₀: ℝ}
  {f g: ℝ → ℝ} (hgx₀ : g x₀ ≠ 0) (hf: HasDerivWithinAt f f'x₀ X x₀)
  (hg: HasDerivWithinAt g g'x₀ X x₀) :
  HasDerivWithinAt (f / g) ((f'x₀ * (g x₀) - (f x₀) * g'x₀) / (g x₀)^2) X x₀ := by
  sorry

Self-contained extraction for Chapter 11 (pieces needed for Section 11.2 target theorem).

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals (Section 11.1). -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- Coercion of a bounded interval to a set. -/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

open Classical in
/-- This is to make Finsets of BoundedIntervals work properly -/
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval := instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

/-- Theorem 11.2.16 (c) (Laws of integration) / Exercise 11.2.4 -/
 theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.integ_sub {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval}
  (hf: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) (hg: PiecewiseConstantOn g I) :
  integ (f - g) I = integ f I - integ g I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

/-!
# Analysis I, Section 9.3: Limiting values of functions (extracted slice)

This file extracts the minimal context needed to state and typecheck the target theorem
`Chapter9.Convergesto.smul` from Section 9.3, together with the supporting definitions.
We copy the relevant declarations verbatim from the textbook source files.
-/

/-- Definition 9.3.1 -/
abbrev Real.CloseFn (ε:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ X, |f x - L| < ε

/-- Definition 9.3.3 -/
abbrev Real.CloseNear (ε:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ δ > 0, ε.CloseFn (X ∩ .Ioo (x₀-δ) (x₀+δ)) f L

/-- Definition 9.1.5.  Note that a slightly different `Real.adherent` was defined in Chapter 6.4 -/
abbrev Real.adherent' (ε:ℝ) (x:ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) := ∃ y ∈ X, |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.1.-/
abbrev AdherentPt (x:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) := ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.adherent' x X

/-- Definition 9.3.6 (Convergence of functions at a point)-/
abbrev Convergesto (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.CloseNear X f L x₀

/-- Proposition 9.3.14 (Limit laws for functions) / Exercise 9.3.2 -/
theorem Convergesto.smul {E:Set ℝ} {f: ℝ → ℝ} {L:ℝ} {x₀:ℝ} (h: AdherentPt x₀ E)
  (hf: Convergesto E f L x₀) (c:ℝ) :
  Convergesto E (c • f) (c * L) x₀ := by
    sorry

/-- Distance-based closeness on ℝ -/
abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

namespace Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). -/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h: n ≥ m) :
    (Sequence.mk' m a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℝ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by simp

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
abbrev Real.CloseSeq (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
abbrev Real.EventuallyClose (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

namespace Chapter6

abbrev Sequence.TendsTo (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

end Chapter6

/-- From Section 6.4: adherence notions for sequences -/
abbrev Real.Adherent (ε:ℝ) (a:Chapter6.Sequence) (x:ℝ) := ∃ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) x

abbrev Real.ContinuallyAdherent (ε:ℝ) (a:Chapter6.Sequence) (x:ℝ) :=
  ∀ N ≥ a.m, ε.Adherent (a.from N) x

namespace Chapter6

abbrev Sequence.LimitPoint (a:Sequence) (x:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.ContinuallyAdherent a x

/-- Definition 6.6.1 -/
abbrev Sequence.subseq (a b: ℕ → ℝ) : Prop := ∃ f : ℕ → ℕ, StrictMono f ∧ ∀ n, b n = a (f n)

/-- Proposition 6.6.6 / Exercise 6.6.5 -/
theorem Sequence.limit_point_iff_subseq (a:ℕ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) :
    (a:Sequence).LimitPoint L ↔ ∃ b:ℕ → ℝ, Sequence.subseq a b ∧ (b:Sequence).TendsTo L := by
  sorry

end Chapter6

abbrev Real.CloseFn (ε:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ x ∈ X, |f x - L| < ε

/-- Definition 9.3.3 -/
abbrev Real.CloseNear (ε:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (L:ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ δ > 0, ε.CloseFn (X ∩ .Ioo (x₀-δ) (x₀+δ)) f L

/-- Definition 9.1.5.  Note that a slightly different `Real.adherent` was defined in Chapter 6.4 -/
abbrev Real.adherent' (ε:ℝ) (x:ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) := ∃ y ∈ X, |x - y| ≤ ε

/-- Proposition 9.3.14 (Limit laws for functions) / Exercise 9.3.2 -/
theorem Convergesto.mul {E:Set ℝ} {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {L M:ℝ} {x₀:ℝ} (h: AdherentPt x₀ E)
  (hf: Convergesto E f L x₀) (hg: Convergesto E g M x₀) :

    sorry

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := {x} ∪ X

instance SetTheory.Set.instSDiff : SDiff Set where
  sdiff X Y := SetTheory.Set.specify X (fun x ↦ x.val ∉ Y)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

structure OrderedPair where
  fst: Object
  snd: Object

@[simp]
theorem OrderedPair.eq (x y x' y' : Object) :
    (⟨ x, y ⟩ : OrderedPair) = (⟨ x', y' ⟩ : OrderedPair) ↔ x = x' ∧ y = y' := by
  aesop

/-- Exercise 3.5.1, first part -/
def OrderedPair.toObject : OrderedPair ↪ Object where
  toFun p := ({ (({p.fst}:Set):Object), (({p.fst, p.snd}:Set):Object) }:Set)
  inj' := by sorry

instance OrderedPair.inst_coeObject : Coe OrderedPair Object where
  coe := OrderedPair.toObject

-- A lightweight stand-in for the tactic used in the textbook code
macro "grind" : tactic => `(tactic| aesop)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.slice (x:Object) (Y:Set) : Set :=
  Y.replace (P := fun y z ↦ z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) (by grind)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.cartesian (X Y:Set) : Set :=
  union (X.replace (P := fun x z ↦ z = slice x Y) (by intro _ _ _ ⟨h1, h2⟩; exact h1.trans h2.symm))

instance SetTheory.Set.inst_SProd : SProd Set Set Set where
  sprod := SetTheory.Set.cartesian

/-- Exercise 3.5.4 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.diff_prod (A B C:Set) : (A \ B) ×ˢ C = (A ×ˢ C) \ (B ×ˢ C) := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

/-- Theorem 11.2.16 (e) (Laws of integration) / Exercise 11.2.4 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.integ_mono {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval} (h: ∀ x ∈ I, f x ≤ g x)
  (hf: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) (hg: PiecewiseConstantOn g I) :
  integ f I ≤ integ g I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

/-!
Self-contained extraction for Chapter 3 Section 3.4 target theorem.
Only the minimal transitive dependencies required for the statement
to parse and typecheck are included.
-/

-- Provide a lightweight alias for the custom tactic used in the textbook source.
macro "grind" : tactic => `(tactic| aesop)

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms. -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset : Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X : Set) (Y : Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X : Set) (Y : Set) (F : Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f : Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq_iff (X Y:Set) : (X: Object) = (Y: Object) ↔  X = Y := by
  constructor
  · intro h; exact SetTheory.set_to_object.inj' h
  · rintro rfl; rfl

/-- Axiom 3.2 (Equality of sets).  The `[ext]` tag allows the `ext` tactic to work for sets. -/
@[ext]
theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

/-- Axiom 3.3 (empty set). -/
@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.not_mem_empty : ∀ x, x ∉ (∅:Set) := SetTheory.emptyset_mem

/-- Lemma 3.1.5 (Single choice) -/
lemma SetTheory.Set.nonempty_def {X:Set} (h: X ≠ ∅) : ∃ x, x ∈ X := by
  -- This proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
  by_contra! this
  have claim (x:Object) : x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ (∅:Set) := by simp [this, not_mem_empty]
  apply ext at claim
  contradiction

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

/-- Axiom 3.6 (axiom of specification) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Axiom 3.6 (axiom of specification) -/
@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.specification_axiom' {A:Set} (P: A → Prop) (x:A) :
    x.val ∈ A.specify P ↔ P x :=
  (SetTheory.specification_axiom A P).2 x

/-- Set difference instance (Definition 3.1.26). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSDiff : SDiff Set where
  sdiff X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∉ Y)

/-- Axiom 3.7 (Axiom of replacement) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

/-- Indexed union (Section 3.4). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.iUnion (I: Set) (A: I → Set) : Set :=
  union (I.replace (P := fun α S ↦ S = A α) (by grind))

/-- A nonempty choice (Section 3.4). -/
noncomputable abbrev SetTheory.Set.nonempty_choose {I:Set} (hI: I ≠ ∅) : I :=
  ⟨(SetTheory.Set.nonempty_def hI).choose, (SetTheory.Set.nonempty_def hI).choose_spec⟩

/-- Indexed intersection primitive (Section 3.4). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.iInter' (I:Set) (β:I) (A: I → Set) : Set :=
  (A β).specify (P := fun x ↦ ∀ α:I, x.val ∈ A α)

/-- Indexed intersection (Section 3.4). -/
noncomputable abbrev SetTheory.Set.iInter (I: Set) (hI: I ≠ ∅) (A: I → Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.Set.iInter' I (SetTheory.Set.nonempty_choose hI) A

/-- Exercise 3.4.11 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.compl_iInter {X I: Set} (hI: I ≠ ∅) (A: I → Set) :
    X \ iInter I hI A = iUnion I (fun α ↦ X \ A α) := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := set_to_object X

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := union_pair

instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := {x} ∪ X

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

instance SetTheory.Object.instNatCast : NatCast Object where
  natCast n := (n:Nat).val

instance SetTheory.Object.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Object n where
  ofNat := ((n:Nat):Object)

  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)

  nat : Set

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 11 Sections 11.1–11.2, sufficient for
`PiecewiseConstantOn.min`.
-/

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Lemma 11.2.8 / Exercise 11.2.2 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.min {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval}
  (hf: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) (hg: PiecewiseConstantOn g I) : PiecewiseConstantOn (min f g) I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11
