/-! Self-contained slice for Chapter 3, Section 3.5 target theorem -/

namespace Chapter3

/- From Section 3.1: core set-theory class and basic instances -/

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Objects can be members of sets. -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Sets are also objects. -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Extensionality for sets. -/
@[ext]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

-- Abbreviations and basic coercions to work with subtypes of objects in a set
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

instance SetTheory.Set.instIntersection : Inter Set where
  inter X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∈ Y)

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := {x} ∪ X

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') (y:Object) :
    y ∈ A.replace hP ↔ ∃ x, P x y := SetTheory.replacement_axiom A P hP y

/-- Alias for the axiom of union. -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.union_axiom (A: Set) (x:Object) :
    x ∈ SetTheory.union A ↔ ∃ (S:Set), x ∈ S ∧ (S:Object) ∈ A := SetTheory.union_axiom A x

/- From Section 3.5: ordered pairs and cartesian product -/

namespace OrderedPair

@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst: Object
  snd: Object

end OrderedPair

open OrderedPair

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair). -/
@[simp]
 theorem OrderedPair.eq (x y x' y' : Object) :
    (⟨ x, y ⟩ : OrderedPair) = (⟨ x', y' ⟩ : OrderedPair) ↔ x = x' ∧ y = y' := by aesop

/-- Exercise 3.5.1, first part -/
def OrderedPair.toObject : OrderedPair ↪ Object where
  toFun p := ({ (({p.fst}:Set):Object), (({p.fst, p.snd}:Set):Object) }:Set)
  inj' := by sorry

instance OrderedPair.inst_coeObject : Coe OrderedPair Object where
  coe := toObject

namespace SetTheory.Set

/-- A technical operation used to define cartesian product. -/
abbrev slice (x:Object) (Y:Set) : Set :=
  Y.replace (P := fun y z ↦ z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) (by grind)

@[simp]
 theorem mem_slice (x z:Object) (Y:Set) :
    z ∈ (slice x Y) ↔ ∃ y:Y, z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair) := replacement_axiom _ _

/-- Cartesian product -/
abbrev cartesian (X Y:Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (X.replace (P := fun x z ↦ z = slice x Y) (by intro _ _ _ ⟨h1, h2⟩; exact h1.trans h2.symm))

/-- This instance enables the ×ˢ notation for Cartesian product. -/
instance inst_SProd : SProd Set Set Set where
  sprod := cartesian

@[simp]
 theorem mem_cartesian (z:Object) (X Y:Set) :
    z ∈ X ×ˢ Y ↔ ∃ x:X, ∃ y:Y, z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair) := by
  simp only [SProd.sprod, SetTheory.Set.union_axiom]; constructor
  · intro ⟨ S, hz, hS ⟩; rw [SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom] at hS; obtain ⟨ x, hx ⟩ := hS
    use x; simp_all
  rintro ⟨ x, y, rfl ⟩; use slice x Y; refine ⟨ by simp, ?_ ⟩
  rw [SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom]; use x

noncomputable abbrev fst {X Y:Set} (z:X ×ˢ Y) : X :=
  ((mem_cartesian _ _ _).mp z.property).choose

noncomputable abbrev snd {X Y:Set} (z:X ×ˢ Y) : Y :=
  (exists_comm.mp ((mem_cartesian _ _ _).mp z.property)).choose

end SetTheory.Set

/-- Indexed union (from Section 3.4) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.iUnion (I: Set) (A: I → Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (I.replace (P := fun α S ↦ S = A α) (by intro _ _ _ ⟨h1, h2⟩; exact h1.trans h2.symm))

-- TARGET THEOREM (Exercise 3.5.9)
 /-- Exercise 3.5.9 -/ 
 theorem SetTheory.Set.iUnion_inter_iUnion {I J: Set} (A: I → Set) (B: J → Set) :
    (iUnion I A) ∩ (iUnion J B) = iUnion (I ×ˢ J) (fun p ↦ (A (SetTheory.Set.fst p)) ∩ (B (SetTheory.Set.snd p))) := by sorry

end Chapter3

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

-- Below are some API for handling coercions. This may not be the optimal way to set things up.
instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := nat_equiv n

/-- Exercise 3.5.12, with errata from web site incorporated -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.recursion (X: Set) (f: nat → X → X) (c:X) :
    ∃! a: nat → X, a 0 = c ∧ ∀ n, a (n + 1:ℕ) = f n (a n) := by sorry

compile without importing the textbook tactic module.

macro "grind" : tactic => `(tactic| aesop)

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq {X Y:Set} (h: (X: Object) = (Y: Object)) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.set_to_object.inj' h

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq_iff (X Y:Set) : (X: Object) = (Y: Object) ↔  X = Y :=
  ⟨ SetTheory.Set.coe_eq, by rintro rfl; rfl ⟩

/-- Axiom 3.2 (Equality of sets).  The `[ext]` tag allows the `ext` tactic to work for sets. -/
@[ext]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := {x} ∪ X

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_union (x:Object) (X Y:Set) : x ∈ (X ∪ Y) ↔ (x ∈ X ∨ x ∈ Y) :=
  SetTheory.union_pair_axiom X Y x

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

/-- Elements of a set (implicitly coerced to a subtype) are also elements of the set. -/
lemma SetTheory.Set.subtype_property (A:Set) (x:A) : x.val ∈ A := x.property

/-- Axiom 3.6 (axiom of specification) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Axiom 3.6 (axiom of specification) -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.specification_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Prop} {x:Object} (h: x ∈ A.specify P) :
    x ∈ A := (SetTheory.specification_axiom A P).1 x h

/-- Axiom 3.6 (axiom of specification) -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.specification_axiom' {A:Set} (P: A → Prop) (x:A) :
    x.val ∈ A.specify P ↔ P x := (SetTheory.specification_axiom A P).2 x

/-- Axiom 3.6 (axiom of specification) -/
@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.specification_axiom'' {A:Set} (P: A → Prop) (x:Object) :
    x ∈ A.specify P ↔ ∃ h:x ∈ A, P ⟨ x, h ⟩ := by
  constructor
  · intro h; use SetTheory.Set.specification_axiom (A:=A) (P:=P) h
    simp [←SetTheory.Set.specification_axiom' (A:=A) (P:=P), h]
  intro ⟨ h, hP ⟩
  simpa [←SetTheory.Set.specification_axiom' (A:=A) (P:=P)] using hP

/-- Axiom 3.7 (Axiom of replacement) -/
@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') (y:Object) :
    y ∈ SetTheory.replace A P hP ↔ ∃ x, P x y :=
  SetTheory.replacement_axiom A P hP y

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

/-- Axiom 3.12 (Union) -/
@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.union_axiom (A: Set) (x:Object) :
    x ∈ SetTheory.union A ↔ ∃ (S:Set), x ∈ S ∧ (S:Object) ∈ A :=
  SetTheory.union_axiom A x

/-- Ordered pairs -/
@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst: Object
  snd: Object

@[simp]
 theorem OrderedPair.eq (x y x' y' : Object) :
    (⟨ x, y ⟩ : OrderedPair) = (⟨ x', y' ⟩ : OrderedPair) ↔ x = x' ∧ y = y' := by aesop

/-- Exercise 3.5.1, first part -/
-- We faithfully copy the definition; the original file contains a `sorry` here.
 def OrderedPair.toObject : OrderedPair ↪ Object where
  toFun p := ({ (({p.fst}:Set):Object), (({p.fst, p.snd}:Set):Object) }:Set)
  inj' := by sorry

instance OrderedPair.inst_coeObject : Coe OrderedPair Object where
  coe := OrderedPair.toObject

/-- slice: needed for Cartesian product -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.slice (x:Object) (Y:Set) : Set :=
  Y.replace (P := fun y z ↦ z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) (by grind)

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_slice (x z:Object) (Y:Set) :
    z ∈ (SetTheory.Set.slice x Y) ↔ ∃ y:Y, z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair) := by
  -- unfold slice and apply replacement axiom
  change z ∈ Y.replace (P := fun y z0 ↦ z0 = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) (by grind) ↔ _
  -- re-prove the uniqueness condition explicitly to match the axiom input
  have hP : ∀ (y : Y) (z z' : Object),
      (z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) ∧ (z' = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) → z = z' := by
    intro y z z' h; exact h.1.trans h.2.symm
  simpa using (
    SetTheory.replacement_axiom (A:=Y)
      (P:=fun y z0 ↦ z0 = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) hP z)

/-- Cartesian product via replacement/union -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.cartesian (X Y:Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (X.replace (P := fun x z ↦ z = slice x Y)
    (by intro _ _ _ ⟨h1, h2⟩; exact h1.trans h2.symm))

/-- This instance enables the ×ˢ notation for Cartesian product. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.inst_SProd : SProd Set Set Set where
  sprod := SetTheory.Set.cartesian

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_cartesian (z:Object) (X Y:Set) :
    z ∈ X ×ˢ Y ↔ ∃ x:X, ∃ y:Y, z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair) := by
  simp only [SProd.sprod, SetTheory.Set.union_axiom]; constructor
  · intro ⟨ S, hz, hS ⟩; rw [SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom] at hS; obtain ⟨ x, hx ⟩ := hS
    use x; simp_all
  rintro ⟨ x, y, rfl ⟩; use slice x Y; refine ⟨ by simp, ?_ ⟩
  rw [SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom]; use x

noncomputable abbrev SetTheory.Set.fst {X Y:Set} (z:X ×ˢ Y) : X :=
  ((SetTheory.Set.mem_cartesian _ _ _).mp z.property).choose

noncomputable abbrev SetTheory.Set.snd {X Y:Set} (z:X ×ˢ Y) : Y :=
  (exists_comm.mp ((SetTheory.Set.mem_cartesian _ _ _).mp z.property)).choose

/-- Graph of a function X → Y as a subset of X ×ˢ Y -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.graph {X Y:Set} (f: X → Y) : Set :=
  (X ×ˢ Y).specify (fun p ↦ (f (SetTheory.Set.fst p) = SetTheory.Set.snd p))

/-- Exercise 3.5.10 -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.graph_inj {X Y:Set} (f f': X → Y) :
    SetTheory.Set.graph f = SetTheory.Set.graph f' ↔ f = f' := by sorry

end Chapter3

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 3 (minimal context for Section 3.5 target).

We only include the parts of Section 3.1 needed to state the target theorem,
plus a lightweight product-notation mechanism so that `×ˢ` parses on `Set`.
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms (from Section 3.1). -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Objects can be members of sets (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Sets are objects (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Empty set notation on Chapter3.Set (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

/-- Subset relation on Chapter3.Set (from Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/-
Provide a lightweight product-notation mechanism so that `×ˢ` parses on `Set`.
This mimics the notation layer used in the textbook project.
-/

/-- A minimal typeclass providing a binary operation for the `×ˢ` notation. -/
class SProd (α : Sort*) (β : Sort*) (γ : outParam (Sort*)) where
  sprod : α → β → γ

infixl:70 " ×ˢ " => SProd.sprod

/-- A placeholder Cartesian product on Chapter3.Set to support the `×ˢ` notation.
This is only to make the target theorem parse and typecheck. -/
def SetTheory.Set.fakeProd (A B : Set) : Set := A

/-- Instance enabling `×ˢ` on Chapter3.Set. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.inst_SProd : SProd Set Set Set where
  sprod := SetTheory.Set.fakeProd

/--
  Exercise 3.5.6 (target theorem).
  We keep the exact statement and end with `by sorry` as required.
-/
theorem SetTheory.Set.prod_subset_prod {A B C D:Set}
  (hA: A ≠ ∅) (hB: B ≠ ∅) (hC: C ≠ ∅) (hD: D ≠ ∅) :
    A ×ˢ B ⊆ C ×ˢ D ↔ A ⊆ C ∧ B ⊆ D := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

/-!
Self-contained slice extracted for Section 3.5 target theorem.
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

-- Below are some API for handling coercions. This may not be the optimal way to set things up.
instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

/-- Exercise 3.5.12, with errata from web site incorporated -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.recursion (X: Set) (f: nat → X → X) (c:X) :
    ∃! a: nat → X, a 0 = c ∧ ∀ n, a (n + 1 : ℕ) = f n (a n) := by
  sorry

/-- Exercise 3.5.13 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.nat_unique (nat':Set) (zero:nat') (succ:nat' → nat')
  (succ_ne: ∀ n:nat', succ n ≠ zero) (succ_of_ne: ∀ n m:nat', n ≠ m → succ n ≠ succ m)
  (ind: ∀ P: nat' → Prop, P zero → (∀ n, P n → P (succ n)) → ∀ n, P n) :
    ∃! f : nat → nat', Function.Bijective f ∧ f 0 = zero
    ∧ ∀ (n:nat) (n':nat'), f n = n' ↔ f (n+1:ℕ) = succ n' := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.of_extend {I J: BoundedInterval} (hIJ: I ⊆ J)
  {f: ℝ → ℝ} (h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) :
  PiecewiseConstantOn (fun x ↦ if x ∈ I then f x else 0) J := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-!
Self-contained slice for Section 3.6 target instance:
`instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩`
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

-- Impose the axioms of ZF with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

-- Coerce a set to its subtype so we can write `x : X` for `x` in set `X`.
instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

end Chapter3

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

open Classical in
/-- This is to make Finsets of BoundedIntervals work properly -/
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval := instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

open Classical in
/-- Theorem 11.2.16 (g) (Laws of integration) / Exercise 11.2.4 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.integ_of_extend {I J: BoundedInterval} (hIJ: I ⊆ J)
  {f: ℝ → ℝ} (h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) :
  integ (fun x ↦ if x ∈ I then f x else 0) J = integ f I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms


namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by
      grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

lemma Rat.steady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by rfl

abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

lemma Rat.eventuallySteady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.EventuallySteady a ↔ ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N) := by rfl

namespace Section_4_3

abbrev dist (x y : ℚ) := |x - y|

end Section_4_3

namespace Chapter5

lemma Rat.Steady.coe (ε : ℚ) (a:ℕ → ℚ) :
    ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n m : ℕ, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by
  constructor
  · intro h n m; specialize h n ?_ m ?_ <;> simp_all
  intro h n hn m hm
  lift n to ℕ using hn
  lift m to ℕ using hm
  simp [h n m]

example : (1:ℚ).Steady ((fun _:ℕ ↦ (3:ℚ)):Sequence) := by
  simp [Rat.Steady.coe, Rat.Close]

example : (1:ℚ).Steady ((fun _:ℕ ↦ (3:ℚ)):Sequence) := by
  intro n _ m _; simp_all [Sequence.n0_coe, Sequence.eval_coe_at_int, Rat.Close]

example : (1:ℚ).Steady ((fun n:ℕ ↦ if Even n then (1:ℚ) else (0:ℚ)):Sequence) := by
  rw [Rat.Steady.coe]
  intro n m
  split_ifs <;> simp [Rat.Close]

example : ¬ (0.5:ℚ).Steady ((fun n:ℕ ↦ if Even n then (1:ℚ) else (0:ℚ)):Sequence) := by
  rw [Rat.Steady.coe]
  by_contra h; specialize h 0 1; simp [Rat.Close] at h
  norm_num at h

example : (0.1:ℚ).Steady ((fun n:ℕ ↦ (10:ℚ) ^ (-(n:ℤ)-1) ):Sequence) := by
  rw [Rat.Steady.coe]
  intro n m; unfold Rat.Close
  wlog h : m ≤ n
  · specialize this m n (by linarith); rwa [abs_sub_comm]
  rw [abs_sub_comm, abs_of_nonneg]
  · rw [show (0.1:ℚ) = (10:ℚ)^(-1:ℤ) - 0 by norm_num]
    gcongr <;> try grind
    positivity
  linarith [show (10:ℚ) ^ (-(n:ℤ)-1) ≤ (10:ℚ) ^ (-(m:ℤ)-1) by gcongr; norm_num]

example : ¬(0.01:ℚ).Steady ((fun n:ℕ ↦ (10:ℚ) ^ (-(n:ℤ)-1) ):Sequence) := by
  sorry

example (ε:ℚ) : ¬ ε.Steady ((fun n:ℕ ↦ (2 ^ (n+1):ℚ) ):Sequence) := by
  sorry

example (ε:ℚ) (hε: ε>0) : ε.Steady ((fun _:ℕ ↦ (2:ℚ) ):Sequence) := by
  rw [Rat.Steady.coe]; simp [Rat.Close]; positivity

example : (10:ℚ).Steady ((fun n:ℕ ↦ if n = 0 then (10:ℚ) else (0:ℚ)):Sequence) := by
  rw [Rat.Steady.coe]; intro n m
  split_ifs <;> simp [Rat.Close]

example (ε:ℚ) (hε:ε<10):  ¬ ε.Steady ((fun n:ℕ ↦ if n = 0 then (10:ℚ) else (0:ℚ)):Sequence) := by
  contrapose! hε; rw [Rat.Steady.coe] at hε; specialize hε 0 1; simpa [Rat.Close] using hε

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_def (a:Sequence) :
  a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a := by rfl

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.coe (a:ℕ → ℚ) :
    (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ∃ N, ∀ j ≥ N, ∀ k ≥ N,
    Section_4_3.dist (a j) (a k) ≤ ε := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.mk {n₀:ℤ} (a: {n // n ≥ n₀} → ℚ) :
    (mk' n₀ a).IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ∃ N ≥ n₀, ∀ j ≥ N, ∀ k ≥ N,
    Section_4_3.dist (mk' n₀ a j) (mk' n₀ a k) ≤ ε := by
  sorry

noncomputable def Sequence.sqrt_two : Sequence := (fun n:ℕ ↦ ((⌊ (Real.sqrt 2)*10^n ⌋ / 10^n):ℚ))

theorem Sequence.ex_5_1_10_a : (1:ℚ).Steady sqrt_two := by sorry

theorem Sequence.ex_5_1_10_b : (0.1:ℚ).Steady (sqrt_two.from 1) := by sorry

theorem Sequence.ex_5_1_10_c : (0.1:ℚ).EventuallySteady sqrt_two := by sorry

theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.harmonic : (mk' 1 (fun n ↦ (1:ℚ)/n)).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

abbrev BoundedBy {n:ℕ} (a: Fin n → ℚ) (M:ℚ) : Prop := ∀ i, |a i| ≤ M

lemma boundedBy_def {n:ℕ} (a: Fin n → ℚ) (M:ℚ) : BoundedBy a M ↔ ∀ i, |a i| ≤ M := by rfl

abbrev Sequence.BoundedBy (a:Sequence) (M:ℚ) : Prop := ∀ n, |a n| ≤ M

lemma Sequence.boundedBy_def (a:Sequence) (M:ℚ) : a.BoundedBy M ↔ ∀ n, |a n| ≤ M := by rfl

abbrev Sequence.IsBounded (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∃ M ≥ 0, a.BoundedBy M

lemma Sequence.isBounded_def (a:Sequence) : a.IsBounded ↔ ∃ M ≥ 0, a.BoundedBy M := by rfl

example : BoundedBy ![1,-2,3,-4] 4 := by intro i; fin_cases i <;> norm_num

example : ¬((fun n:ℕ ↦ (-1)^n * (n+1:ℚ)):Sequence).IsBounded := by sorry

example : ((fun n:ℕ ↦ (-1:ℚ)^n):Sequence).IsBounded := by
  refine ⟨ 1, by norm_num, ?_ ⟩; intro i; by_cases h: 0 ≤ i <;> simp [h]

example : ¬((fun n:ℕ ↦ (-1:ℚ)^n):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  rw [Sequence.IsCauchy.coe]
  by_contra h; specialize h (1/2 : ℚ) (by norm_num)
  choose N h using h; specialize h N _ (N+1) _ <;> try omega
  by_cases h': Even N
  · simp [h'.neg_one_pow, (h'.add_one).neg_one_pow, Section_4_3.dist] at h
    norm_num at h
  observe h₁: Odd N
  observe h₂: Even (N+1)
  simp [h₁.neg_one_pow, h₂.neg_one_pow, Section_4_3.dist] at h
  norm_num at h

lemma IsBounded.finite {n:ℕ} (a: Fin n → ℚ) : ∃ M ≥ 0,  BoundedBy a M := by
  induction' n with n hn
  · use 0; simp
  set a' : Fin n → ℚ := fun m ↦ a m.castSucc
  choose M hpos hM using hn a'
  have h1 : BoundedBy a' (M + |a (Fin.ofNat _ n)|) := fun m ↦ (hM m).trans (by simp)
  have h2 : |a (Fin.ofNat _ n)| ≤ M + |a (Fin.ofNat _ n)| := by simp [hpos]
  refine ⟨ M + |a (Fin.ofNat _ n)|, by positivity, ?_ ⟩
  intro m; obtain ⟨ j, rfl ⟩ | rfl := Fin.eq_castSucc_or_eq_last m
  · grind
  convert h2; simp

lemma Sequence.isBounded_of_isCauchy {a:Sequence} (h: a.IsCauchy) : a.IsBounded := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.isBounded_add {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsBounded) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsBounded):
    (a + b:Sequence).IsBounded := by sorry

lemma Sequence.isBounded_sub {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsBounded) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsBounded):
    (a - b:Sequence).IsBounded := by sorry

lemma Sequence.isBounded_mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsBounded) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsBounded):
    (a * b:Sequence).IsBounded := by sorry

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

lemma Rat.closeSeq_def (ε: ℚ) (a b: Sequence) :
    ε.CloseSeq a b ↔ ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n) := by rfl

lemma Rat.eventuallyClose_def (ε: ℚ) (a b: Sequence) :
    ε.EventuallyClose a b ↔ ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N) := by rfl

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

lemma Sequence.equiv_def (a b: ℕ → ℚ) :
    Equiv a b ↔ ∀ (ε:ℚ), ε > 0 → ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence) := by rfl

lemma Sequence.equiv_iff (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Equiv a b ↔ ∀ ε > 0, ∃ N, ∀ n ≥ N, |a n - b n| ≤ ε := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.equiv_example :
  Equiv (fun n:ℕ ↦ (1:ℚ)+10^(-(n:ℤ)-1)) (fun n:ℕ ↦ (1:ℚ)-10^(-(n:ℤ)-1)) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_of_equiv {a b: ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) :
    (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ↔ (b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

lemma Sequence.isBounded_of_eventuallyClose {ε:ℚ} {a b: ℕ → ℚ} (hab: ε.EventuallyClose a b) :
    (a:Sequence).IsBounded ↔ (b:Sequence).IsBounded := by sorry

@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

theorem CauchySequence.ext' {a b: CauchySequence} (h: a.seq = b.seq) : a = b := by
  apply CauchySequence.ext _ h
  rw [a.zero, b.zero]

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

lemma Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by sorry

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

theorem CauchySequence.equiv_iff (a b: CauchySequence) : a ≈ b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by rfl

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

theorem LIM_def {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' ha) := by
  rw [LIM, dif_pos ha]

theorem Real.eq_lim (x:Real) : ∃ (a:ℕ → ℚ), (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a := by
  apply Quotient.ind _ x; intro a; use (a:ℕ → ℚ)
  observe : ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence
  rw [this, LIM_def (by convert a.cauchy)]
  refine ⟨ a.cauchy, ?_ ⟩
  congr; ext n; simp; replace := congr($this n); simp_all

 theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  · intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff]

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

lemma Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

theorem Real.LIM_add {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a + LIM b = LIM (a + b) := by
  simp_rw [LIM_def ha, LIM_def hb, LIM_def (Sequence.IsCauchy.add ha hb)]
  convert Quotient.liftOn₂_mk _ _ _ _
  rw [dif_pos]

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by simp_rw [mul_comm]; exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
    equiv_trans (mul_equiv_right _ ha hbb') (mul_equiv_left _ hb' haa')

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · exact Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy) (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

theorem Real.LIM_mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a * LIM b = LIM (a * b) := by
  simp_rw [LIM_def ha, LIM_def hb, LIM_def (Sequence.IsCauchy.mul ha hb)]
  convert Quotient.liftOn₂_mk _ _ _ _
  rw [dif_pos]

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

theorem Real.ratCast_def (q:ℚ) : (q:Real) = LIM (fun _ ↦ q) := by rw [LIM_def]; rfl

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

instance Real.instNatCast : NatCast Real where
  natCast n := ((n:ℚ):Real)

@[simp]
theorem Real.LIM.zero : LIM (fun _ ↦ (0:ℚ)) = 0 := by rw [←ratCast_def 0]; rfl

instance Real.instIntCast : IntCast Real where

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Close relation on rationals, from Section 4.3 -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y : ℚ) := |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

-- Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence)
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn; simp [hn]

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn; simp [hn]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by
  have : (n:ℤ) ≥ (0:ℤ) := by exact_mod_cast (Nat.zero_le n)
  simp [Sequence.ofNatFun, this]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by
  rfl

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  Sequence.mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

-- ε-steadiness and eventual ε-steadiness
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

end Chapter5

-- Section 5.2: ε-close and equivalent sequences (at top level)
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

lemma Sequence.equiv_iff (a b: ℕ → ℚ) :
    Equiv a b ↔ ∀ ε > 0, ∃ N, ∀ n ≥ N, |a n - b n| ≤ ε := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_of_equiv {a b: ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) :
    (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ↔ (b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

-- Section 5.3: CauchySequence and Real
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
lemma CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
lemma CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence.mk' ha = a := by rfl

-- Constant sequences are Cauchy (from Section 5.3)
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _ : ℕ ↦ a) : Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

-- We need Zero CauchySequence for the LIM default value
instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ : ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const 0)

-- Equivalence relation on CauchySequence
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv :=
    { refl := by sorry
      symm := by sorry
      trans := by sorry }

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

lemma LIM_def {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' ha) := by
  rw [LIM, dif_pos ha]

-- Equality of limits
lemma Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  · intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb]

-- Addition on reals
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a') (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') := by
  sorry

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_)

-- Multiplication on reals
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') := by
  sorry

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · exact
          Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy)
            (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'

-- Section 4.3: define closeness and distance on ℚ needed later.

def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Section_4_3

abbrev dist (x y : ℚ) := |x - y|

@[simp] theorem dist_eq (x y: ℚ) : dist x y = |x-y| := rfl

namespace Chapter5

-- Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
--   sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ.
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

-- Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

-- If `a : ℕ → ℚ` is used in a context where a `Sequence` is expected, automatically coerce `a` to `Sequence.ofNatFun a`.
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

-- a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`. It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`.
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

-- Definition 5.1.3 - ε-steadiness for a sequence with arbitrary starting point n₀.
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady).
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

-- Cauchy sequences
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.coe (a:ℕ → ℚ) :
    (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ∃ N, ∀ j ≥ N, ∀ k ≥ N,
    Section_4_3.dist (a j) (a k) ≤ ε := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

-- Section 5.2: Equivalence of sequences and eventual closeness.

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences)
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

-- Exercise 5.2.1
lemma Sequence.isCauchy_of_equiv {a b: ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Sequence.Equiv a b) :
    (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ↔ (b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

-- Section 5.3: Real numbers as limits of Cauchy sequences.

-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

-- A sequence starting at zero that is Cauchy, can be viewed as a Cauchy sequence.
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  sorry

-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy
 theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ : ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
-- It is convenient in Lean to assign the "dummy" value of 0 to `LIM a` when `a` is not Cauchy.
-- This requires Classical logic, because the property of being Cauchy is not computable or decidable.
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

theorem LIM_def {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' ha) := by
  rw [LIM, dif_pos ha]

-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers)
theorem Real.eq_lim (x:Real) : ∃ (a:ℕ → ℚ), (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a := by
  sorry

-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers)
theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  sorry

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

-- We will need addition and multiplication on reals.

-- Lemma 5.3.6 (Sum of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

-- Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)
lemma Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Sequence.Equiv a a') :
    Sequence.Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Sequence.Equiv b b') :
    Sequence.Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by
  simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

lemma Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Sequence.Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Sequence.Equiv b b') :
    Sequence.Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') := by
  sorry

-- Definition 5.3.4 (Addition of reals)
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_ )

-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Sequence.Equiv a a') :
  Sequence.Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Sequence.Equiv b b') :
  Sequence.Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by simp_rw [mul_comm]; exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Sequence.Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Sequence.Equiv b b') : Sequence.Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') := by
    sorry

-- Definition 5.3.9 (Product of reals)
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · exact
          Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy)
            (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_ )

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

-- Some basic lemmas for LIM of constants.
@[simp]
theorem Real.LIM.zero : LIM (fun _ ↦ (0:ℚ)) = 0 := by
  sorry

-- Bounded away from zero (Section 5.3.12).
abbrev BoundedAwayZero (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, |a n| ≥ c

end Chapter5

-- Section 5.4: Positivity/negativity and order on reals.

namespace Chapter5

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev Real.abs (x:Real) : Real := if x.IsPos then x else (if x.IsNeg then -x else 0)

-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals)
instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals)
instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

-- (Not from textbook) Real has the structure of a linear ordering. The order is not computable,
--   and so classical logic is required to impose decidability.
open Classical in
noncomputable instance Real.instLinearOrder : LinearOrder Real where
  le_refl := by sorry
  le_trans := by sorry
  lt_iff_le_not_ge := by sorry
  le_antisymm := by sorry
  le_total := by sorry
  toDecidableLE := Classical.decRel _

@[simp]
theorem Real.abs_of_pos (x:Real) (hx: x.IsPos) : abs x = x := by
  sorry

@[simp]
theorem Real.abs_of_zero : abs 0 = 0 := by
  sorry

lemma bounded_away_zero_def (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Chapter5.BoundedAwayZero a ↔ ∃ (c : ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, |a n| ≥ c := by rfl

-- Not from textbook: the reals are nonzero if bounded away from zero.
lemma Real.boundedAwayZero_of_nonzero {x : Real} (hx : x ≠ 0) :
    ∃ a : ℕ → ℚ, (a : Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ BoundedAwayZero a ∧ x = LIM a := by
  -- This proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
  obtain ⟨b, hb, rfl⟩ := Real.eq_lim x
  simp only [← Real.LIM.zero, ne_eq] at hx
  -- following original proof (details elided)
  sorry

-- Definition 5.3.16 (Reciprocation of real numbers). Requires classical logic.
noncomputable instance Real.instInv : Inv Real where
  inv x := if h : x ≠ 0 then LIM (Real.boundedAwayZero_of_nonzero h).choose⁻¹ else 0

@[simp] theorem Real.max_eq (x y:Real) : max x y = if x ≥ y then x else y := max_def' x y
@[simp] theorem Real.min_eq (x y:Real) : min x y = if x ≤ y then x else y := rfl

-- Exercise 5.4.9
 theorem Real.inv_max {x y :Real} (hx:x.IsPos) (hy:y.IsPos) : (max x y)⁻¹ = min x⁻¹ y⁻¹ := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 11 Sections 11.1–11.2 minimal dependencies
for the target theorem `PiecewiseConstantOn.of_join`.
-/

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

abbrev BoundedInterval.joins (K I J: BoundedInterval) : Prop := (I:Set ℝ) ∩ (J:Set ℝ) = ∅
  ∧ (K:Set ℝ) = (I:Set ℝ) ∪ (J:Set ℝ) ∧ |K|ₗ = |I|ₗ + |J|ₗ

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Theorem 11.2.16 (h) (Laws of integration) / Exercise 11.2.4 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.of_join {I J K: BoundedInterval} (hIJK: K.joins I J)
  (f: ℝ → ℝ) : PiecewiseConstantOn f K ↔ PiecewiseConstantOn f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn f J := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by
      sorry

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by sorry

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by sorry

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by
  sorry

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by
  sorry

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

-- From Section 4.3
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

lemma Rat.steady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by rfl

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

lemma Rat.eventuallySteady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.EventuallySteady a ↔ ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N) := by rfl

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_def (a:Sequence) :
  a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a := by rfl

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by
    sorry
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
lemma CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (by
    sorry)

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv :=
    { refl := by sorry
      symm := by sorry
      trans := by sorry }

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

open Classical in
lemma LIM_def {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' ha) := by
  classical
  unfold LIM
  simp [dif_pos ha]

open Classical in
lemma Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  classical
  constructor
  · intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    simp [LIM, dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb] at h
    simpa [LIM, dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb] using h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  simpa [LIM, dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb]

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      have : Sequence.Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') := by sorry
      have hsum : ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry
      have hsum' : ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry
      have := (Real.LIM_eq_LIM (a:= (a + b)) (b:= (a' + b')) hsum hsum').mpr this
      simpa using this)

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦ Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (by
    sorry))

instance Real.instIntCast : IntCast Real where intCast n := ((n:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      have : Sequence.Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') := by sorry
      have h1 : ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry
      have h2 : ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry
      have := (Real.LIM_eq_LIM (a:= (a * b)) (b:= (a' * b')) h1 h2).mpr this
      simpa using this)

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

-- provide an explicit Zero instance for Real (needed by AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms below)
instance Real.instZero : Zero Real where
  zero := ((0:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

abbrev BoundedAwayZero (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, |a n| ≥ c

lemma bounded_away_zero_def (a:ℕ → ℚ) : BoundedAwayZero a ↔
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, |a n| ≥ c := by rfl

lemma Real.boundedAwayZero_of_nonzero {x:Real} (hx: x ≠ 0) :
    ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ BoundedAwayZero a ∧ x = LIM a := by
  sorry

open Classical in
noncomputable instance Real.instInv : Inv Real where
  inv x := if h: x ≠ 0 then LIM (boundedAwayZero_of_nonzero h).choose⁻¹ else ((0:ℚ):Real)

abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

theorem Real.exist_inv_nat_le {x:Real} (hx: x.IsPos) : ∃ N:ℤ, N>0 ∧ (N:Real)⁻¹ < x := by
  sorry

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 3 Section 3.6 target theorem
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- ZF-style axioms used throughout Chapter 3. -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset : Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X : Set) (Y : Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X : Set) (Y : Set) (F : Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f : Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
      function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Objects are elements of sets. -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Sets are objects. -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Coerce a set to a type (its subtype of elements). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

/-- Empty set notation for Chapter3.Set. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

/-- A convenient abbreviation for specification in the Set namespace. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Alias for the Chapter-3 naturals set. -/
abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- The equivalence between ℕ and the Chapter-3 naturals set. -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

/-- Coerce a Chapter-3 natural to ℕ. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y : Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X : Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  -- original proof replaced to keep snippet minimal
  sorry

@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y : Set} (h : EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  -- original proof replaced to keep snippet minimal
  sorry

@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z : Set}
    (h1 : EqualCard X Y) (h2 : EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  -- original proof replaced to keep snippet minimal
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1: EqualCard is an equivalence relation. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set :=
  ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard,
    ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl,
      SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm,
      SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans ⟩ ⟩

/-- A Chapter-3-style Fin n set. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set := nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

/-- Definition 3.6.5: has_cardinality n. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X : Set) (n : ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

/-- Exercise 3.6.2a -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.has_card_zero {X : Set} : X.has_card 0 ↔ X = ∅ := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-!
Self-contained extraction for Chapter 3 minimal context to typecheck the target theorem
-*/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := set_to_object X

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

/-- Abbreviation for the set of naturals less than `n` (as a set in our set theory). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set := nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

export SetTheory (nat)

/-- Exercise 3.6.3, phrased using Mathlib natural numbers -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.bounded_on_finite {n:ℕ} (f: Fin n → nat) : ∃ M, ∀ i, (f i:ℕ) ≤ M := by
  sorry

end Chapter3
