/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 11, Sections 11.1–11.2, sufficient to typecheck
Theorem 11.2.16(h) `PiecewiseConstantOn.integ_of_join`.
-/

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals (Section 11.1) -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instEmpty : EmptyCollection BoundedInterval where
  emptyCollection := Ioo 0 0

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.coe_empty : ((∅ : BoundedInterval):Set ℝ) = ∅ := by
  simp [toSet]

open Classical in
/-- This is to make Finsets of BoundedIntervals work properly -/
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval := instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioo (a b:ℝ) : (Ioo a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioo a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Icc (a b:ℝ) : (Icc a b : Set ℝ) = .Icc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioc (a b:ℝ) : (Ioc a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ico (a b:ℝ) : (Ico a b : Set ℝ) = .Ico a b := by rfl

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

theorem BoundedInterval.mem_iff (I: BoundedInterval) (x:ℝ) :
  x ∈ I ↔ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ) := by rfl

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

abbrev BoundedInterval.joins (K I J: BoundedInterval) : Prop := (I:Set ℝ) ∩ (J:Set ℝ) = ∅
  ∧ (K:Set ℝ) = (I:Set ℝ) ∪ (J:Set ℝ) ∧ |K|ₗ = |I|ₗ + |J|ₗ

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

/-- Theorem 11.2.16 (h) (Laws of integration) / Exercise 11.2.4 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.integ_of_join {I J K: BoundedInterval} (hIJK: K.joins I J)
  {f: ℝ → ℝ} (h: PiecewiseConstantOn f K) :
  integ f K = integ f I + integ f J := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

/-! Section 5.1 essentials -/

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
  vanish := by
    sorry

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by
    sorry

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by
  sorry

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by
  sorry

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by
  sorry

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by
  sorry

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

/-- Slight generalization of ε-steadiness for sequences with arbitrary start -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

lemma Rat.steady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

lemma Rat.eventuallySteady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.EventuallySteady a ↔ ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N) := by rfl

namespace Chapter5

lemma Rat.Steady.coe (ε : ℚ) (a:ℕ → ℚ) :
    ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n m : ℕ, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by
  sorry

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_def (a:Sequence) :
  a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a := by rfl

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.coe (a:ℕ → ℚ) :
    (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ∃ N, ∀ j ≥ N, ∀ k ≥ N,
    Section_4_3.dist (a j) (a k) ≤ ε := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.mk {n₀:ℤ} (a: {n // n ≥ n₀} → ℚ) :
    (mk' n₀ a).IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ∃ N ≥ n₀, ∀ j ≥ N, ∀ k ≥ N,
    Section_4_3.dist (mk' n₀ a j) (mk' n₀ a k) ≤ ε := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

/-! Section 5.2 essentials -/

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

lemma Rat.closeSeq_def (ε: ℚ) (a b: Sequence) :
    ε.CloseSeq a b ↔ ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n) := by rfl

lemma Rat.eventuallyClose_def (ε: ℚ) (a b: Sequence) :
    ε.EventuallyClose a b ↔ ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N) := by rfl

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

lemma Sequence.equiv_def (a b: ℕ → ℚ) :
    Equiv a b ↔ ∀ (ε:ℚ), ε > 0 → ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence) := by rfl

end Chapter5

/-! Section 5.3 essentials -/

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

lemma CauchySequence.ext' {a b: CauchySequence} (h: a.seq = b.seq) : a = b := by
  apply CauchySequence.ext _ h
  rw [a.zero, b.zero]

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

theorem Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by sorry

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := by sorry
     symm := by sorry
     trans := by sorry
  }

theorem CauchySequence.equiv_iff (a b: CauchySequence) : a ≈ b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by rfl

theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

lemma LIM_def {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' ha) := by
  rw [LIM, dif_pos ha]

lemma Real.eq_lim (x:Real) : ∃ (a:ℕ → ℚ), (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a := by
  apply Quotient.ind _ x; intro a; use (a:ℕ → ℚ)
  observe : ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence
  rw [this, LIM_def (by convert a.cauchy)]
  refine ⟨ a.cauchy, ?_ ⟩
  congr; ext n; simp; replace := congr($this n); simp_all

lemma Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  · intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff]

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

lemma Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

lemma Real.LIM_add {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a + LIM b = LIM (a + b) := by
  simp_rw [LIM_def ha, LIM_def hb, LIM_def (Sequence.IsCauchy.add ha hb)]
  convert Quotient.liftOn₂_mk _ _ _ _
  rw [dif_pos]

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by simp_rw [mul_comm]; exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
    equiv_trans (mul_equiv_right _ ha hbb') (mul_equiv_left _ hb' haa')

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · exact Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy) (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

lemma Real.LIM_mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a * LIM b = LIM (a * b) := by
  simp_rw [LIM_def ha, LIM_def hb, LIM_def (Sequence.IsCauchy.mul ha hb)]
  convert Quotient.liftOn₂_mk _ _ _ _
  rw [dif_pos]

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

lemma Real.ratCast_def (q:ℚ) : (q:Real) = LIM (fun _ ↦ q) := by rw [LIM_def]; rfl

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

instance Real.instNatCast : NatCast Real where
  natCast n := ((n:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

lemma Real.sub_eq_add_neg (x y:Real) : x - y = x + (-y) := rfl

end Chapter5

/-! Section 5.4 essentials (ordering and min/max) -/

namespace Chapter5

abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x-y).IsNeg

instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

noncomputable instance Real.instLinearOrder : LinearOrder Real where
  le_refl := by intro x; exact Or.inr rfl
  le_trans := by
    intro a b c hab hbc
    sorry
  lt_iff_le_not_ge := by
    intro a b; sorry
  le_antisymm := by
    intro a b hab hba; sorry
  le_total := by
    intro a b; sorry
  toDecidableLE := Classical.decRel _

lemma Real.min_eq (x y:Real) : min x y = if x ≤ y then x else y := rfl

lemma Real.max_eq (x y:Real) : max x y = if x ≥ y then x else y := max_def' x y

/-- Exercise 5.4.9 -/
lemma Real.min_add (x y z:Real) : min (x + z) (y + z) = min x y + z := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-! Self-contained slice for Chapter5 target theorem Real.LIM_of_ge -/

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). -/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0. -/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

/-- Coerce ℕ → ℚ to a Sequence (starting at 0). -/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by
  norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

/-- Start a sequence from a later index. -/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

/-- ε-closeness of rationals. -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

/-- Definition 5.1.3-like: ε-steadiness for a sequence with arbitrary starting point. -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady). -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

/-- From Section 5.2: ε-close sequences and eventual ε-closeness. -/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Cauchy property for sequences. -/
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences). -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero. -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

/-- Build a CauchySequence from a ℕ-indexed function which is Cauchy as a Sequence. -/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy. -/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

/-- Setoid on Cauchy sequences given by eventual ε-closeness. -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := by sorry
     symm := by sorry
     trans := by sorry
  }

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/--
  It is convenient in Lean to assign the "dummy" value of 0 to `LIM a` when `a` is not Cauchy.
  This requires Classical logic, because the property of being Cauchy is not computable.
-/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1: transitivity of equivalence (used later). -/
@[simp] theorem CauchySequence.equiv_iff (a b: CauchySequence) : a ≈ b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by rfl

/-- Addition on reals via limits of sums. -/
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      -- Well-definedness via equivalence is left abstract here.
      sorry)

/-- Product on reals via limits of products. -/
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      -- Well-definedness via equivalence is left abstract here.
      sorry)

/-- Rational numbers embed into reals via constant Cauchy sequences. -/
instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

/-- Negation on reals via multiplication by −1. -/
noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

/-- Axioms of an additive group for reals (proofs omitted). -/
noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

/-- Sequences bounded away from zero with sign (negative case). -/
abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals). -/
instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

/-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals). -/
instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

end Chapter5

namespace Chapter5

 theorem Real.LIM_of_ge {x:Real} {a:ℕ → ℚ} (hcauchy: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (h: ∀ n, a n ≥ x) :
    LIM a ≥ x := by sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-!
Self-contained slice to typecheck Proposition 3.6.14 (c).
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A 
      ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Axiom 3.2 (Equality of sets).  The `[ext]` tag allows the `ext` tactic to work for sets. -/
@[ext]
theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

/-- Definition 3.1.14.   -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

/-- Re-expose `specify` at the `Set` namespace level. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv.symm n

instance SetTheory.Object.instNatCast : NatCast Object where
  natCast n := (n:Nat).val

/-- Equal cardinality (Definition 3.6.1) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

/-- Reflexivity of equal cardinality -/
@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

/-- Symmetry of equal cardinality -/
@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) :
    SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

/-- Transitivity of equal cardinality -/
@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set}
    (h1: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) (h2: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y Z) :
    SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set :=
  ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard,
    ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl, SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm, SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans ⟩ ⟩

/-- `Fin n` as a set (Definition 3.6.5 auxiliary) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

/-- Definition 3.6.5 -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

/-- `finite` sets (Section 3.6) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, SetTheory.Set.has_card X n

open Classical in
/-- It is convenient for Lean purposes to give infinite sets the ``junk`` cardinality of zero. -/
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Proposition 3.6.14 (c) / Exercise 3.6.4 -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.card_subset {X Y:Set} (hX: X.finite) (hY: Y ⊆ X) :
    Y.finite ∧ Y.card ≤ X.card := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq_iff (X Y:Set) : (X: Object) = (Y: Object) ↔  X = Y := by
  constructor
  · intro h; exact SetTheory.set_to_object.inj' h
  · rintro rfl; rfl

/-- Axiom 3.2 (Equality of sets).  The `[ext]` tag allows the `ext` tactic to work for sets. -/
@[ext]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.not_mem_empty : ∀ x, x ∉ (∅:Set) := SetTheory.emptyset_mem

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_singleton (x a:Object) : x ∈ ({a}:Set) ↔ x = a :=
  SetTheory.singleton_axiom x a

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_union (x:Object) (X Y:Set) : x ∈ (X ∪ Y) ↔ (x ∈ X ∨ x ∈ Y) :=
  SetTheory.union_pair_axiom X Y x

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
 def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv.symm n

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) : SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) (h2: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y Z) : SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard, {refl := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl, symm := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm, trans := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans} ⟩

/-- Definition 3.6.5 -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ ((m : ℕ) < n))

/-- Definition 3.6.5 -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, SetTheory.Set.has_card X n

open Classical in
/-- It is convenient for Lean purposes to give infinite sets the ``junk`` cardinality of zero. -/
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Proposition 3.6.14 (a) / Exercise 3.6.4 -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.card_insert {X:Set} (hX: X.finite) {x:Object} (hx: x ∉ X) :
    (X ∪ {x}).finite ∧ (X ∪ {x}).card = X.card + 1 := by sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Set.Lattice
import Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Basic

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter11

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

open BigOperators

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.6.1 -/
abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

namespace Chapter11
open BoundedInterval Chapter9

/-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

/-- Definition 11.3.2 (Uppper and lower Riemann integrals )-/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Definition 11.3.9 (Riemann sums).  The restriction to positive length J is not needed thanks to various junk value conventions. -/
noncomputable abbrev upper_riemann_sum (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : ℝ :=
  ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, (sSup (f '' (J:Set ℝ))) * |J|ₗ

/-- Proposition 11.3.12 / Exercise 11.3.5 -/
theorem upper_integ_le_upper_sum {f:ℝ → ℝ} {I:BoundedInterval} (hf: BddOn f I)
  (P: Partition I): upper_integral f I ≤ upper_riemann_sum f P := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

/--
  This definition needs to be made outside of the Section 4.3 namespace for technical reasons.
-/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

lemma Rat.steady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

lemma Rat.eventuallySteady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.EventuallySteady a ↔ ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N) := by rfl

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_def (a:Sequence) :
  a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a := by rfl

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

theorem CauchySequence.ext' {a b: CauchySequence} (h: a.seq = b.seq) : a = b := by
  apply CauchySequence.ext _ h
  rw [a.zero, b.zero]

/-- A sequence starting at zero that is Cauchy, can be viewed as a Cauchy sequence.-/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy -/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/--
  It is convenient in Lean to assign the "dummy" value of 0 to `LIM a` when `a` is not Cauchy.
  This requires Classical logic, because the property of being Cauchy is not computable or
  decidable.
-/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

/-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers) -/
theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  sorry

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

/--Lemma 5.3.6 (Sum of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)-/
 theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

/-- Definition 5.3.4 (Addition of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy) -/
 theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by simp_rw [mul_comm]; exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
    equiv_trans (mul_equiv_right _ ha hbb') (mul_equiv_left _ hb' haa')

/-- Definition 5.3.9 (Product of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . exact Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy) (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

/-- Proposition 5.3.11 (laws of algebra) -/
noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

/-- Definition 5.4.1 (sequences bounded away from zero with sign). Sequences are indexed to start
  from zero as this is more convenient for Mathlib purposes.
-/
abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

/-- Definition 5.4.1 (sequences bounded away from zero with sign). -/
abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals) -/
instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x-y).IsNeg

/-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals) -/
instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

open Classical in
/--
  (Not from textbook) Real has the structure of a linear ordering. The order is not computable,
  and so classical logic is required to impose decidability.
-/
noncomputable instance Real.instLinearOrder : LinearOrder Real where
  le_refl := sorry
  le_trans := sorry
  lt_iff_le_not_ge := sorry
  le_antisymm := sorry
  le_total := sorry
  toDecidableLE := Classical.decRel _

/-- Exercise 5.4.9 -/
theorem Real.max_comm (x y:Real) : max x y = max y x := by sorry

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instEmpty : EmptyCollection BoundedInterval where
  emptyCollection := Ioo 0 0

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.coe_empty : ((∅ : BoundedInterval):Set ℝ) = ∅ := by
  simp [toSet]

open Classical in
/-- This is to make Finsets of BoundedIntervals work properly -/
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval := instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioo (a b:ℝ) : (Ioo a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioo a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Icc (a b:ℝ) : (Icc a b : Set ℝ) = .Icc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioc (a b:ℝ) : (Ioc a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ico (a b:ℝ) : (Ico a b : Set ℝ) = .Ico a b := by rfl

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.6.1 -/
abbrev BddAboveOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, f x ≤ M

abbrev BddBelowOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, -M ≤ f x

abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

namespace Chapter11
open BoundedInterval Chapter9

/-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

/-- Definition 11.3.4 (Riemann integral)
As we permit junk values, the simplest definition for the Riemann integral is the upper integral.-/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

/-- Definition 11.3.9 (Riemann sums).  The restriction to positive length J is not needed thanks to various junk value conventions. -/
noncomputable abbrev upper_riemann_sum (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : ℝ :=
  ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, (sSup (f '' (J:Set ℝ))) * |J|ₗ

/-- Lemma 11.3.11 / Exercise 11.3.4 -/
theorem upper_riemann_sum_le {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {I:BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)
  (hf: Chapter9.BddOn f I) (hgf: MajorizesOn g f I) (hg: PiecewiseConstantOn g I) :
  upper_riemann_sum f P ≤ integ g I := by
   sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

-- Allow writing x ∈ X for x:Object and X:Set
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

@[ext]
theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_union (x:Object) (X Y:Set) : x ∈ (X ∪ Y) ↔ (x ∈ X ∨ x ∈ Y) :=
  SetTheory.union_pair_axiom X Y x

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity)
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ SetTheory.nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

-- Treat `Nat` as alias for the set of naturals in the set theory
abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

-- Coerce elements of Nat to ℕ via the equivalence
instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv.symm n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set := nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X X := by
  refine ⟨id, ?_⟩; exact Function.bijective_id

@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) :
    SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y X := by
  rcases h with ⟨f, hf⟩
  refine ⟨(Equiv.ofBijective f hf).symm, ?_⟩
  simpa using (Equiv.ofBijective f hf).symm.bijective

@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set}
    (h1: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) (h2: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y Z) :
    SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Z := by
  rcases h1 with ⟨f, hf⟩; rcases h2 with ⟨g, hg⟩
  refine ⟨fun x => g (f x), ?_⟩
  exact hg.comp hf

instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set :=
  ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard,
    ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl, SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm, SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans ⟩ ⟩

abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

-- Proposition 3.6.14 (b) / Exercise 3.6.4
 theorem SetTheory.Set.card_union {X Y:Set} (hX: X.finite) (hY: Y.finite) :
    (X ∪ Y).finite ∧ (X ∪ Y).card ≤ X.card + Y.card := by sorry

end Chapter3

  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

 MajorizesOn (f+h) (g+h) I) := by

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u

  emptyset: Set

  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set

    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq_iff (X Y:Set) : (X: Object) = (Y: Object) ↔  X = Y := by
  constructor
  · intro h; exact SetTheory.set_to_object.inj' h
  · rintro rfl; rfl

@[ext]
theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') (y:Object) :
    y ∈ A.replace hP ↔ ∃ x, P x y := SetTheory.replacement_axiom A P hP y

/-- Equip `Nat` as the set of natural numbers. -/
abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := (SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv).symm n

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

/-- Abbreviation for subsets of Nat less than n. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set := nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
/-- It is convenient for Lean purposes to give infinite sets the ``junk`` cardinality of zero. -/
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

abbrev SetTheory.Set.image {X Y : Set} (f : X → Y) (S : Set) : Set :=
  X.replace (P := fun x y ↦ f x = y ∧ x.val ∈ S) (by simp_all)

/-- Proposition 3.6.14 (d) / Exercise 3.6.4 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.card_image_inj {X Y:Set} (hX: X.finite) {f: X → Y}
  (hf: Function.Injective f) : (image f X).card = X.card := by sorry

end Chapter3

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

 theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq {X Y:Set} (h: (X: Object) = (Y: Object)) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.set_to_object.inj' h

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq_iff (X Y:Set) : (X: Object) = (Y: Object) ↔  X = Y :=
  ⟨ coe_eq, by rintro rfl; rfl ⟩

@[ext]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y := SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.not_mem_empty : ∀ x, x ∉ (∅:Set) := SetTheory.emptyset_mem

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_union (x:Object) (X Y:Set) : x ∈ (X ∪ Y) ↔ (x ∈ X ∨ x ∈ Y) :=
  SetTheory.union_pair_axiom X Y x

instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

 theorem SetTheory.Set.subset_def (X Y:Set) : X ⊆ Y ↔ ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y := by rfl

@[simp, refl]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.subset_self (A:Set) : A ⊆ A := by
  sorry

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.empty_subset (A:Set) : ∅ ⊆ A := by
  sorry

 theorem SetTheory.Set.subset_trans {A B C:Set} (hAB:A ⊆ B) (hBC:B ⊆ C) : A ⊆ C := by
  rw [subset_def]
  intro x hx
  rw [subset_def] at hAB
  apply hAB x at hx
  apply hBC x at hx
  assumption

 theorem SetTheory.Set.subset_antisymm (A B:Set) (hAB:A ⊆ B) (hBA:B ⊆ A) : A = B := by
  sorry

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

instance SetTheory.Set.instIntersection : Inter Set where
  inter X Y :=
    X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∈ Y)

 theorem  SetTheory.Set.union_inter_distrib_left (A B C:Set) :
    A ∪ (B ∩ C) = (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C) := by
  sorry

instance SetTheory.Set.instDistribLattice : DistribLattice Set where
  le := (· ⊆ ·)
  le_refl := SetTheory.Set.subset_self
  le_trans := fun _ _ _ ↦ SetTheory.Set.subset_trans
  le_antisymm := SetTheory.Set.subset_antisymm
  inf := (· ∩ ·)
  sup := (· ∪ ·)
  le_sup_left := by
    sorry
  le_sup_right := by
    sorry
  sup_le := by
    sorry
  inf_le_left := by
    sorry
  inf_le_right := by
    sorry
  le_inf := by
    sorry
  le_sup_inf := by
    intro X Y Z; change (X ∪ Y) ∩ (X ∪ Z) ⊆ X ∪ (Y ∩ Z)
    rw [← union_inter_distrib_left]

instance SetTheory.Set.instOrderBot : OrderBot Set where
  bot := ∅
  bot_le := SetTheory.Set.empty_subset

lemma SetTheory.Set.subtype_property (A:Set) (x:A) : x.val ∈ A := x.property

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

 def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X : Set) : Prop := ∃ n : ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

 theorem SetTheory.Set.card_union_disjoint {X Y:Set} (hX: X.finite) (hY: Y.finite)
  (hdisj: Disjoint X Y) : (X ∪ Y).card = X.card + Y.card := by sorry

end Chapter3

theorem inj_trichotomy {X Y : Type}
    (h : ¬∃ f : X → Y, Function.Injective f) :
    ∃ g : Y → X, Function.Injective g := by
  sorry

def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by
      grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

@[simp] theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := by sorry
     symm := by sorry
     trans := by sorry
  }

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

@[simp] theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

@[simp] theorem Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') := by
    sorry

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      have : Sequence.Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
        Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy)
                            (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy)
                            haa' hbb'
      sorry
    )

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instZero : Zero Real where
  zero := ((0:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      sorry
    )

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x-y).IsNeg

instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

open Classical in
noncomputable instance Real.instLinearOrder : LinearOrder Real where
  le_refl := by sorry
  le_trans := by sorry
  lt_iff_le_not_ge := by sorry
  le_antisymm := by sorry
  le_total := by sorry
  toDecidableLE := Classical.decRel _

 theorem Real.min_comm (x y:Real) : min x y = min y x := by sorry

end Chapter5
