import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

/--
  This definition needs to be made outside of the Section 4.3 namespace for technical reasons.
-/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

/-- A slight generalization of Definition 5.1.3 - definition of ε-steadiness for a sequence with an
arbitrary starting point n₀ -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

lemma Rat.steady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

lemma Rat.eventuallySteady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.EventuallySteady a ↔ ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.2.1 ($ε$-close sequences) -/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

/-- Definition 5.2.3 (Eventually ε-close sequences) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_def (a:Sequence) :
  a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a := by rfl

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

theorem CauchySequence.ext' {a b: CauchySequence} (h: a.seq = b.seq) : a = b := by
  apply CauchySequence.ext _ h
  rw [a.zero, b.zero]

/-- A sequence starting at zero that is Cauchy, can be viewed as a Cauchy sequence.-/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
theorem Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

@[simp] theorem CauchySequence.equiv_iff (a b: CauchySequence) : a ≈ b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by rfl

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy -/
@[simp] theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun (_:ℕ) ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun (_: ℕ) ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/--
  It is convenient in Lean to assign the "dummy" value of 0 to `LIM a` when `a` is not Cauchy.
  This requires Classical logic, because the property of being Cauchy is not computable or
  decidable.
-/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

@[simp] theorem LIM_def {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' ha) := by
  rw [LIM, dif_pos ha]

/-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers) -/
@[simp] theorem Real.eq_lim (x:Real) : ∃ (a:ℕ → ℚ), (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a := by
  apply Quotient.ind _ x; intro a; use (a:ℕ → ℚ)
  observe : ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence
  rw [this, LIM_def (by convert a.cauchy)]
  refine ⟨ a.cauchy, ?_ ⟩
  congr; ext n; simp; replace := congr($this n); simp_all

/-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers) -/
@[simp] theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  . intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff]

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
lemma Sequence.equiv_def (a b: ℕ → ℚ) :
    Equiv a b ↔ ∀ (ε:ℚ), ε > 0 → ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.2.3 (Eventually ε-close sequences) -/
lemma Rat.eventuallyClose_def (ε: ℚ) (a b: Sequence) :
    ε.EventuallyClose a b ↔ ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.2.3 (Eventually ε-close sequences) -/
lemma Rat.eventuallyClose_iff (ε: ℚ) (a b: ℕ → ℚ) :
    ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence) ↔ ∃ N, ∀ n ≥ N, |a n - b n| ≤ ε := by sorry

/-- Definition 5.2.1 ($ε$-close sequences) -/
lemma Rat.closeSeq_def (ε: ℚ) (a b: Sequence) :
    ε.CloseSeq a b ↔ ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n) := by rfl

/--Lemma 5.3.6 (Sum of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)-/
@[simp] theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
@[simp] theorem Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
@[simp] theorem Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
@[simp] theorem Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

/-- Definition 5.3.4 (Addition of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

/-- Definition 5.3.10 (Product of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy) -/
@[simp] theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
@[simp] theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by sorry

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
@[simp] theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by simp_rw [mul_comm]; exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
@[simp] theorem Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
    equiv_trans (mul_equiv_right _ ha hbb') (mul_equiv_left _ hb' haa')

/-- Definition 5.3.9 (Product of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . exact Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy) (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

/-- Proposition 5.3.11 (laws of algebra) -/
noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

open Classical in
/--
  (Not from textbook) Real has the structure of a linear ordering. The order is not computable,
  and so classical logic is required to impose decidability.
-/
noncomputable instance Real.instLinearOrder : LinearOrder Real where
  le_refl := by sorry
  le_trans := by sorry
  lt_iff_le_not_ge := by sorry
  le_antisymm := by sorry
  le_total := by sorry
  toDecidableLE := Classical.decRel _

/-- Exercise 5.4.9 -/
 theorem Real.max_mul (x y :Real) {z:Real} (hz: z.IsPos) : max (x * z) (y * z) = max x y * z := by
  sorry
/- Additional exercise: What happens if z is negative? -/

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-- Exercise 8.5.16: The set of partial orderings on X, ordered by "coarser than",
is itself a partial order. -/
instance PartialOrder.coarserOrder (X : Type) : PartialOrder (PartialOrder X) where
  le p1 p2 := ∀ x y : X, p1.le x y → p2.le x y
  le_refl := by simp
  le_trans p1 p2 p3 h12 h23 := fun x y h => h23 x y (h12 x y h)
  le_antisymm p1 p2 h12 h21 := by ext x y; exact ⟨h12 x y, h21 x y⟩

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 3, minimal context to state Set product and EqualCard,
plus the target theorem. We avoid importing any Analysis.Section_* modules.
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms (from Section 3.1). -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset : Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets). -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects). -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

/-- Axiom 3.2 (Equality of sets) with `[ext]`. -/
@[ext]
 theorem ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

/-- Coerce a Chapter3 set to a type (as a subtype of Object). -/
abbrev toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

end Set
end SetTheory

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

/-- Union instance for sets (pairwise union). -/
instance instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

/-- Axiom 3.4 (Pairwise union). -/
@[simp]
 theorem mem_union (x:Object) (X Y:Set) : x ∈ (X ∪ Y) ↔ (x ∈ X ∨ x ∈ Y) :=
  SetTheory.union_pair_axiom X Y x

/-- Singleton instance for sets. -/
instance instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

@[simp]
 theorem mem_singleton (x a:Object) : x ∈ ({a}:Set) ↔ x = a := SetTheory.singleton_axiom x a

/-- Insert instance for sets: `insert b X = {b} ∪ X`. -/
instance instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := ({x}:Set) ∪ X

/-- Abbreviation for replacement on sets. -/
abbrev replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

/-- Replacement axiom specialized to `Set.replace`. -/
@[simp]
 theorem replacement_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') (y:Object) :
    y ∈ A.replace hP ↔ ∃ x, P x y := SetTheory.replacement_axiom A P hP y

end Set
end SetTheory

open SetTheory.Set

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair). -/
@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst: Object
  snd: Object

/-- Helper: embed an ordered pair as an object (Kuratowski-style). -/
def OrderedPair.toObject : OrderedPair ↪ Object where
  toFun p := ({ (({p.fst}:Set):Object), (({p.fst, p.snd}:Set):Object) }:Set)
  inj' := by
    sorry

/-- Coercion from ordered pairs to objects via Kuratowski encoding. -/
instance OrderedPair.inst_coeObject : Coe OrderedPair Object where
  coe := OrderedPair.toObject

/-- Provide a very small `grind` tactic used in the textbook sources. -/
macro "grind" : tactic => `(tactic| aesop)

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

/-- A technical operation: `{x} × Y` as a set via replacement (Section 3.5). -/
abbrev slice (x:Object) (Y:Set) : Set :=
  Y.replace (P := fun y z ↦ z = (⟨x, y⟩:OrderedPair)) (by grind)

/-- Definition 3.5.4 (Cartesian product) via union and replacement. -/
abbrev cartesian (X Y:Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (X.replace (P := fun x z ↦ z = slice x Y)
    (by intro _ _ _ h; exact h.1.trans h.2.symm))

end Set
end SetTheory

/-- Local notation for the Cartesian product on Chapter3 sets. -/
infixr:70 " ×ˢ " => SetTheory.Set.cartesian

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality). -/
abbrev EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

end Set
end SetTheory

/-- Exercise 3.6.5. You might find `SetTheory.Set.prod_commutator` useful. -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.prod_EqualCard_prod (A B:Set) :
    EqualCard (A ×ˢ B) (B ×ˢ A) := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

namespace Chapter9

abbrev BddOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

open Chapter9

abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

namespace PiecewiseConstantWith

open Classical
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

end PiecewiseConstantWith

namespace PiecewiseConstantOn

open Classical
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

end PiecewiseConstantOn

abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

theorem IntegrableOn.const (c:ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  IntegrableOn (fun _ ↦ c) I ∧ integ (fun _ ↦ c) I = c * |I|ₗ := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 11.4 target theorem.

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals, as in Section 11.1. -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

namespace Partition
instance instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals
end Partition

/-- Constant maps and their values (Section 11.2). -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Piecewise constant functions (Section 11.2). -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

namespace PiecewiseConstantWith
open BigOperators
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ
end PiecewiseConstantWith

namespace PiecewiseConstantOn
open Classical
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

/-- Shorthand used elsewhere in the chapter. -/
noncomputable abbrev integ' {f:ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval} (_:PiecewiseConstantOn f I) := integ f I
end PiecewiseConstantOn

/-- Majorization/minorization (Section 11.3). -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x
abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

/-- Upper and lower integrals (Section 11.3). -/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- The Riemann integral is the upper integral (junk value convention) (Section 11.3). -/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter9
/-- Boundedness on a set (from Section 9, repeated in Section 11.4). -/
abbrev BddOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M
end Chapter9

namespace Chapter11
open Chapter9

/-- Integrable functions (bounded with coinciding lower/upper integrals). -/
noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

/-- Theorem 11.4.1(d) / Exercise 11.4.1 -/ 
theorem IntegrableOn.nonneg {I: BoundedInterval} {f:ℝ → ℝ} (hf: IntegrableOn f I) (hf_nonneg: ∀ x ∈ I, 0 ≤ f x) :
  0 ≤ integ f I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 3 Section 3.6 target theorem card_ssubset.
-/

namespace Chapter3

/- The ability to work in multiple universe is not relevant immediately, but
becomes relevant when constructing models of set theory in the Chapter 3 epilogue. -/
universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Definition 3.1.14.   -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/--
  Definition 3.1.14.
  Note that the strict subset operation in Mathlib is denoted `⊂` rather than `⊊`.
-/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSSubset : HasSSubset Set where
  SSubset X Y := X ⊆ Y ∧ X ≠ Y

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

-- Bridge between ℕ and the set `Nat` of natural numbers
abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

/-- Definition of `Fin` as a set in this development. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

/-- Definition 3.6.5 -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
/-- It is convenient for Lean purposes to give infinite sets the ``junk`` cardinality of zero. -/
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Proposition 3.6.14 (c) / Exercise 3.6.4 -/
-- target theorem
 theorem SetTheory.Set.card_ssubset {X Y:Set} (hX: X.finite) (hY: Y ⊂ X) :
    Y.card < X.card := by sorry

end Chapter3

theorem zpow_ge_zpow {x y:ℚ} {n:ℤ} (hxy: x ≥ y) (hy: y > 0) (hn: n > 0): x^n ≥ y^n := by sorry

import Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Basic

/-!
Self-contained extraction for Chapter 11.4 target theorem IntegrableOn.mono,
with minimal dependent definitions from earlier sections.
-/

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals (Section 11.1) -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- Coercion to sets (Section 11.1) -/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

/-- Membership and subset (Section 11.1) -/
instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I : Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

/-- Endpoints (Section 11.1) -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

/-- Length (Section 11.1) -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

/-- Partitions (Section 11.1) -/
@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Constant and piecewise-constant functions (Section 11.2) -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Piecewise constant predicates (Section 11.2) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Piecewise constant integrals (Section 11.2) -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter9

/-- Boundedness on a set (Section 9.6) -/
abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

namespace Chapter11
open BoundedInterval Chapter9

/-- Majorization/minorization (Section 11.3) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

/-- Upper and lower integrals (Section 11.3) -/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Riemann integral as upper integral (Section 11.3) -/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

/-- Integrability (Section 11.3) -/
noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

/-- Theorem 11.4.1(e) / Exercise 11.4.1 -/ 
 theorem IntegrableOn.mono {I: BoundedInterval} {f g:ℝ → ℝ} (hf: IntegrableOn f I) (hg: IntegrableOn g I)
  (h: MajorizesOn g f I) :
  integ f I ≤ integ g I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

/-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

/-- Exercise 11.3.1 -/
theorem MajorizesOn.anti_symm {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval}:
  ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x = g x ↔ MajorizesOn f g I ∧ MajorizesOn g f I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset : Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X : Set) (Y : Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X : Set) (Y : Set) (F : Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f : Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
      function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

@[ext]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

@[simp] abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

theorem SetTheory.Set.specification_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Prop} {x:Object}
    (h: x ∈ A.specify P) : x ∈ A := (SetTheory.specification_axiom A P).1 x h

@[simp] theorem SetTheory.Set.specification_axiom' {A:Set} (P: A → Prop) (x:A) :
    x.val ∈ A.specify P ↔ P x := (SetTheory.specification_axiom A P).2 x

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := (SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv).symm n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) :
    EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set :=
  ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard,
    { refl := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl,
      symm := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm,
      trans := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans } ⟩

abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

-- TARGET THEOREM
/-- Exercise 3.6.7 -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.injection_iff_card_le {A B:Set} (hA: A.finite) (hB: B.finite) :
    (∃ f:A → B, Function.Injective f) ↔ A.card ≤ B.card := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by
  norm_cast

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 4.3.4 (eps-closeness). -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

/--
A slight generalization of Definition 5.1.3 - definition of ε-steadiness for a sequence with an
arbitrary starting point n₀
-/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- Cauchy sequence (starting from 0) -/
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
lemma CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence.mk' ha = a := by rfl

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := by sorry
     symm := by sorry
     trans := by sorry
  }

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

lemma LIM_def {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' ha) := by
  rw [LIM, dif_pos ha]

lemma CauchySequence.equiv_iff (a b: CauchySequence) : a ≈ b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by rfl

lemma Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  · intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff]

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

lemma Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by sorry

lemma Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  Sequence.equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by simp_rw [mul_comm]; exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

lemma Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
    Sequence.equiv_trans (mul_equiv_right _ ha hbb') (mul_equiv_left _ hb' haa')

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · exact Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy) (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n : ℕ} : OfNat Real n where ofNat := ((n : ℚ) : Real)

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

open Classical in
noncomputable instance Real.instLinearOrder : LinearOrder Real where
  le_refl := by sorry
  le_trans := by sorry
  lt_iff_le_not_ge := by sorry
  le_antisymm := by sorry
  le_total := by sorry
  toDecidableLE := Classical.decRel _

/-- Exercise 5.4.9 -/
theorem Real.max_add (x y z:Real) : max (x + z) (y + z) = max x y + z := by sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals (from Section 11.1) -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := BoundedInterval.toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Constant functions (from Section 11.2) -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Piecewise constant functions (Section 11.2) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

namespace PiecewiseConstantWith

noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

end PiecewiseConstantWith

namespace PiecewiseConstantOn

open Classical

noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

noncomputable abbrev integ' {f:ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval} (_:PiecewiseConstantOn f I) := integ f I

end PiecewiseConstantOn

namespace Chapter9

/-- Boundedness on a set (Section 9.6) -/
abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

open Chapter9

/-- Majorization/minorization (Section 11.3) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Riemann integral as upper integral (Section 11.3) -/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

/-- Theorem 11.4.1(c) / Exercise 11.4.1 -/
theorem IntegrableOn.sub {I: BoundedInterval} {f g:ℝ → ℝ} (hf: IntegrableOn f I) (hg: IntegrableOn g I) :
  IntegrableOn (f - g) I ∧ integ (f - g) I = integ f I - integ g I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := ({x}:Set) ∪ X

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') (y:Object) :
    y ∈ A.replace hP ↔ ∃ x, P x y := SetTheory.replacement_axiom A P hP y

@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.union_axiom (A: Set) (x:Object) :
    x ∈ SetTheory.union A ↔ ∃ (S:Set), x ∈ S ∧ (S:Object) ∈ A := SetTheory.union_axiom A x

instance SetTheory.Set.inst_pow : Pow Set Set where
  pow := SetTheory.pow

@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst : Object
  snd : Object

def OrderedPair.toObject : OrderedPair ↪ Object where
  toFun p := ({ (({p.fst}:Set):Object), (({p.fst, p.snd}:Set):Object) }: Set)
  inj' := by
    sorry

instance OrderedPair.inst_coeObject : Coe OrderedPair Object where
  coe := OrderedPair.toObject

abbrev SetTheory.Set.slice (x : Object) (Y : Set) : Set :=
  Y.replace (P := fun y z ↦ z = (⟨x, y⟩ : OrderedPair)) (by grind)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.cartesian (X Y : Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (X.replace (P := fun x z ↦ z = slice x Y)
    (by intro _ _ _ ⟨h1, h2⟩; exact h1.trans h2.symm))

instance SetTheory.Set.inst_SProd : SProd Set Set Set where
  sprod := SetTheory.Set.cartesian

abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

/-- Exercise 3.6.6. You may find `SetTheory.Set.curry_equiv` useful. -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.pow_pow_EqualCard_pow_prod (A B C:Set) :
    EqualCard ((A ^ B) ^ C) (A ^ (B ×ˢ C)) := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

namespace Chapter11

-- From Section 11.1

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instEmpty : EmptyCollection BoundedInterval where
  emptyCollection := Ioo 0 0

open Classical in
/-- This is to make Finsets of BoundedIntervals work properly -/
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval := instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

-- From Section 11.2

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

-- From Section 9.6
namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.6.1 -/
abbrev BddAboveOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, f x ≤ M

abbrev BddBelowOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, -M ≤ f x

abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

-- From Section 11.3
open BoundedInterval Chapter9

/-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

/-- Definition 11.3.2 (Uppper and lower Riemann integrals )-/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Definition 11.3.4 (Riemann integral)
As we permit junk values, the simplest definition for the Riemann integral is the upper integral.-/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

-- Target theorem from Section 11.4
open Classical in
/-- Theorem 11.4.1 (g)  / Exercise 11.4.1 -/
theorem IntegrableOn.of_extend {I J: BoundedInterval} (hIJ: I ⊆ J)
  {f: ℝ → ℝ} (h: IntegrableOn f I) :
  IntegrableOn (fun x ↦ if x ∈ I then f x else 0) J := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset : Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom (A : Set) (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom (A : Set) (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X : Set) (Y : Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X : Set) (Y : Set) (F : Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f : Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
      function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom (A : Set) (x : Object) : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

@[ext]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y : Set} (h : ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

instance SetTheory.Set.instIntersection : Inter Set where
  inter X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∈ Y)

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

@[simp]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.mem_union (x : Object) (X Y : Set) : x ∈ (X ∪ Y) ↔ (x ∈ X ∨ x ∈ Y) :=
  SetTheory.union_pair_axiom X Y x

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n : ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := (SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv).symm n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  (Nat).specify (fun m ↦ ((m : ℕ) < n))

abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y : Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X : Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y : Set} (h : EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z : Set}
    (h1 : EqualCard X Y) (h2 : EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set :=
  ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X : Set) (n : ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X : Set) : Prop := ∃ n : ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X : Set) : ℕ :=
  if h : X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Exercise 3.6.9 -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.card_union_add_card_inter {A B : Set}
    (hA : A.finite) (hB : B.finite) :
    A.card + B.card = (A ∪ B).card + (A ∩ B).card := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

  specify A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set

    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)

  replacement_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)

  nat : Set

export SetTheory (Set Object)

@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst : Object
  snd : Object

namespace OrderedPair

def toObject : OrderedPair ↪ Object
    where
  toFun p := ({(({ p.fst } : Set) : Object), (({ p.fst, p.snd } : Set) : Object)} : Set)
  inj' := by
    sorry

instance inst_coeObject : Coe OrderedPair Object where
  coe := toObject

end OrderedPair

abbrev SetTheory.Set.slice (x : Object) (Y : Set) : Set :=
  Y.replace (P := fun y z ↦ z = (⟨x, y⟩ : OrderedPair)) (by
    intro x y y'
    intro h
    exact h.1.trans h.2.symm)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.cartesian (X Y : Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (X.replace (P := fun x z ↦ z = slice x Y) (by
    intro x y y'
    intro h
    exact h.1.trans h.2.symm))

infixl:70 " ×ˢ " => SetTheory.Set.cartesian

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter11
open BoundedInterval

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.6.1 -/
abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

namespace Chapter11
open BoundedInterval Chapter9

/-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

/-- Definition 11.3.2 (Uppper and lower Riemann integrals )-/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Definition 11.3.4 (Riemann integral)
As we permit junk values, the simplest definition for the Riemann integral is the upper integral.-/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

open Classical in
/-- Theorem 11.4.1 (g)  / Exercise 11.4.1 -/
theorem IntegrableOn.of_extend' {I J: BoundedInterval} (hIJ: I ⊆ J)
  {f: ℝ → ℝ} (h: IntegrableOn f I) :
  integ (fun x ↦ if x ∈ I then f x else 0) J = integ f I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := emptyset

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

/-- Exercise 3.6.8 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.surjection_from_injection {A B:Set} (hA: A ≠ ∅) (f: A → B)
  (hf: Function.Injective f) : ∃ g:B → A, Function.Surjective g := by sorry

end Chapter3

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Define a coercion so any set can be treated as a type, as its subtype of elements. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

namespace SetTheory.Set

abbrev specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Axioms of infinity: expose a type alias and coercions between our `Nat` set and `ℕ`. -/
abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

def nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := nat_equiv n

instance toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

instance inst_pow : Pow Set Set where pow := SetTheory.pow

abbrev Fin (n : ℕ) : Set := nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

abbrev EqualCard (X Y : Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
theorem EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
theorem EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

abbrev has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

abbrev finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
noncomputable def card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Proposition 3.6.14 (f) / Exercise 3.6.4 -/
 theorem card_pow {X Y:Set} (hY: Y.finite) (hX: X.finite) :
    (Y ^ X).finite ∧ (Y ^ X).card = Y.card ^ X.card := by sorry

end SetTheory.Set
