/-! Self-contained extraction for Chapter5.Real.min_self -/

/-- Definition 4.3.4 (eps-closeness) from Section 4.3. -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by
      grind

/--
If `a : ℕ → ℚ` is used in a context where a `Sequence` is expected, automatically coerce `a` to `Sequence.ofNatFun a` (which will be pretty-printed as `↑a`)
-/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

lemma Rat.steady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

lemma Rat.eventuallySteady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.EventuallySteady a ↔ ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N) := by rfl

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_def (a:Sequence) :
  a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a := by rfl

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

 theorem CauchySequence.ext' {a b: CauchySequence} (h: a.seq = b.seq) : a = b := by
  apply CauchySequence.ext _ h
  rw [a.zero, b.zero]

/-- A sequence starting at zero that is Cauchy, can be viewed as a Cauchy sequence.-/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
 theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
 theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
 theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.1 ($ε$-close sequences) -/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
 theorem Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
 instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

 theorem CauchySequence.equiv_iff (a b: CauchySequence) : a ≈ b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by rfl

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy -/
 theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/--
  It is convenient in Lean to assign the "dummy" value of 0 to `LIM a` when `a` is not Cauchy.
  This requires Classical logic, because the property of being Cauchy is not computable or
  decidable.
-/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

 theorem LIM_def {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' ha) := by
  rw [LIM, dif_pos ha]

/-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers) -/
 theorem Real.eq_lim (x:Real) : ∃ (a:ℕ → ℚ), (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a := by
  apply Quotient.ind _ x; intro a; use (a:ℕ → ℚ)
  observe : ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence
  rw [this, LIM_def (by convert a.cauchy)]
  refine ⟨ a.cauchy, ?_ ⟩
  congr; ext n; simp; replace := congr($this n); simp_all

/-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers) -/
 theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  . intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff]

/--Lemma 5.3.6 (Sum of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)-/
 theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

/-- Definition 5.3.4 (Addition of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy) -/
 theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by simp_rw [mul_comm]; exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
    equiv_trans (mul_equiv_right _ ha hbb') (mul_equiv_left _ hb' haa')

/-- Definition 5.3.9 (Product of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . exact Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy) (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

/-- Proposition 5.3.11 (laws of algebra) -/
noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

 theorem Real.sub_eq_add_neg (x y:Real) : x - y = x + (-y) := rfl

namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.4.1 (sequences bounded away from zero with sign). Sequences are indexed to start
  from zero as this is more convenient for Mathlib purposes.
-/
abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

/-- Definition 5.4.1 (sequences bounded away from zero with sign). -/
abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals) -/
instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := Real.IsNeg (x - y)

/-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals) -/
instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

open Classical in
/--
  (Not from textbook) Real has the structure of a linear ordering. The order is not computable,
  and so classical logic is required to impose decidability.
-/
noncomputable instance Real.instLinearOrder : LinearOrder Real where
  le_refl := sorry
  le_trans := sorry
  lt_iff_le_not_ge := sorry
  le_antisymm := sorry
  le_total := sorry
  toDecidableLE := Classical.decRel _

/-- The standard min on a linear order -/
 theorem Real.min_eq (x y:Real) : min x y = if x ≤ y then x else y := rfl

/-- Exercise 5.4.9 -/
 theorem Real.min_self (x:Real) : min x x = x := by sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

@[ext]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

abbrev SetTheory.Set.iUnion (I: Set) (A: I → Set) : Set :=
  SetTheory.union (SetTheory.Set.replace I (P := fun α S ↦ S = A α)
    (by
      intro _ _ _ h; exact (h.1).trans h.2.symm))

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := (SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv).symm n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n:ℕ) : Set := nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m:ℕ) < n)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) :
    SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set}
    (h1: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) (h2: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y Z) :
    SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set :=
  { r := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard
  , iseqv :=
    { refl := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl
    , symm := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm
    , trans := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans } }

abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, SetTheory.Set.has_card X n

open Classical in
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Exercise 3.6.10 -/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.pigeonhole_principle {n:ℕ} {A: SetTheory.Set.Fin n → Set}
  (hA: ∀ i, (A i).finite) (hAcard: (SetTheory.Set.iUnion _ A).card > n) :
  ∃ i, (A i).card ≥ 2 := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 5 (Sections 5.1–5.4) sufficient to typecheck
Chapter5.Real.dist_lt_iff.
-/

/-- From Section 4.3: epsilon-closeness on rationals -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/-- Section 5.1: Sequences of rationals -/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℚ, viewed as sequences starting at 0. -/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn; simp [hn]

/-- Coercion from functions to sequences. -/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn; simp [hn]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by
  rfl

lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by rfl

/-- Start a sequence from a chosen index. -/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

/-- Section 5.1: Eventually steady (defined outside namespace in the textbook) -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

/-- Section 5.2: ε-close and eventually ε-close sequences (top-level) -/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Cauchy sequences (Section 5.1): definition for a sequence starting at arbitrary n₀,
    specialized in text to start at 0. -/
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

/-- Cauchy sequences starting at 0 (Section 5.3). -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

/-- A Cauchy sequence built from a Cauchy function a : ℕ → ℚ. -/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    have h' := (a:Sequence).vanish n (by simpa [Sequence.n0_coe] using hn)
    simpa using h'
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

/-- Equivalent sequences (Section 5.2). -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

lemma Sequence.equiv_def (a b: ℕ → ℚ) :
    Equiv a b ↔ ∀ (ε:ℚ), ε > 0 → ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence) := by rfl

/-- Setoid on Cauchy sequences (Section 5.3). -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := by sorry
     symm := by sorry
     trans := by sorry
  }

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy (Section 5.3). -/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/-- Zero Cauchy sequence instance (Section 5.3). -/
instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

/-- Reals as a quotient of Cauchy sequences (Section 5.3). -/
abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/-- Formal limit (Section 5.3). -/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

/-- Equality of limits corresponds to sequence equivalence (Section 5.3). -/
 theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  -- We retain the exact statement; proof omitted.
  sorry

/-- Lemmas about Cauchy sequences under addition/multiplication (statements only). -/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

theorem Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by sorry

theorem Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by sorry

theorem Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') := by sorry

/-- Addition on reals (Section 5.3). -/
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_)

/-- Multiplicative Cauchy lemmas and equivalences (statements only). -/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by sorry

theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by
  simp_rw [mul_comm]
  exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

theorem Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') := by
  sorry

/-- Product on reals (Section 5.3). -/
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . exact
          Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy)
            (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_)

/-- Rational embedding into reals (Section 5.3). -/
instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

/-- OfNat instance for reals (Section 5.3). -/
instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

/-- Negation on reals via multiplication by -1 (Section 5.3). -/
noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

/-- Additive group structure on reals (Section 5.3). -/
noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

/-- Section 5.4: Positivity/negativity via approximating sequences. -/
abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Order on reals in terms of negativity of differences (Section 5.4). -/
instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x-y).IsNeg

/-- Non-strict order induced by < and = (Section 5.4). -/
instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

open Classical in
/-- Real is a linear order (noncomputable, Section 5.4). -/
noncomputable instance Real.instLinearOrder : LinearOrder Real where
  le_refl := by sorry
  le_trans := by sorry
  lt_iff_le_not_ge := by sorry
  le_antisymm := by sorry
  le_total := by sorry
  toDecidableLE := Classical.decRel _

/-- Exercise 5.4.6 -/
theorem Real.dist_lt_iff (ε x y:Real) : |x-y| < ε ↔ y-ε < x ∧ x < y+ε := by sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter11

-- Bounded intervals (Section 11.1)
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override ||
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

abbrev BoundedInterval.joins (K I J: BoundedInterval) : Prop := (I:Set ℝ) ∩ (J:Set ℝ) = ∅
  ∧ (K:Set ℝ) = (I:Set ℝ) ∪ (J:Set ℝ) ∧ |K|ₗ = |I|ₗ + |J|ₗ

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

-- Section 11.2: Piecewise constant functions and their integral
-- Definition 11.2.1
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I)
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions II)
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

-- Section 9.6: Boundedness on sets
namespace Chapter9

-- Definition 9.6.1
abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

-- Section 11.3: Upper/lower integrals and the Riemann integral
open BoundedInterval Chapter9

-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions)
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

-- Definition 11.3.2 (Uppper and lower Riemann integrals )
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

-- Definition 11.3.4 (Riemann integral)
-- As we permit junk values, the simplest definition for the Riemann integral is the upper integral.
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

-- Theorem 11.4.1 (h) (Laws of integration) / Exercise 11.4.1
theorem IntegrableOn.join {I J K: BoundedInterval} (hIJK: K.joins I J)
  {f: ℝ → ℝ} (h: IntegrableOn f K) :
  IntegrableOn f I ∧ IntegrableOn f J ∧ integ f K = integ f I + integ f J := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

theorem Nat.no_infinite_descent : ¬ ∃ a:ℕ → ℕ, ∀ n, a (n+1) < a n := by

theorem closure_inter (X Y:Set ℝ): closure (X ∩ Y) ⊆ closure X ∩ closure Y := by

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 3 Section 3.6 target theorem.
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

/-- Definition 3.1.14.   -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

/-- Axiom 3.7 (Axiom of replacement) -/
@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.replacement_axiom {A:Set} {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') (y:Object) :
    y ∈ A.replace hP ↔ ∃ x, P x y := SetTheory.replacement_axiom A P hP y

/-- Abbreviation for specification (from Section 3.1). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat nat n where
  ofNat := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast nat where
  natCast n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe nat ℕ where
  coe n := (SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv).symm n

/-- Image of a set under a function between sets (Section 3.6). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.image {X Y : Set} (f : X → Y) (S : Set) : Set :=
  X.replace (P := fun x y ↦ f x = y ∧ x.val ∈ S) (by simp_all)

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

/-- Finite cardinals as sets: `Fin n` -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

/-- Definition 3.6.5 -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X : Set) : Prop := ∃ n : ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
/-- It is convenient for Lean purposes to give infinite sets the ``junk`` cardinality of zero. -/
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Exercise 3.6.11 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.two_to_two_iff {X Y:Set} (f: X → Y): Function.Injective f ↔
    ∀ S ⊆ X, S.card = 2 → (image f S).card = 2 := by sorry

end Chapter3

theorem closure_subset {X Y:Set ℝ} (h: X ⊆ Y): closure X ⊆ closure Y := by

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq {X Y:Set} (h: (X: Object) = (Y: Object)) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.set_to_object.inj' h

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.coe_eq_iff (X Y:Set) : (X: Object) = (Y: Object) ↔  X = Y :=
  ⟨ SetTheory.Set.coe_eq, by rintro rfl; rfl ⟩

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

-- Below are some API for handling coercions. This may not be the optimal way to set things up.
instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := nat_equiv n

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset : Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P : Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X : Set) (Y : Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X : Set) (Y : Set) (F : Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f : Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
      function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

@[ext]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y : Set} (h : ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

abbrev toSubtype (A : Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

end Set
end SetTheory

instance : CoeSort (SetTheory.Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

abbrev specify (A : Set) (P : A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

theorem specification_axiom {A : Set} {P : A → Prop} {x : Object}
    (h : x ∈ A.specify P) : x ∈ A :=
  (SetTheory.specification_axiom A P).1 x h

theorem specification_axiom' {A : Set} (P : A → Prop) (x : A) :
    x.val ∈ A.specify P ↔ P x :=
  (SetTheory.specification_axiom A P).2 x

@[simp]
 theorem specification_axiom'' {A : Set} (P : A → Prop) (x : Object) :
    x ∈ A.specify P ↔ ∃ h : x ∈ A, P ⟨x, h⟩ := by
  constructor
  · intro h; exact ⟨specification_axiom h, by
      have := (SetTheory.specification_axiom A P).2
      simpa [specify] using (this ⟨x, specification_axiom h⟩).mp h⟩
  · rintro ⟨h, hP⟩
    have := (SetTheory.specification_axiom A P).2
    simpa [specify] using (this ⟨x, h⟩).mpr hP

abbrev replace (A : Set) {P : A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set :=
  SetTheory.replace A P hP

@[simp]
 theorem replacement_axiom {A : Set} {P : A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') (y : Object) :
    y ∈ A.replace hP ↔ ∃ x, P x y :=
  SetTheory.replacement_axiom A P hP y

end Set
end SetTheory

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

namespace SetTheory

def nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance instOfNat {n : ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := nat_equiv n

instance instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := nat_equiv n

instance toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

end Set
end SetTheory

namespace Object

instance instNatCast : NatCast Object where
  natCast n := (n : Chapter3.Nat).val

instance instOfNat {n : ℕ} : OfNat Object n where
  ofNat := ((n : Chapter3.Nat) : Object)

end Object
end SetTheory

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

instance inst_pow : Pow Set Set where
  pow := SetTheory.pow

@[coe]
 def coe_of_fun {X Y : Set} (f : X → Y) : Object :=
  SetTheory.function_to_object X Y f

instance inst_coe_of_fun {X Y : Set} : CoeOut (X → Y) Object where
  coe := coe_of_fun

@[simp]
 theorem powerset_axiom {X Y : Set} (F : Object) :
    F ∈ (X ^ Y) ↔ ∃ f : Y → X, f = F := SetTheory.powerset_axiom X Y F

end Set
end SetTheory

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

abbrev Fin (n : ℕ) : Set := SetTheory.nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

 theorem mem_Fin (n : ℕ) (x : Object) : x ∈ Fin n ↔ ∃ m, m < n ∧ x = m := by
  sorry

abbrev Fin_mk (n m : ℕ) (h : m < n) : Fin n := ⟨(m : Object),
  (mem_Fin n (m : Object)).2 ⟨m, h, rfl⟩⟩

end Set
end SetTheory

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

abbrev EqualCard (X Y : Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
 theorem Set.EqualCard.refl (X : Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
 theorem Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y : Set} (h : EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
 theorem Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z : Set} (h1 : EqualCard X Y) (h2 : EqualCard Y Z) :
    EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

instance Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := by
  classical
  refine
    { r := EqualCard
      iseqv := ?_ }
  refine
    { refl := ?_ , symm := ?_ , trans := ?_ }
  · intro X; exact Set.EqualCard.refl X
  · intro X Y h; exact Set.EqualCard.symm h
  · intro X Y Z h1 h2; exact Set.EqualCard.trans h1 h2

abbrev has_card (X : Set) (n : ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

abbrev finite (X : Set) : Prop := ∃ n : ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
noncomputable def card (X : Set) : ℕ := if h : X.finite then h.choose else 0

end Set
end SetTheory

namespace SetTheory
namespace Set

noncomputable def pow_fun_equiv {A B : Set} : ↑(A ^ B) ≃ (B → A) where
  toFun := by sorry
  invFun := by sorry
  left_inv := by sorry
  right_inv := by sorry

 def Permutations (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  (Fin n ^ Fin n).specify (fun F ↦ Function.Bijective (pow_fun_equiv F))

noncomputable def Permutations_toFun {n : ℕ} (p : Permutations n) : (Fin n) → (Fin n) := by
  have := p.property
  simp only [Permutations, specification_axiom'', powerset_axiom] at this
  exact this.choose.choose

 theorem Permutations_bijective {n : ℕ} (p : Permutations n) :
    Function.Bijective (Permutations_toFun p) := by sorry

noncomputable def perm_equiv_equiv {n : ℕ} : Permutations n ≃ (Fin n ≃ Fin n) :=
  { toFun := fun p => Equiv.ofBijective (Permutations_toFun p) (Permutations_bijective p)
    invFun := by sorry
    left_inv := by sorry
    right_inv := by sorry }

 def Fin.last (n : ℕ) : Fin (n + 1) := Fin_mk _ n (by omega)

end Set
end SetTheory

open SetTheory SetTheory.Set

/-- Exercise 3.6.12 (i), second part -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.Permutations_ih (n: ℕ):
    (Permutations (n + 1)).card = (n + 1) * (Permutations n).card := by
  let S i := (Permutations (n + 1)).specify (fun p ↦ perm_equiv_equiv p (Fin.last n) = i)

  have hSe : ∀ i, S i ≈ Permutations n := by
    intro i
    -- Hint: you might find `perm_equiv_equiv`, `Fin.succAbove`, and `Fin.predAbove` useful.
    have equiv : S i ≃ Permutations n := sorry
    use equiv, equiv.injective, equiv.surjective

  -- Hint: you might find `card_iUnion_card_disjoint` and `Permutations_finite` useful.
  sorry

end Chapter3

  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

  x ∈ I ↔ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ) := by rfl

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

namespace Chapter11

/-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

/-- Definition 11.3.2 (Uppper and lower Riemann integrals )-/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Definition 11.3.4 (Riemann integral)
As we permit junk values, the simplest definition for the Riemann integral is the upper integral.-/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.6.1 -/
abbrev BddAboveOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, f x ≤ M

abbrev BddBelowOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, -M ≤ f x

abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

namespace Chapter11

open Chapter9

/-- Integrable functions on an interval (Section 11.3) -/
noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

    sorry

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := (SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv).symm n

instance SetTheory.Set.inst_pow : Pow Set Set where pow := SetTheory.pow

noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.pow_fun_equiv {A B : Set} : ↑(A ^ B) ≃ (B → A) where
  toFun := by exact sorry
  invFun := by exact sorry
  left_inv := by exact sorry
  right_inv := by exact sorry

abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, X.has_card n

def SetTheory.Set.Permutations (n: ℕ): Set := (Fin n ^ Fin n).specify (fun F ↦
    Function.Bijective (pow_fun_equiv F))

theorem SetTheory.Set.Permutations_finite (n: ℕ): (Permutations n).finite := by sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 5 Section 5.4 target theorem Real.rat_between.
We copy only the minimal transitive dependency needed for parsing/typechecking.
-/

/-- From Section 4.3: definition of ε-closeness on ℚ -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y : ℚ) := |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). -/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by
      grind

/-- If `a : ℕ → ℚ` is used in a context where a `Sequence` is expected, automatically coerce. -/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by
    grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by
  norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

/-- start a sequence from index n₁ (junk if n₁ < n₀) -/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.1.3 (ε-steadiness), generalized to arbitrary starting point. -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady). -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

/-- From Section 5.2: ε-close and eventually ε-close sequences -/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Cauchy sequences. -/
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

/-- A sequence starting at zero that is Cauchy, can be viewed as a Cauchy sequence.-/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy -/
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
lemma Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := by
       sorry
     symm := by
       sorry
     trans := by
       sorry
  }

lemma CauchySequence.equiv_iff (a b: CauchySequence) : a ≈ b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by rfl

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/--
  It is convenient in Lean to assign the "dummy" value of 0 to `LIM a` when `a` is not Cauchy.
  This requires Classical logic, because the property of being Cauchy is not computable or
  decidable.
-/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

/-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers) -/
lemma Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  . intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb, CauchySequence.equiv_iff]

/--Lemma 5.3.6 (Sum of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)-/
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
lemma Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
lemma Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
lemma Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

/-- Definition 5.3.4 (Addition of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy) -/
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by simp_rw [mul_comm]; exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
lemma Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
    equiv_trans (mul_equiv_right _ ha hbb') (mul_equiv_left _ hb' haa')

/-- Definition 5.3.9 (Product of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . exact Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy) (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

/-- OfNat instance for Real via ℚ -/
instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

/-- Proposition 5.3.11 (laws of algebra) -/
noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

/-- Definition 5.4.1 (sequences bounded away from zero with sign). -/
abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

/-- Definition 5.4.1 (sequences bounded away from zero with sign). -/
abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals) -/
instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x-y).IsNeg

/-- Proposition 5.4.14 / Exercise 5.4.5 -/
 theorem Real.rat_between {x y:Real} (hxy: x < y) : ∃ q:ℚ, x < (q:Real) ∧ (q:Real) < y := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib/Topology/Instances/Real
import Mathlib/Data/Real/Basic

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

open BoundedInterval

theorem integ_zero {a b:ℝ} (hab: a ≤ b) (f: ℝ → ℝ) (hf: ContinuousOn f (Icc a b))
  (hnonneg: MajorizesOn f (fun _ ↦ 0) (Icc a b)) (hinteg : integ f (Icc a b) = 0) :
  ∀ x ∈ Icc a b, f x = 0 := by
    sorry

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals (as in Section 11.1) -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- Coercion to sets of real numbers -/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instEmpty : EmptyCollection BoundedInterval where
  emptyCollection := Ioo 0 0

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.coe_empty : ((∅ : BoundedInterval):Set ℝ) = ∅ := by
  simp [toSet]

open Classical in
/-- Decidable equality for bounded intervals (for Finset usage) -/
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval := instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioo (a b:ℝ) : (Ioo a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioo a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Icc (a b:ℝ) : (Icc a b : Set ℝ) = .Icc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioc (a b:ℝ) : (Ioc a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ico (a b:ℝ) : (Ico a b : Set ℝ) = .Ico a b := by rfl

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.mem_iff (I: BoundedInterval) (x:ℝ) :
  x ∈ I ↔ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ) := by rfl

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

@[simp]
 theorem BoundedInterval.subset_iff (I J: BoundedInterval) :
  I ⊆ J ↔ (I:Set ℝ) ⊆ (J:Set ℝ) := by rfl

/-- Left endpoint of a bounded interval -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

/-- Right endpoint of a bounded interval -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

/-- Length of a bounded interval -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Constant functions and their values (Section 11.2) -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

/-- Constant on a set -/
abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Constant value on a set -/
noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Piecewise constant on a fixed partition -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Piecewise constant on an interval -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Piecewise constant integral on a fixed partition -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Piecewise constant integral on an interval -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter9

/-- Boundedness on a set (Section 9.6) -/
abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

namespace Chapter11
open BoundedInterval Chapter9

/-- Majorization/minorization (Section 11.3) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x
abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

theorem MinorizesOn.iff (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : MinorizesOn g f I ↔ MajorizesOn f g I := by rfl

/-- Upper and lower Riemann integrals (Section 11.3) -/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Riemann integrability on a bounded interval (Section 11.3) -/
noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

/-- Theorem 11.4.5 / Exercise 11.4.3.  The objective here is to create a shorter proof than the one above.-/
theorem IntegrableOn.min {I: BoundedInterval} {f g:ℝ → ℝ} (hf: IntegrableOn f I) (hg: IntegrableOn g I) :
  IntegrableOn (f ⊓ g) I  := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.BoundedBy (a:Sequence) (M:ℚ) : Prop := ∀ n, |a n| ≤ M

abbrev Sequence.IsBounded (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∃ M ≥ 0, a.BoundedBy M

/-- Exercise 5.1.2 -/
theorem Sequence.isBounded_add {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsBounded) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsBounded):
    (a + b:Sequence).IsBounded := by
  sorry

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

-- Minimal pieces from Section 4.3 used downstream
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y : ℚ) : Prop := |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    have hneg : ¬ n ≥ 0 := by exact Int.not_le.mpr hn
    have : (if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0) = 0 := by simp [hneg]
    simpa using this

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := rfl

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := rfl

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    have : ¬ n ≥ n₀ := by exact Int.not_le.mpr hn
    simp [this]

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → Rat.Close ε (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, Rat.CloseSeq ε (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, Rat.Close ε (a n) (a m)

abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, Rat.Steady ε (a.from N)

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), Rat.EventuallySteady ε a

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), Rat.EventuallyClose ε (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by sorry
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
lemma CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  sorry

@[simp]
lemma CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv :=
    { refl := by sorry
      symm := by sorry
      trans := by sorry }

theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ : ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      sorry)

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      sorry)

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦ Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

theorem Real.lt_trans {x y z:Real} (hxy: x < y) (hyz: y < z) : x < z := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Factorial

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 3 Section 3.6 target theorem.
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

/-- Axiom 3.6 (axiom of specification) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

-- Coerce elements of the set `Nat` to natural numbers.
instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv.symm n

-- (not strictly needed, but mirrors Section 3.1)
instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

/-- Provide `^` on sets via the `pow` field from the axioms. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.inst_pow : Pow Set Set where pow := SetTheory.pow

namespace SetTheory.Set

/-- `Fin n` as a set, following Section 3.6. -/
abbrev Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality) -/
abbrev EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
 theorem EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
 theorem EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
 theorem EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

/-- Definition 3.6.5 -/
abbrev has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

/-- `finite` in terms of `has_card`. -/
abbrev finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical
/-- It is convenient for Lean purposes to give infinite sets the ``junk`` cardinality of zero. -/
noncomputable def card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Pow-to-function equivalence, as in Section 3.6. -/
noncomputable def pow_fun_equiv {A B : Set} : ↑(A ^ B) ≃ (B → A) where
  toFun := sorry
  invFun := sorry
  left_inv := sorry
  right_inv := sorry

/-- Exercise 3.6.12 -/
 def Permutations (n: ℕ): Set := (Fin n ^ Fin n).specify (fun F ↦
    Function.Bijective (pow_fun_equiv F))

end SetTheory.Set

open SetTheory.Set

namespace SetTheory.Set

/-- Exercise 3.6.12 (ii) -/
 theorem Permutations_card (n: ℕ):
    (Permutations n).card = n.factorial := by sorry

end SetTheory.Set

end Chapter3
