open scoped BigOperators

namespace Chapter11

/-! From Section 11.1: bounded intervals and partitions (minimal slice) -/

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-! From Section 11.8: constant functions on sets, left/right limits, α-length, and RS integrals -/

abbrev Constant {X Y : Type} (f : X → Y) : Prop :=
  ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

abbrev ConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop :=
  Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y : Type} [hY : Nonempty Y] (f : X → Y) : Y :=
  if h : Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : ℝ :=
  constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev right_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ := lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Ioi x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev left_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ := lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Iio x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev α_length (α : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Icc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
  | Ico a b => if a ≤ b then (left_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
  | Ioc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0
  | Ioo a b => if a < b then (left_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0

notation3:max α"["I"]ₗ" => α_length α I

abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J : Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) (α : ℝ → ℝ) : ℝ :=
  ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J : Set ℝ) * α[J]ₗ

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) (α : ℝ → ℝ) : ℝ :=
  if h : PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ f h.choose α else 0

/-- Analogue of Theorem 11.2.16 (a) (Laws of integration) / Exercise 11.8.3 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ_add {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval}
  (hf: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) (hg: PiecewiseConstantOn g I) {α:ℝ → ℝ} (hα: Monotone α):
  RS_integ (f + g) I α = RS_integ f I α + RS_integ g I α := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic

/--
  This definition needs to be made outside of the Section 4.3 namespace for technical reasons.
-/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

/-!
Section 5.1: Sequences and Cauchy sequences
-/
namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by
      grind

/--
If `a : ℕ → ℚ` is used in a context where a `Sequence` is expected, automatically coerce `a` to `Sequence.ofNatFun a` (which will be pretty-printed as `↑a`)
-/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n n₀:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) (h: n ≥ n₀) :
    (Sequence.mk' n₀ a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

/--
A slight generalization of Definition 5.1.3 - definition of ε-steadiness for a sequence with an
arbitrary starting point n₀
-/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

lemma Rat.steady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

lemma Rat.eventuallySteady_def (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) :
  ε.EventuallySteady a ↔ ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N) := by rfl

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

lemma Sequence.isCauchy_def (a:Sequence) :
  a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a := by rfl

end Chapter5

/-!
Section 5.2: Equivalent sequences
-/

/-- Definition 5.2.1 ($ε$-close sequences) -/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

/-- Definition 5.2.3 (Eventually ε-close sequences) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
lemma Sequence.equiv_def (a b: ℕ → ℚ) :
    Equiv a b ↔ ∀ (ε:ℚ), ε > 0 → ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence) := by rfl

end Chapter5

/-!
Section 5.3: Construction of the real numbers
-/
namespace Chapter5

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

theorem CauchySequence.ext' {a b: CauchySequence} (h: a.seq = b.seq) : a = b := by
  apply CauchySequence.ext _ h
  rw [a.zero, b.zero]

/-- A sequence starting at zero that is Cauchy, can be viewed as a Cauchy sequence.-/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by aesop
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
theorem Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

theorem CauchySequence.equiv_iff (a b: CauchySequence) : a ≈ b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by rfl

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy -/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/--
  It is convenient in Lean to assign the "dummy" value of 0 to `LIM a` when `a` is not Cauchy.
  This requires Classical logic, because the property of being Cauchy is not computable or
  decidable.
-/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

/-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers) -/
theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.6 (Sum of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)-/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
theorem Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') := by
  sorry

/-- Definition 5.3.4 (Addition of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

end Chapter5

/-!
Section 5.4: Ordering the reals (positivity)
-/
namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.4.1 (sequences bounded away from zero with sign). Sequences are indexed to start
  from zero as this is more convenient for Mathlib purposes.
-/
abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Proposition 5.4.4 (basic properties of positive reals) / Exercise 5.4.1-/
theorem Real.pos_add {x y:Real} (hx: x.IsPos) (hy: y.IsPos) : (x+y).IsPos := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

/-! Self-contained slice for Chapter5 Real ordering minimal context -/

/-- From Section 4.3: eps-closeness on rationals -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Section_4_3

abbrev dist (x y : ℚ) := |x - y|

/-- Definition 4.2 (Distance). -/
theorem dist_eq (x y: ℚ) : dist x y = |x-y| := rfl

/-- eps-closeness iff abs difference ≤ ε -/
theorem close_iff (ε x y:ℚ): ε.Close x y ↔ |x - y| ≤ ε := by rfl

/-- reflexivity of closeness at ε=0 -/
theorem close_refl (x:ℚ) : (0:ℚ).Close x x := by
  simpa [Rat.Close, sub_self]

/-- Proposition 4.3.7(d): add_close (kept with sorry as in source) -/
theorem add_close {ε δ x y z w:ℚ} (hxy: ε.Close x y) (hzw: δ.Close z w) :
    (ε + δ).Close (x+z) (y+w) := by
  -- exact proof omitted in this slice
  sorry

end Section_4_3

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.1.1: Sequence of rationals indexed by ℤ with vanishing before n₀ -/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be used as functions ℤ → ℚ -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/-- Coerce functions ℕ → ℚ to sequences starting at 0 -/
@[coe]
 def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by
      grind

/-- Automatically coerce ℕ → ℚ to sequences -/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by
  norm_cast

/-- Start a sequence from N (as in §5.1) -/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.1 ($ε$-close sequences) at top-level Rat namespace -/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

/-- Definition 5.2.3 (Eventually ε-close sequences) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

/-- Eventually ε-steady from Section 5.1 (defined outside namespace) -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Cauchy sequences (as in Section 5.1) -/
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

/-- View a Cauchy sequence as a function ℕ → ℚ -/
instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

/-- For a sequence starting at zero that is Cauchy, make a CauchySequence -/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    have hn' : n < (a:Sequence).n₀ := by simpa [Sequence.n0_coe (a:=a)] using hn
    simpa using (a:Sequence).vanish n hn'
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  -- original proof uses Sequence.ext; keep as sorry
  sorry

/-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy (from §5.3) -/
 theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/-- Equivalent sequences of rationals (from §5.2) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- transitivity of Equiv (from §5.3) -/
 theorem Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by
  sorry

/-- Setoid identifying equivalent Cauchy sequences -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     , symm := sorry
     , trans := by intro a b c hab hbc; exact Sequence.equiv_trans hab hbc }

/-- Zero instance for CauchySequence (constant zero sequence) -/
instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ : ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0 : ℚ))

/-- The real numbers as a quotient of Cauchy sequences -/
abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/-- Formal limit of a sequence (dummy value 0 if not Cauchy) -/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

/-- Equality of limits iff Equiv (from §5.3) -/
 theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  sorry

/-- Lemma 5.3.6 (Sum of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)-/
 theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  sorry

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
 theorem Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

/-- Definition 5.3.4 (Addition of reals) -/
 noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy) -/
 theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by simp_rw [mul_comm]; exact mul_equiv_left a ha hbb'

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
    equiv_trans (mul_equiv_right _ ha hbb') (mul_equiv_left _ hb' haa')

/-- Definition 5.3.9 (Product of reals) -/
 noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . exact Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy) (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

/-- ratCast into Real -/
 instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

/-- OfNat instance for Real via rationals -/
 instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

/-- Negation on Real using multiplication by -1 (as in §5.3) -/
 noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

/-- AddGroup structure on Real (axioms omitted as sorries, as in source) -/
 noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

/-- Definition 5.4.1: sequences bounded away from zero with sign -/
 abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

/-- Definition 5.4.1 -/
 abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

/-- Sign predicates on Real (def 5.4.1 via limits of sequences) -/
 abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

 abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Ordering on Real (def 5.4.6) -/
 instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

/-- Proposition 5.4.7(a) (order trichotomy) / Exercise 5.4.2 -/
 theorem Real.not_gt_and_lt (x y:Real) : ¬ (x > y ∧ x < y):= by sorry

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := {x} ∪ X

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair).  One could also have used `Object × Object` to
define `OrderedPair` here. -/
@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst: Object
  snd: Object

/-- Exercise 3.5.1, second part (requires axiom of regularity) -/
abbrev OrderedPair.toObject' : OrderedPair ↪ Object where
  toFun p := ({ p.fst, (({p.fst, p.snd}:Set):Object) }:Set)
  inj' := by sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms


-- We place Rat.Close outside the Chapter5 namespace, as in Section 4.3
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y : ℚ) := |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
  vanish := by
    sorry

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀ : ℤ) (a : { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by
    sorry

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.Steady (ε : ℚ) (a : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

abbrev Chapter5.Sequence.from (a : Chapter5.Sequence) (n₁ : ℤ) : Chapter5.Sequence :=
  Chapter5.Sequence.mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n : ℤ))

abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε : ℚ) (a : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a : Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0 : ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε : ℚ) (a b : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε : ℚ) (a b : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

abbrev Chapter5.Sequence.Equiv (a b : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0 : ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a : Chapter5.Sequence) (b : Chapter5.Sequence)

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a : ℕ → ℚ} (ha : (a : Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a : Sequence).seq
  vanish := by
    sorry
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n : ℤ)

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv :=
    { refl := by
        sorry
      symm := by
        sorry
      trans := by
        sorry }

@[simp]
theorem Sequence_IsCauchy_const (a : ℚ) : ((fun _ : ℕ ↦ a) : Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  -- This matches Sequence.IsCauchy.const from Section 5.3
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ : ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence_IsCauchy_const (0 : ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a : Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0 : CauchySequence))

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b))
      (by
        intro a b a' b' _ _
        -- well-definedness proof, omitted
        sorry)

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b))
      (by
        intro a b a' b' _ _
        -- well-definedness proof, omitted
        sorry)

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦ Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence_IsCauchy_const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n : ℕ} : OfNat Real n where ofNat := ((n : ℚ) : Real)

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where neg x := ((-1 : ℚ) : Real) * x

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

-- Section 5.4 ordering and positivity
abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ c : ℚ, c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ c : ℚ, c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x : Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a : ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a : Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x : Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a : ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a : Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

theorem Real.mul_le_mul_left {x y z : Real} (hxy : x ≤ y) (hz : z.IsPos) : z * x ≤ z * y := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic

/--
  This definition needs to be made outside of the Section 4.3 namespace for technical reasons.
-/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

/--
If `a : ℕ → ℚ` is used in a context where a `Sequence` is expected, automatically coerce `a` to `Sequence.ofNatFun a` (which will be pretty-printed as `↑a`)
-/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/--  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise. -/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.1.3 - definition of ε-steadiness for a sequence with an
arbitrary starting point n₀ -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/--Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
 theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by sorry

end Chapter5

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I : Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

open Classical in
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval :=
  instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) :
    Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

abbrev Constant {X Y : Type} (f : X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

abbrev ConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop :=
  Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y : Type} [hY : Nonempty Y] (f : X → Y) : Y :=
  if h : Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : ℝ :=
  constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev right_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ :=
  lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Ioi x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev left_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ :=
  lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Iio x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev α_length (α: ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
| Icc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| Ico a b => if a ≤ b then (left_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| Ioc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0
| Ioo a b => if a < b then (left_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0

notation3:max α"["I"]ₗ" => α_length α I

abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J : Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval}
    (P: Partition I) (α: ℝ → ℝ) : ℝ :=
  ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * α[J]ₗ

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ (f:ℝ → ℝ)
    (I: BoundedInterval) (α:ℝ → ℝ): ℝ :=
  if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then
    PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ f h.choose α
  else 0

open PiecewiseConstantOn

/-- Analogue of Theorem 11.2.16 (b) (Laws of integration) / Exercise 11.8.3 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ_smul {f: ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval} (c:ℝ)
  (hf: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) {α:ℝ → ℝ} (hα: Monotone α) :
  RS_integ (c • f) I α = c * RS_integ f I α
   := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

/-!
# Analysis I, Section 4.1: The integers

I have attempted to make the translation as faithful a paraphrasing as possible of the original
text. When there is a choice between a more idiomatic Lean solution and a more faithful
translation, I have generally chosen the latter. In particular, there will be places where the
Lean code could be "golfed" to be more elegant and idiomatic, but I have consciously avoided
doing so.

Main constructions and results of this section:

- Definition of the "Section 4.1" integers, `Section_4_1.Int`, as formal differences `a —— b` of
  natural numbers `a b:ℕ`, up to equivalence.  (This is a quotient of a scaffolding type
  `Section_4_1.PreInt`, which consists of formal differences without any equivalence imposed.)

- ring operations and order these integers, as well as an embedding of ℕ.

- Equivalence with the Mathlib integers `_root_.Int` (or `ℤ`), which we will use going forward.

## Tips from past users

Users of the companion who have completed the exercises in this section are welcome to send their tips for future users in this section as PRs.

- (Add tip here)

-/

namespace Section_4_1

structure PreInt where
  minuend : ℕ
  subtrahend : ℕ

/-- Definition 4.1.1 -/
instance PreInt.instSetoid : Setoid PreInt where
  r a b := a.minuend + b.subtrahend = b.minuend + a.subtrahend
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by
      -- This proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
      intro ⟨ a,b ⟩ ⟨ c,d ⟩ ⟨ e,f ⟩ h1 h2; simp_all
      have h3 := congrArg₂ (· + ·) h1 h2; simp at h3
      have : (a + f) + (c + d) = (e + b) + (c + d) := calc
        (a + f) + (c + d) = a + d + (c + f) := by abel
        _ = c + b + (e + d) := h3
        _ = (e + b) + (c + d) := by abel
      exact Nat.add_right_cancel this
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreInt.eq (a b c d:ℕ) : (⟨ a,b ⟩: PreInt) ≈ ⟨ c,d ⟩ ↔ a + d = c + b := by rfl

abbrev Int := Quotient PreInt.instSetoid

abbrev Int.formalDiff (a b:ℕ)  : Int := Quotient.mk PreInt.instSetoid ⟨ a,b ⟩

infix:100 " —— " => Int.formalDiff

/-- Definition 4.1.4 (Negation of integers) / Exercise 4.1.2 -/
instance Int.instNeg : Neg Int where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b ⟩ ↦ b —— a) (by sorry)

end Section_4_1

/--
  This definition needs to be made outside of the Section 4.3 namespace for technical reasons.

def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Section_4_3

abbrev dist (x y : ℚ) := |x - y|

theorem dist_eq (x y: ℚ) : dist x y = |x-y| := rfl

/-- Proposition 4.3.7(d) (addition preserves closeness) -/
theorem add_close {ε δ x y z w:ℚ} (hxy: ε.Close x y) (hzw: δ.Close z w) :
    (ε + δ).Close (x+z) (y+w) := by
  sorry

/-- Reflexivity of close at 0 -/
theorem close_refl (x:ℚ) : (0:ℚ).Close x x := by
  sorry

end Section_4_3

/-! Section 5.1: Sequences and Cauchy sequences -/
namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n:ℤ) (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by
  norm_cast

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  Sequence.mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

/-- Eventually steady for sequences -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Eventually ε-steady (exists a tail that is ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Cauchy sequences (eventually steady for every ε>0) -/
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

/-- Characterization for sequences from ℕ -/
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.coe (a:ℕ → ℚ) :
    (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ∃ N, ∀ j ≥ N, ∀ k ≥ N,
    Section_4_3.dist (a j) (a k) ≤ ε := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

/-! Section 5.2: Equivalent sequences -/

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.1 ($ε$-close sequences) -/
lemma Rat.closeSeq_def (ε: ℚ) (a b: Sequence) :
    ε.CloseSeq a b ↔ ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.2.3 (Eventually ε-close sequences) -/
lemma Rat.eventuallyClose_def (ε: ℚ) (a b: Sequence) :
    ε.EventuallyClose a b ↔ ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N) := by rfl

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
lemma Sequence.equiv_def (a b: ℕ → ℚ) :
    Equiv a b ↔ ∀ (ε:ℚ), ε > 0 → ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence) := by rfl

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
lemma Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) (hbc: Equiv b c) :
  Equiv a c := by sorry

end Chapter5

/-! Section 5.3: Construction of the reals (only pieces we need) -/
namespace Chapter5

/-- A class of Cauchy sequences that start at zero -/
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

/-- A sequence starting at zero that is Cauchy, can be viewed as a Cauchy sequence.-/
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {f:ℕ → ℚ} (hf: (f:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (f:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    have hneg : ¬ n ≥ 0 := by linarith
    simpa [Sequence.instCoeFun, Sequence.eval_coe_at_int, hneg]
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := hf

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (cs: CauchySequence) :
    ((cs:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = cs.toSequence := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 5.3.3 / Exercise 5.3.1 -/
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv := {
     refl := sorry
     symm := sorry
     trans := sorry
  }

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (f := fun (_: ℕ) ↦ 0) (by
    -- use the theorem from section 5.3 (we include it with sorry)
    have : ((fun (_:ℕ) ↦ (0:ℚ)):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
      -- will be provided by a lemma below; keep a sorry here
      sorry
    simpa using this)

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
/--
  It is convenient in Lean to assign the "dummy" value of 0 to `LIM a` when `a` is not Cauchy.
  This requires Classical logic, because the property of being Cauchy is not computable or
  decidable.
-/
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

/-- Definition 5.3.1 (Real numbers) -/
theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  . intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound
  rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb]

/--Lemma 5.3.6 (Sum of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy)-/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a + b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  -- This proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
  rw [Sequence.IsCauchy.coe] at *
  intro ε hε
  choose N1 ha using ha _ (half_pos hε)
  choose N2 hb using hb _ (half_pos hε)
  use max N1 N2
  intro j hj k hk
  have h1 := ha j ?_ k ?_ <;> try omega
  have h2 := hb j ?_ k ?_ <;> try omega
  simp [Section_4_3.dist] at *; rw [←Rat.Close] at *
  convert Section_4_3.add_close h1 h2
  linarith

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
theorem Sequence.add_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (haa': Equiv a a') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b) := by
  -- This proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
  rw [equiv_def] at *
  peel 2 haa' with ε hε haa'
  rw [Rat.eventuallyClose_def] at *
  choose N haa' using haa'; use N
  simp [Rat.closeSeq_def] at *
  peel 5 haa' with n hn hN _ _ haa'
  simp [hn, hN] at *
  convert Section_4_3.add_close haa' (Section_4_3.close_refl (b n.toNat))
  simp

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
theorem Sequence.add_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ) (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a + b') := by simp_rw [add_comm]; exact add_equiv_left _ hbb'

/--Lemma 5.3.7 (Sum of equivalent sequences is equivalent)-/
theorem Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ} (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') :
    Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') :=
  equiv_trans (add_equiv_left _ haa') (add_equiv_right _ hbb')

/-- Definition 5.3.4 (Addition of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) + (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) + (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      . solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

/-- (Not from textbook) product of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy (placeholder) -/
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

/-- (Not from textbook) product of equivalent sequences is equivalent (placeholder) -/
theorem Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Equiv b b') : Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') := by
  sorry

/-- Definition 5.3.9 (Product of reals) -/
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      change LIM ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ))
      rw [LIM_eq_LIM]
      · exact
          Sequence.mul_equiv (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact a.cauchy)
            (by rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence]; exact b'.cauchy) haa' hbb'
      all_goals
        apply Sequence.IsCauchy.mul <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_
      )

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (f := fun _ ↦ q) (by
      -- constant sequences are Cauchy
      have : ((fun (_:ℕ) ↦ q):Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry
      simpa using this))

instance Real.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n:ℚ):Real)

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1:ℚ):Real) * x

/-- Proposition 5.3.11 (laws of algebra) -/
noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

/-- Notation: subtraction equals addition with negation -/
theorem Real.sub_eq_add_neg (x y:Real) : x - y = x + (-y) := rfl

end Chapter5

/-! Section 5.4: Ordering on the reals (required pieces) -/
namespace Chapter5

abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

/-- Definition 5.4.6 (Ordering of the reals) -/
instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

/-- Proposition 5.4.7(b) (order is anti-symmetric) / Exercise 5.4.2 -/
 theorem Real.antisymm (x y:Real) : x < y ↔ y > x := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-!
Minimal dependencies extracted to typecheck the target theorem from Section 5.3
without importing any `Analysis.Section_*` modules.
-/

/-- Definition from Section 4.3: ε-closeness on rationals -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y : ℚ) := |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

-- Notice how the delaborator prints this as `↑fun x ↦ ↑x ^ 2 : Sequence`.
#check Sequence.ofNatFun (· ^ 2)

/--
If `a : ℕ → ℚ` is used in a context where a `Sequence` is expected, automatically coerce `a` to `Sequence.ofNatFun a` (which will be pretty-printed as `↑a`)
-/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

/--
A slight generalization of Definition 5.1.3 - definition of ε-steadiness for a sequence with an
arbitrary starting point n₀
-/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

end Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.1 ($ε$-close sequences) -/
abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

/-- Definition 5.2.3 (Eventually ε-close sequences) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- Proposition 5.3.10 (Product of equivalent sequences is equivalent) / Exercise 5.3.2 -/
theorem Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Equiv a a') :
  Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 3 minimal context to typecheck the target theorem

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms. -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Coercion of sets to objects. -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Coerce a set to a type (its elements as a subtype of `Object`). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

/-- Singleton notation for sets. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := SetTheory.singleton

/-- Abbreviation for specification on sets. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Use the set of natural numbers from the set theory axioms as a set. -/
abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity): bijection with Lean naturals. -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

/-- Provide numerals for `Nat` (elements of the set of naturals). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

/-- Cast ℕ to `Nat`. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv n

/-- Cast `Nat` to ℕ. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := (SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv).symm n

/-- Cast ℕ to `Object`. -/
instance SetTheory.Object.instNatCast : NatCast Object where
  natCast n := ((n:Nat):Object)

/-- Some simp lemma used later in other parts. -/
@[simp]
lemma SetTheory.Object.ofnat_eq {n:ℕ} : ((n:Nat):Object) = (n:Object) := rfl

/-- Another simp lemma from the source. -/
@[simp]
lemma SetTheory.Object.natCast_inj (n m:ℕ) :
    (n : Object) = (m : Object) ↔ n = m := by
  -- This proof in the original file relies on more API; we can leave it as sorry.
  -- It is not needed for the target theorem to typecheck.
  sorry

/-- Equal cardinality of sets (Definition 3.6.1). -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

/-- Reflexivity of equal cardinality. -/
@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

/-- Symmetry of equal cardinality. -/
@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set}
    (h: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) : SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

/-- Transitivity of equal cardinality. -/
@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set}
    (h1: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Y) (h2: SetTheory.Set.EqualCard Y Z) :
    SetTheory.Set.EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Make equal cardinality into a setoid to support the `≈` notation. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set where
  r := SetTheory.Set.EqualCard
  iseqv := ⟨ SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl, SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm, SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans ⟩

/-- The finite set `{0,1,...,n-1}` inside set theory. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n : ℕ) : Set :=
  (Nat).specify (fun m ↦ ((m : ℕ) < n))

/-- Cardinality of a set as equivalence to `Fin n`. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ SetTheory.Set.Fin n

/-- Example 3.6.7(a) from the text. -/
-- Target theorem to be proved (left as sorry as per instructions).
 theorem SetTheory.Set.Example_3_6_7a (a:Object) : ({a}:Set).has_card 1 := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

open Lean Elab Tactic in
elab "grind" : tactic => do
  evalTactic (← `(tactic| first | intros | skip))
  evalTactic (← `(tactic| simp [*] at *))
  pure ()

def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y : ℚ) := |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
  vanish := by
    grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀ : ℤ) (a : { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by
    grind

abbrev Sequence.from (a : Sequence) (n₁ : ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n : ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a : Sequence) {n₁ n : ℤ} (hn : n ≥ n₁) :
    (a.from n₁) n = a n := by
  unfold Sequence.from
  by_cases hmax : n ≥ max a.n₀ n₁
  · simp [Sequence.mk', hmax]
  · have hlt : n < max a.n₀ n₁ := lt_of_not_ge hmax
    have hlt' : n < a.n₀ :=
      (lt_max_iff.mp hlt).resolve_right (not_lt.mpr hn)
    have hv : a.seq n = 0 := a.vanish n hlt'
    simp [Sequence.mk', hmax, hv]

end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.Steady (ε : ℚ) (a : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε : ℚ) (a : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε : ℚ) (a b : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ∀ n, n ≥ (a.from N).n₀ → n ≥ (b.from N).n₀ → ε.Close (a.from N n) (b.from N n)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a : Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0 : ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0 : ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a : Sequence) (b : Sequence)

@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n : ℤ)

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a : ℕ → ℚ} (ha : (a : Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a : Sequence).seq
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    change (if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0) = 0
    have : ¬ n ≥ 0 := by exact not_le.mpr hn
    simp [this]
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a : CauchySequence) :
    ((a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv :=
    { refl := by sorry
      symm := by sorry
      trans := by sorry }

theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a : ℚ) : ((fun _ : ℕ ↦ a) : Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ : ℕ ↦ (0 : ℚ)) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const 0)

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a : Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0 : CauchySequence))

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      sorry)

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' _ _
      sorry)

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦
    Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q))

instance Real.instOfNat {n : ℕ} : OfNat Real n where
  ofNat := ((n : ℚ) : Real)

noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1 : ℚ) : Real) * x

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ c : ℚ, c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ c : ℚ, c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x : Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a : ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a : Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x : Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a : ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a : Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where
  lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

instance Real.instLE : LE Real where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

theorem Real.trichotomous' (x y : Real) : x > y ∨ x < y ∨ x = y := by sorry

end Chapter5

/-!
# Analysis I, Section 4.1: The integers

A dependency-minimal slice to typecheck the target theorem.
-/

namespace Section_4_1

structure PreInt where
  minuend : ℕ
  subtrahend : ℕ

/-- Definition 4.1.1 -/
instance PreInt.instSetoid : Setoid PreInt where
  r a b := a.minuend + b.subtrahend = b.minuend + a.subtrahend
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by
      -- This proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
      intro ⟨ a,b ⟩ ⟨ c,d ⟩ ⟨ e,f ⟩ h1 h2; simp_all
      have h3 := congrArg₂ (· + ·) h1 h2; simp at h3
      have : (a + f) + (c + d) = (e + b) + (c + d) := calc
        (a + f) + (c + d) = a + d + (c + f) := by abel
        _ = c + b + (e + d) := h3
        _ = (e + b) + (c + d) := by abel
      exact Nat.add_right_cancel this
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreInt.eq (a b c d:ℕ) : (⟨ a,b ⟩: PreInt) ≈ ⟨ c,d ⟩ ↔ a + d = c + b := by rfl

abbrev Int := Quotient PreInt.instSetoid

abbrev Int.formalDiff (a b:ℕ)  : Int := Quotient.mk PreInt.instSetoid ⟨ a,b ⟩

infix:100 " —— " => Int.formalDiff

/-- Definition 4.1.1 (Integers) -/
theorem Int.eq (a b c d:ℕ): a —— b = c —— d ↔ a + d = c + b :=
  ⟨ Quotient.exact, by intro h; exact Quotient.sound h ⟩

/-- Lemma 4.1.3 (Addition well-defined) -/
instance Int.instAdd : Add Int where
  add := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b ⟩ ⟨ c, d ⟩ ↦ (a+c) —— (b+d) ) (by
    intro ⟨ a, b ⟩ ⟨ c, d ⟩ ⟨ a', b' ⟩ ⟨ c', d' ⟩ h1 h2
    simp [eq] at *
    omega)

instance Int.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Int n where
  ofNat := n —— 0

/-- Definition 4.1.4 (Negation of integers) / Exercise 4.1.2 -/
instance Int.instNeg : Neg Int where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b ⟩ ↦ b —— a) (by sorry)

/-- Proposition 4.1.6 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.1.4 -/
instance Int.instAddGroup : AddGroup Int :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

end Section_4_1

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.5.1 (Ordered pair).  One could also have used `Object × Object` to
define `OrderedPair` here. -/
@[ext]
structure OrderedPair where
  fst: Object
  snd: Object

/--
  Exercise 3.5.3. The spirit here is to avoid direct rewrites (which make all of these claims
  trivial), and instead use `OrderedPair.eq` or `SetTheory.Set.tuple_inj`
-/
theorem OrderedPair.refl (p: OrderedPair) : p = p := by sorry

end Chapter3

/-!

PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ_sub.

namespace Chapter11

open Classical

/-- Bounded intervals (from Section 11.1). -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- Coercion to a set of real numbers. -/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instEmpty : EmptyCollection BoundedInterval where
  emptyCollection := Ioo 0 0

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.coe_empty : ((∅ : BoundedInterval):Set ℝ) = ∅ := by
  simp [toSet]

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioo (a b:ℝ) : (Ioo a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioo a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Icc (a b:ℝ) : (Icc a b : Set ℝ) = .Icc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioc (a b:ℝ) : (Ioc a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ico (a b:ℝ) : (Ico a b : Set ℝ) = .Ico a b := by rfl

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.mem_iff (I: BoundedInterval) (x:ℝ) :
  x ∈ I ↔ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ) := by rfl

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.subset_iff (I J: BoundedInterval) :
  I ⊆ J ↔ (I:Set ℝ) ⊆ (J:Set ℝ) := by rfl

/-- Constant and ConstantOn (from Section 11.8). -/
abbrev Constant {X Y : Type} (f : X → Y) : Prop :=
  ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

abbrev ConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop :=
  Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- `constant_value` and `constant_value_on` (from Section 11.8). -/
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y : Type} [hY : Nonempty Y] (f : X → Y) : Y :=
  if h : Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : ℝ :=
  constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Right and left limits (from Section 11.8). -/
noncomputable abbrev right_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ :=
  lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Ioi x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev left_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ :=
  lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Iio x₀)).map f)

/-- α-length (from Section 11.8). -/
noncomputable abbrev α_length (α: ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
| Icc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| Ico a b => if a ≤ b then (left_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| Ioc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0
| Ioo a b => if a < b then (left_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0

notation3:max α"["I"]ₗ" => α_length α I

/-- Partitions (from Section 11.1). -/
@[ext]
structure Partition (I : BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x : ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I : BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Piecewise constant (with a given partition) and on an interval (from Section 11.8). -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J : Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Piecewise-constant RS integral over a partition (from Section 11.8). -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) (α: ℝ → ℝ)   :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * α[J]ₗ

/-- Piecewise-constant RS integral on an interval (from Section 11.8). -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) (α:ℝ → ℝ):
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ f h.choose α else 0

/-- Theorem 11.8.8 (c) (Laws of RS integration) / Exercise 11.8.8 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ_sub {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval}
  {α:ℝ → ℝ} (hα: Monotone α)
  (hf: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) (hg: PiecewiseConstantOn g I) :
  RS_integ (f - g) I α = RS_integ f I α - RS_integ g I α := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals, as in Section 11.1 -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I : Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Constant functions, used to define piecewise constant -/
abbrev Constant {X Y : Type} (f : X → Y) : Prop :=
  ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

/-- Constant on a set X -/
abbrev ConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop :=
  Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- A choice of constant value (junk value if not constant). -/
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y : Type} [hY : Nonempty Y] (f : X → Y) : Y := by
  classical
  exact if h : Constant f then h.choose else Classical.choice hY

/-- A choice of constant value on a set (junk value if not constant). -/
noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : ℝ :=
  constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Right and left limits used to define α-length. -/
noncomputable abbrev right_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ := lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Ioi x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev left_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ := lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Iio x₀)).map f)

/-- Definition 11.8.1: α-length of a bounded interval. -/
noncomputable abbrev α_length (α: ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
| Icc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| Ico a b => if a ≤ b then (left_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| Ioc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0
| Ioo a b => if a < b then (left_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0

notation3:max α"["I"]ₗ" => α_length α I

/-- Piecewise-constant on a given partition. -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J : Set ℝ)

/-- Piecewise-constant on an interval (exists some partition). -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Piecewise constant Riemann–Stieltjes integral along a partition. -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) (α: ℝ → ℝ)   :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * α[J]ₗ

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) (α:ℝ → ℝ):
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ f h.choose α else 0

/-- Theorem 11.8.8 (e) (Laws of RS integration) / Exercise 11.8.8 -/
 theorem PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ_mono {f g: ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval}
  {α:ℝ → ℝ} (hα: Monotone α)
  (h: ∀ x ∈ I, f x ≤ g x) (hf: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) (hg: PiecewiseConstantOn g I) :
  RS_integ f I α ≤ RS_integ g I α := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

/-!
# Analysis I, Section 4.1: The integers (dependency-minimal slice)

This file extracts the minimal context needed to typecheck the target theorem
`Section_4_1.Int.instAddCommGroup`.
-/

namespace Section_4_1

structure PreInt where
  minuend : ℕ
  subtrahend : ℕ

/-- Definition 4.1.1 -/
instance PreInt.instSetoid : Setoid PreInt where
  r a b := a.minuend + b.subtrahend = b.minuend + a.subtrahend
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by
      -- This proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
      intro ⟨ a,b ⟩ ⟨ c,d ⟩ ⟨ e,f ⟩ h1 h2; simp_all
      have h3 := congrArg₂ (· + ·) h1 h2; simp at h3
      have : (a + f) + (c + d) = (e + b) + (c + d) := calc
        (a + f) + (c + d) = a + d + (c + f) := by abel
        _ = c + b + (e + d) := h3
        _ = (e + b) + (c + d) := by abel
      exact Nat.add_right_cancel this
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreInt.eq (a b c d:ℕ) : (⟨ a,b ⟩: PreInt) ≈ ⟨ c,d ⟩ ↔ a + d = c + b := by rfl

abbrev Int := Quotient PreInt.instSetoid

abbrev Int.formalDiff (a b:ℕ)  : Int := Quotient.mk PreInt.instSetoid ⟨ a,b ⟩

infix:100 " —— " => Int.formalDiff

/-- Definition 4.1.1 (Integers) -/
theorem Int.eq (a b c d:ℕ): a —— b = c —— d ↔ a + d = c + b :=
  ⟨ Quotient.exact, by intro h; exact Quotient.sound h ⟩

/-- Lemma 4.1.3 (Addition well-defined) -/
instance Int.instAdd : Add Int where
  add := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b ⟩ ⟨ c, d ⟩ ↦ (a+c) —— (b+d) ) (by
    intro ⟨ a, b ⟩ ⟨ c, d ⟩ ⟨ a', b' ⟩ ⟨ c', d' ⟩ h1 h2
    simp [eq] at *
    omega)

/-- Definition 4.1.2 (Definition of addition) -/
@[simp]
theorem Int.add_eq (a b c d:ℕ) : a —— b + c —— d = (a+c)——(b+d) := Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _

instance Int.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Int n where
  ofNat := n —— 0

instance Int.instZero : Zero Int where
  zero := 0

/-- Definition 4.1.4 (Negation of integers) / Exercise 4.1.2 -/
instance Int.instNeg : Neg Int where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b ⟩ ↦ b —— a) (by sorry)

/-- Proposition 4.1.6 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.1.4 -/
instance Int.instAddGroup : AddGroup Int :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

/-- Proposition 4.1.6 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.1.4 -/
instance Int.instAddCommGroup : AddCommGroup Int where
  add_comm := by sorry

end Section_4_1
