namespace Section_4_2

structure PreRat where
  numerator : ℤ
  denominator : ℤ
  nonzero : denominator ≠ 0

/-- Exercise 4.2.1 -/
instance PreRat.instSetoid : Setoid PreRat where
  r a b := a.numerator * b.denominator = b.numerator * a.denominator
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by sorry
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreRat.eq (a b c d:ℤ) (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (⟨ a,b,hb ⟩: PreRat) ≈ ⟨ c,d,hd ⟩ ↔ a * d = c * b := by rfl

abbrev Rat := Quotient PreRat.instSetoid

/-- We give division a "junk" value of 0//1 if the denominator is zero -/
abbrev Rat.formalDiv (a b:ℤ) : Rat :=
  Quotient.mk PreRat.instSetoid (if h:b ≠ 0 then ⟨ a,b,h ⟩ else ⟨ 0, 1, by decide ⟩)

infix:100 " // " => Rat.formalDiv

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Multiplication well-defined) -/
instance Rat.mul_inst : Mul Rat where
  mul := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*c) // (b*d)) (by sorry)

/-- Embedding the integers in the rationals -/
instance Rat.instIntCast : IntCast Rat where
  intCast a := a // 1

instance Rat.instNatCast : NatCast Rat where
  natCast n := (n:ℤ) // 1

instance Rat.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Rat n where
  ofNat := (n:ℤ) // 1

/-- Proposition 4.2.4 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.2.3 -/
instance Rat.instCommMonoid : CommMonoid Rat where
  mul_comm := by sorry
  mul_assoc := by sorry
  one_mul := by sorry
  mul_one := by sorry

end Section_4_2

namespace Chapter7

open BigOperators

/--
  Definition 7.2.1 (Formal infinite series). This is similar to Chapter 6 sequence, but is
  manipulated differently. As with Chapter 5, we will start series from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

abbrev Series.mk' {m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Series where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/-- Definition 7.2.2 (Convergence of series) -/
abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop := ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev Series.sum (s : Series) : ℝ := if h : s.converges then h.choose else 0

/-- Definition 7.2.8 (Absolute convergence) -/
abbrev Series.abs (s:Series) : Series := mk' (m:=s.m) (fun n ↦ |s.seq n|)

abbrev Series.absConverges (s:Series) : Prop := s.abs.converges

/-- Corollary 7.3.2 (Comparison test) / Exercise 7.3.1 -/
theorem Series.converges_of_le {s t: Series} (hm: s.m = t.m) (hcomp: ∀ n ≥ s.m, |s.seq n| ≤ t.seq n) (hconv : t.converges) : s.absConverges ∧ |s.sum| ≤ s.abs.sum ∧ s.abs.sum ≤ t.sum := by sorry

end Chapter7

/--
  Assumption 2.6 (Existence of natural numbers).  Here we use an explicit construction of the
  natural numbers (using an inductive type).  For a more axiomatic approach, see the epilogue to
  this chapter.
-/
inductive Nat where
| zero : Nat
| succ : Nat → Nat
deriving Repr, DecidableEq  -- this allows `decide` to work on `Nat`

/-- Axiom 2.1 (0 is a natural number) -/
instance Nat.instZero : Zero Nat := ⟨ zero ⟩

/-- Axiom 2.2 (Successor of a natural number is a natural number) -/
postfix:100 "++" => Nat.succ

/--
  Recursion. Analogous to the inbuilt Mathlib method `Nat.rec` associated to
  the Mathlib natural numbers
-/
abbrev Nat.recurse (f: Nat → Nat → Nat) (c: Nat) : Nat → Nat := fun n ↦ match n with
| 0 => c
| n++ => f n (recurse f c n)

/-- Definition 2.2.1. (Addition of natural numbers).
    Compare with Mathlib's `Nat.add` -/
abbrev Nat.add (n m : Nat) : Nat := Nat.recurse (fun _ sum ↦ sum++) m n

/-- This instance allows for the `+` notation to be used for natural number addition. -/

/-!
We extract the minimal context to typecheck a theorem from Chapter 3.3.
This file intentionally avoids importing any `Analysis.Section_*` modules
and instead inlines the required parts.

/-! Tools/ExistsUnique (from Analysis.Tools.ExistsUnique) - copy verbatim -/ 
#check existsUnique_of_exists_of_unique
#check ExistsUnique.exists
#check ExistsUnique.unique
#check ExistsUnique.intro

/-- This implements the axiom of unique choice. -/
noncomputable def ExistsUnique.choose {α: Sort*} {p: α → Prop} (h : ∃! x, p x) : α := h.exists.choose

 theorem ExistsUnique.choose_spec {α: Sort*} {p: α → Prop} (h : ∃! x, p x) :
  p h.choose := h.exists.choose_spec

 theorem ExistsUnique.choose_eq {α: Sort*} {p: α → Prop} (h : ∃! x, p x) {x : α} (hx : p x) :
  h.choose = x := h.unique h.choose_spec hx

 theorem ExistsUnique.choose_iff {α: Sort*} {p: α → Prop} (h : ∃! x, p x) (x : α):
  p x ↔ x = h.choose :=
  ⟨ by intro hx; exact (h.choose_eq hx).symm, by rintro rfl; exact h.choose_spec ⟩

 theorem ExistsUnique.choose_eq_choose {α: Sort*} {p: α → Prop} (h : ∃! x, p x) : Exists.choose h = h.choose := by
  rw [←choose_iff]; exact (Exists.choose_spec h).1

/-- An alternate form of the axiom of unique choice.   -/
noncomputable def Subsingleton.choose {α: Sort*} [Subsingleton α] [hn: Nonempty α] : α := hn.some

 theorem Subsingleton.choose_spec {α: Sort*} [hs: Subsingleton α] [Nonempty α] (x:α) : x = hs.choose := Subsingleton.elim _ _

/-- The equivalence between `ExistsUnique` and `[Subsingleton] [Nonempty]` does not require choice. -/
 theorem ExistsUnique.iff_subsingleton_nonempty  {α: Sort*} {p: α → Prop} :
  (∃! x, p x) ↔ (Subsingleton {x // p x} ∧ Nonempty {x // p x}) := by
  constructor
  · intro h; obtain ⟨ x₀, hx₀ ⟩ := h.exists
    refine ⟨ ⟨ ?_ ⟩, ⟨ _, hx₀ ⟩⟩
    intro ⟨ x, hx ⟩ ⟨ y, hy ⟩
    exact (Subtype.mk.injEq _ _ _ _).symm ▸ (h.unique hx hy)
  intro ⟨ hsing, ⟨ x₀, hx₀ ⟩ ⟩
  apply ExistsUnique.intro _ hx₀; intro y hy
  exact Subtype.mk.injEq _ _ _ _ ▸ (hsing.elim ⟨ _, hy ⟩ ⟨ _, hx₀ ⟩)

#print axioms ExistsUnique.iff_subsingleton_nonempty 


/-! Section 3.1 essentials - minimal slice -/ 
namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

/--
  Definition 3.3.1. `Function X Y` is the structure of functions from `X` to `Y`.
  Analogous to the Mathlib type `X → Y`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Function (X Y: Set) where
  P : X → Y → Prop
  unique : ∀ x: X, ∃! y: Y, P x y

#check Function.mk

/--
  Converting a Chapter 3 function `f: Function X Y` to a Mathlib function `f: X → Y`.
  The Chapter 3 definition of a function was nonconstructive, so we have to use the
  axiom of choice here.
-/
noncomputable def Function.to_fn {X Y: Set} (f: Function X Y) : X → Y :=
  fun x ↦ (f.unique x).choose

noncomputable instance Function.inst_coefn (X Y: Set) : CoeFun (Function X Y) (fun _ ↦ X → Y) where
  coe := Function.to_fn

 theorem Function.to_fn_eval {X Y: Set} (f: Function X Y) (x:X) : f.to_fn x = f x := rfl

/-- Converting a Mathlib function to a Chapter 3 `Function` -/
abbrev Function.mk_fn {X Y: Set} (f: X → Y) : Function X Y :=
  Function.mk (fun x y ↦ y = f x) (by simp)

/-- Definition 3.3.1 -/
 theorem Function.eval {X Y: Set} (f: Function X Y) (x: X) (y: Y) : y = f x ↔ f.P x y := by
  convert ((f.unique x).choose_iff y).symm

@[simp]
 theorem Function.eval_of {X Y: Set} (f: X → Y) (x:X) : (Function.mk_fn f) x = f x := by
  symm; rw [eval]

/-- Definition 3.3.13 (Composition) -/
noncomputable abbrev Function.comp {X Y Z: Set} (g: Function Y Z) (f: Function X Y) :
    Function X Z :=
  Function.mk_fn (fun x ↦ g (f x))

-- `∘` is already taken in Mathlib for the composition of Mathlib functions,
-- so we use `○` here instead to avoid ambiguity.
infix:90 "○" => Function.comp

/-- Composition evaluation lemma (not strictly needed for the target theorem) -/
 theorem Function.comp_eval {X Y Z: Set} (g: Function Y Z) (f: Function X Y) (x: X) :
    (g ○ f) x = g (f x) := Function.eval_of _ _

abbrev Function.one_to_one {X Y: Set} (f: Function X Y) : Prop := ∀ x x': X, x ≠ x' → f x ≠ f x'

/-- Exercise 3.3.2 -/
 theorem Function.comp_of_inj {X Y Z:Set} {f: Function X Y} {g : Function Y Z} (hf: f.one_to_one)
  (hg: g.one_to_one) : (g ○ f).one_to_one := by sorry

end Chapter3

@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as series. -/
instance Series.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Series where
  coe := fun a ↦ {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind
  }

@[simp]
theorem Series.eval_coe (a: ℕ → ℝ) (n: ℕ) : (a: Series).seq n = a n := by simp

/-- Definition 7.2.2 (Convergence of series) -/
abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

/-- Lemma 7.3.3 (Geometric series) / Exercise 7.3.2 -/
theorem Series.converges_geom {x: ℝ} (hx: |x| < 1) : (fun n ↦ x ^ n : Series).convergesTo (1 / (1 - x)) := by sorry

end Chapter7

/-!
# Analysis I, Section 4.2

This file is a translation of Section 4.2 of Analysis I to Lean 4.
All numbering refers to the original text.

I have attempted to make the translation as faithful a paraphrasing as possible of the original
text. When there is a choice between a more idiomatic Lean solution and a more faithful
translation, I have generally chosen the latter. In particular, there will be places where the
Lean code could be "golfed" to be more elegant and idiomatic, but I have consciously avoided
doing so.

Main constructions and results of this section:

- Definition of the "Section 4.2" rationals, `Section_4_2.Rat`, as formal quotients `a // b` of
  integers `a b:ℤ`, up to equivalence.  (This is a quotient of a scaffolding type
  `Section_4_2.PreRat`, which consists of formal quotients without any equivalence imposed.)

- Field operations and order on these rationals, as well as an embedding of ℕ and ℤ.

- Equivalence with the Mathlib rationals `_root_.Rat` (or `ℚ`), which we will use going forward.

Note: here (and in the sequel) we use Mathlib's natural numbers `ℕ` and integers `ℤ` rather than
the Chapter 2 natural numbers and Section 4.1 integers.

## Tips from past users

Users of the companion who have completed the exercises in this section are welcome to send their tips for future users in this section as PRs.

- (Add tip here)

-/

namespace Section_4_2

structure PreRat where
  numerator : ℤ
  denominator : ℤ
  nonzero : denominator ≠ 0

/-- Exercise 4.2.1 -/
instance PreRat.instSetoid : Setoid PreRat where
  r a b := a.numerator * b.denominator = b.numerator * a.denominator
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by sorry
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreRat.eq (a b c d:ℤ) (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (⟨ a,b,hb ⟩: PreRat) ≈ ⟨ c,d,hd ⟩ ↔ a * d = c * b := by rfl

abbrev Rat := Quotient PreRat.instSetoid

/-- We give division a "junk" value of 0//1 if the denominator is zero -/
abbrev Rat.formalDiv (a b:ℤ) : Rat :=
  Quotient.mk PreRat.instSetoid (if h:b ≠ 0 then ⟨ a,b,h ⟩ else ⟨ 0, 1, by decide ⟩)

infix:100 " // " => Rat.formalDiv

/-- Definition 4.2.1 (Rationals) -/
theorem Rat.eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0): a // b = c // d ↔ a * d = c * b := by
  simp [formalDiv, hb, hd, Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]

/-- Definition 4.2.1 (Rationals) -/
theorem Rat.eq_diff (n:Rat) : ∃ a b, b ≠ 0 ∧ n = a // b := by
  apply Quotient.ind _ n; intro ⟨ a, b, h ⟩
  refine ⟨ a, b, h, ?_ ⟩
  simp [formalDiv, h]

/--
  Decidability of equality. Hint: modify the proof of `DecidableEq Int` from the previous
  section. However, because formal division handles the case of zero denominator separately, it
  may be more convenient to avoid that operation and work directly with the `Quotient` API.
-/
instance Rat.decidableEq : DecidableEq Rat := by
  sorry

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Addition well-defined) -/
instance Rat.add_inst : Add Rat where
  add := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*d+b*c) // (b*d)) (by
    intro ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ⟨ a', b', h1' ⟩ ⟨ c', d', h2' ⟩ h3 h4
    simp_all [Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]
    linear_combination d * d' * h3 + b * b' * h4
  )

/-- Definition 4.2.2 (Addition of rationals) -/
theorem Rat.add_eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (a // b) + (c // d) = (a*d + b*c) // (b*d) := by
  convert Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _ <;> simp [hb, hd]

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Multiplication well-defined) -/
instance Rat.mul_inst : Mul Rat where
  mul := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*c) // (b*d)) (by sorry)

/-- Definition 4.2.2 (Multiplication of rationals) -/
theorem Rat.mul_eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (a // b) * (c // d) = (a*c) // (b*d) := by
  convert Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _ <;> simp [hb, hd]

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Negation well-defined) -/
instance Rat.neg_inst : Neg Rat where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ↦ (-a) // b) (by sorry)

/-- Definition 4.2.2 (Negation of rationals) -/
theorem Rat.neg_eq (a:ℤ) {b:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) : - (a // b) = (-a) // b := by
  convert Quotient.lift_mk _ _ _ <;> simp [hb]

/-- Embedding the integers in the rationals -/
instance Rat.instIntCast : IntCast Rat where
  intCast a := a // 1

instance Rat.instNatCast : NatCast Rat where
  natCast n := (n:ℤ) // 1

instance Rat.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Rat n where
  ofNat := (n:ℤ) // 1

theorem Rat.coe_Int_eq (a:ℤ) : (a:Rat) = a // 1 := rfl

theorem Rat.coe_Nat_eq (n:ℕ) : (n:Rat) = n // 1 := rfl

theorem Rat.of_Nat_eq (n:ℕ) : (ofNat(n):Rat) = (ofNat(n):Nat) // 1 := rfl

/-- natCast distributes over successor -/
theorem Rat.natCast_succ (n: ℕ) : ((n + 1: ℕ): Rat) = (n: Rat) + 1 := by sorry

/-- intCast distributes over addition -/
lemma Rat.intCast_add (a b:ℤ) : (a:Rat) + (b:Rat) = (a+b:ℤ) := by sorry

/-- intCast distributes over multiplication -/
lemma Rat.intCast_mul (a b:ℤ) : (a:Rat) * (b:Rat) = (a*b:ℤ) := by sorry

/-- intCast commutes with negation -/
lemma Rat.intCast_neg (a:ℤ) : - (a:Rat) = (-a:ℤ) := rfl

theorem Rat.coe_Int_inj : Function.Injective (fun n:ℤ ↦ (n:Rat)) := by sorry

/-- Proposition 4.2.4 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.2.3 -/
instance Rat.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Rat :=
AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by
  -- this proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
  intro x y z
  obtain ⟨ a, b, hb, rfl ⟩ := eq_diff x
  obtain ⟨ c, d, hd, rfl ⟩ := eq_diff y
  obtain ⟨ e, f, hf, rfl ⟩ := eq_diff z
  have hbd : b*d ≠ 0 := Int.mul_ne_zero hb hd     -- can also use `observe hbd : b*d ≠ 0` here
  have hdf : d*f ≠ 0 := Int.mul_ne_zero hd hf     -- can also use `observe hdf : d*f ≠ 0` here
  have hbdf : b*d*f ≠ 0 := Int.mul_ne_zero hbd hf -- can also use `observe hbdf : b*d*f ≠ 0` here
  rw [add_eq _ _ hb hd, add_eq _ _ hbd hf, add_eq _ _ hd hf,
      add_eq _ _ hb hdf, ←mul_assoc b, eq _ _ hbdf hbdf]
  ring
)
 (by sorry) (by sorry)

/-- Proposition 4.2.4 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.2.3 -/
instance Rat.instAddCommGroup : AddCommGroup Rat where
  add_comm := by sorry

/-- Proposition 4.2.4 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.2.3 -/
instance Rat.instCommMonoid : CommMonoid Rat where
  mul_comm := by sorry
  mul_assoc := by sorry
  one_mul := by sorry
  mul_one := by sorry

/-- Proposition 4.2.4 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.2.3 -/
instance Rat.instCommRing : CommRing Rat where
  left_distrib := by sorry
  right_distrib := by sorry
  zero_mul := by sorry
  mul_zero := by sorry
  mul_assoc := by sorry
  -- Usually CommRing will generate a natCast instance and a proof for this.
  -- However, we are using a custom natCast for which `natCast_succ` cannot
  -- be proven automatically by `rfl`. Luckily we have proven it already.
  natCast_succ := natCast_succ

end Section_4_2

theorem Rat.coe_Rat_eq (a:ℤ) {b:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) : (a/b:ℚ) = a // b := by
  set q := (a/b:ℚ)
  set num :ℤ := q.num
  set den :ℤ := (q.den:ℤ)
  have hden : den ≠ 0 := by simp [den, q.den_nz]
  change num // den = a // b
  rw [eq _ _ hden hb]
  qify
  have hq : num / den = q := Rat.num_div_den q
  rwa [div_eq_div_iff] at hq <;> simp [hden, hb]

    intro q
    set num := q.num
    set den := q.den
    have hden : (den:ℤ) ≠ 0 := by simp [den, q.den_nz]
    rw [← Rat.num_div_den q]
    convert coe_Rat_eq _ hden
    rw [coe_Int_eq, coe_Nat_eq, div_eq, inv_eq, mul_eq, eq] <;> simp [num, den, q.den_nz]
  qsmul := _
  nnqsmul := _

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 6, Section 6.4 target theorem
-*/

abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

namespace Chapter6

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

end Chapter6

abbrev Real.Adherent (ε:ℝ) (a:Chapter6.Sequence) (x:ℝ) := ∃ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) x

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Exercise 3.2.3 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.no_univ : ¬ ∃ (U:Set), ∀ (x:Object), x ∈ U := by sorry

end Chapter3

namespace Section_4_2

structure PreRat where
  numerator : ℤ
  denominator : ℤ
  nonzero : denominator ≠ 0

/-- Exercise 4.2.1 -/
instance PreRat.instSetoid : Setoid PreRat where
  r a b := a.numerator * b.denominator = b.numerator * a.denominator
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by sorry
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreRat.eq (a b c d:ℤ) (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (⟨ a,b,hb ⟩: PreRat) ≈ ⟨ c,d,hd ⟩ ↔ a * d = c * b := by rfl

abbrev Rat := Quotient PreRat.instSetoid

/-- We give division a "junk" value of 0//1 if the denominator is zero -/
abbrev Rat.formalDiv (a b:ℤ) : Rat :=
  Quotient.mk PreRat.instSetoid (if h:b ≠ 0 then ⟨ a,b,h ⟩ else ⟨ 0, 1, by decide ⟩)

infix:100 " // " => Rat.formalDiv

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Negation well-defined) -/
instance Rat.neg_inst : Neg Rat where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ↦ (-a) // b) (by sorry)

/-- Embedding the integers in the rationals -/
instance Rat.instIntCast : IntCast Rat where
  intCast a := a // 1

instance Rat.instNatCast : NatCast Rat where
  natCast n := (n:ℤ) // 1

instance Rat.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Rat n where
  ofNat := (n:ℤ) // 1

instance Rat.instRatCast : RatCast Rat where
  ratCast q := q.num // q.den

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (positivity) -/
def Rat.isPos (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ a b:ℤ, a > 0 ∧ b > 0 ∧ q = a/b

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (negativity) -/
def Rat.isNeg (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ r:Rat, r.isPos ∧ q = -r

/-- Lemma 4.2.7 (trichotomy of rationals) / Exercise 4.2.4 -/
theorem Rat.trichotomous (x:Rat) : x = 0 ∨ x.isPos ∨ x.isNeg := by sorry

end Section_4_2

/-- Proposition 9.4.7 / Exercise 9.4.1. -/
theorem tfae (X : Set ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ) (x₀ : ℝ) :
  [
    ContinuousWithinAt f X x₀,
    ∀ a : ℕ → ℝ, (∀ n, a n ∈ X) → Filter.atTop.Tendsto a (nhds x₀) →
      Filter.atTop.Tendsto (fun n ↦ f (a n)) (nhds (f x₀)),
    ∀ ε > 0, ∃ δ > 0, ∀ x ∈ X, |x - x₀| < δ → |f x - f x₀| < ε,
    ∀ ε > 0, ∃ δ > 0, ∀ x ∈ X, |x - x₀| ≤ δ → |f x - f x₀| ≤ ε
  ].TFAE := by
  sorry

end ContinuousWithinAt

namespace Chapter6

-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/

  ε.CloseSeq a L ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.m, dist (a n) L ≤ ε := by rfl

  ε.EventuallyClose a L ↔ ∃ N, (N ≥ a.m) ∧ ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L := by rfl

@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

instance Series.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Series where
  coe := fun a ↦ {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind
  }

@[simp]
theorem Series.eval_coe (a: ℕ → ℝ) (n: ℕ) : (a: Series).seq n = a n := by simp

abbrev Series.mk' {m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Series where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

theorem Series.eval_mk' {m:ℤ} (a : { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) {n : ℤ} (h:n ≥ m) :
    (Series.mk' a).seq n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop := ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

abbrev Series.abs (s:Series) : Series := mk' (m:=s.m) (fun n ↦ |s.seq n|)

abbrev Series.absConverges (s:Series) : Prop := s.abs.converges

theorem Series.absConverges_of_subseries {a:ℕ → ℝ} (ha: (a:Series).absConverges) {f: ℕ → ℕ} (hf: StrictMono f) :
  (fun n ↦ a (f n):Series).absConverges := by sorry

end Chapter7

abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

/--
  Definition 6.1.2 (ε-close). This is similar to the previous notion of ε-closeness, but where
  all quantities are real instead of rational.
-/
theorem Real.close_def (ε x y : ℝ) : ε.Close x y ↔ dist x y ≤ ε := by rfl

namespace Chapter6

/--
  Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). This is similar to the Chapter 5 sequence, except that now the
  sequence is real-valued. As with Chapter 5, we start sequences from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h: n ≥ m) :
    (Sequence.mk' m a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℝ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by simp

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/

  (a.from m₁) n = a n := by
  simp [hn]; intros; symm; solve_by_elim [a.vanish]

/-- Definition 6.1.3 (ε-steady) -/
abbrev Real.Steady (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.m, ∀ m ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

  ε.Steady a ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.m, ∀ m ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) (a m) := by rfl

  ε.EventuallyClose a L ↔ ∃ N, (N ≥ a.m) ∧ ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L := by rfl

universe u v

class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object)

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/

  mem X x := mem x X

  ∀ P : Object → Prop, ∃ A : Set, ∀ x : Object, x ∈ A ↔ P x

  ∃ (U:Set), ∀ x, x ∈ U := by

instance PreRat.instSetoid : Setoid PreRat where
  r a b := a.numerator * b.denominator = b.numerator * a.denominator
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by sorry
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreRat.eq (a b c d:ℤ) (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (⟨ a,b,hb ⟩: PreRat) ≈ ⟨ c,d,hd ⟩ ↔ a * d = c * b := by rfl

abbrev Rat := Quotient PreRat.instSetoid

/-- We give division a "junk" value of 0//1 if the denominator is zero -/
abbrev Rat.formalDiv (a b:ℤ) : Rat :=
  Quotient.mk PreRat.instSetoid (if h:b ≠ 0 then ⟨ a,b,h ⟩ else ⟨ 0, 1, by decide ⟩)

infix:100 " // " => Rat.formalDiv

/-- Definition 4.2.1 (Rationals) -/
theorem Rat.eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0): a // b = c // d ↔ a * d = c * b := by
  simp [formalDiv, hb, hd, Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]

/-- Definition 4.2.1 (Rationals) -/
theorem Rat.eq_diff (n:Rat) : ∃ a b, b ≠ 0 ∧ n = a // b := by
  apply Quotient.ind _ n; intro ⟨ a, b, h ⟩
  refine ⟨ a, b, h, ?_ ⟩
  simp [formalDiv, h]

/--
  Decidability of equality. Hint: modify the proof of `DecidableEq Int` from the previous
  section. However, because formal division handles the case of zero denominator separately, it
  may be more convenient to avoid that operation and work directly with the `Quotient` API.
-/
instance Rat.decidableEq : DecidableEq Rat := by
  sorry

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Addition well-defined) -/
instance Rat.add_inst : Add Rat where
  add := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*d+b*c) // (b*d)) (by
    intro ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ⟨ a', b', h1' ⟩ ⟨ c', d', h2' ⟩ h3 h4
    simp_all [Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]
    linear_combination d * d' * h3 + b * b' * h4
  )

/-- Definition 4.2.2 (Addition of rationals) -/
theorem Rat.add_eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (a // b) + (c // d) = (a*d + b*c) // (b*d) := by
  convert Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _ <;> simp [hb, hd]

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Multiplication well-defined) -/
instance Rat.mul_inst : Mul Rat where
  mul := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*c) // (b*d)) (by sorry)

/-- Definition 4.2.2 (Multiplication of rationals) -/
theorem Rat.mul_eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (a // b) * (c // d) = (a*c) // (b*d) := by
  convert Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _ <;> simp [hb, hd]

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Negation well-defined) -/
instance Rat.neg_inst : Neg Rat where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ↦ (-a) // b) (by sorry)

/-- Definition 4.2.2 (Negation of rationals) -/
theorem Rat.neg_eq (a:ℤ) {b:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) : - (a // b) = (-a) // b := by
  convert Quotient.lift_mk _ _ _ <;> simp [hb]

/-- Embedding the integers in the rationals -/
instance Rat.instIntCast : IntCast Rat where
  intCast a := a // 1

instance Rat.instNatCast : NatCast Rat where
  natCast n := (n:ℤ) // 1

instance Rat.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Rat n where
  ofNat := (n:ℤ) // 1

theorem Rat.coe_Int_eq (a:ℤ) : (a:Rat) = a // 1 := rfl

theorem Rat.coe_Nat_eq (n:ℕ) : (n:Rat) = n // 1 := rfl

theorem Rat.of_Nat_eq (n:ℕ) : (ofNat(n):Rat) = (ofNat(n):Nat) // 1 := rfl

/--
  Whereas the book leaves the inverse of 0 undefined, it is more convenient in Lean to assign a
  "junk" value to this inverse; we arbitrarily choose this junk value to be 0.
-/
instance Rat.instInv : Inv Rat where
  inv := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ↦ b // a) (by
    sorry -- hint: split into the `a=0` and `a≠0` cases
)

lemma Rat.inv_eq (a:ℤ) {b:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) : (a // b)⁻¹ = b // a := by
  convert Quotient.lift_mk _ _ _ <;> simp [hb]

@[simp]
theorem Rat.inv_zero : (0:Rat)⁻¹ = 0 := rfl

/-- Proposition 4.2.4 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.2.3 -/
instance Rat.instCommMonoid : CommMonoid Rat where
  mul_comm := by sorry
  mul_assoc := by sorry
  one_mul := by sorry
  mul_one := by sorry

/-- Default definition of division -/
instance Rat.instDivInvMonoid : DivInvMonoid Rat where

theorem Rat.div_eq (q r:Rat) : q/r = q * r⁻¹ := by rfl

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (positivity) -/
def Rat.isPos (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ a b:ℤ, a > 0 ∧ b > 0 ∧ q = a/b

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (negativity) -/
def Rat.isNeg (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ r:Rat, r.isPos ∧ q = -r

/-- Lemma 4.2.7 (trichotomy of rationals) / Exercise 4.2.4 -/
theorem Rat.not_zero_and_neg (x:Rat) : ¬(x = 0 ∧ x.isNeg) := by sorry

end Section_4_2

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Exercise 3.2.2 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.not_mem_mem (A B:Set) : (A:Object) ∉ B ∨ (B:Object) ∉ A := by sorry

end Chapter3

structure Series where

  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as series. -/
instance Series.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Series where
  coe := fun a ↦ {
    m := 0

  ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

  Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

namespace Chapter6

/--
  Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). This is similar to the Chapter 5 sequence, except that now the
  sequence is real-valued. As with Chapter 5, we start sequences from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.5 (ε-close to a limit along a sequence). -/
abbrev Real.CloseSeq (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 (eventually ε-close to a limit along a sequence). -/
abbrev Real.EventuallyClose (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

namespace Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.5 (limit of a real sequence). -/
abbrev Sequence.TendsTo (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

/-- Lemma 6.5.2 / Exercise 6.5.2 -/
theorem Sequence.lim_of_geometric {x:ℝ} (hx: |x| < 1) : ((fun (n:ℕ) ↦ x^n):Sequence).TendsTo 0 := by
  sorry

end Chapter6

 {

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h: n ≥ m) :

 theorem Series.eval_mk' {m:ℤ} (a : { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) {n : ℤ} (h:n ≥ m) :

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Exercise 3.2.2 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.not_mem_self (A:Set) : (A:Object) ∉ A := by sorry

end Chapter3

open BigOperators Filter

@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

abbrev Series.mk' {m : ℤ} (a : { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Series where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

abbrev Series.abs (s : Series) : Series :=
  mk' (m := s.m) (fun n ↦ |s.seq n|)

abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N : ℤ) : ℝ :=
  ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L : ℝ) : Prop :=
  Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop :=
  ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

abbrev Series.absConverges (s : Series) : Prop :=
  s.abs.converges

/-- Corollary 7.5.3 (Ratio test) / Exercise 7.5.3 -/
theorem Series.ratio_test_inconclusive' : ∃ s:Series, (∀ n ≥ s.m, s.seq n ≠ 0) ∧
  atTop.Tendsto (fun n ↦ |s.seq (n+1)| / |s.seq n|) (nhds 1) ∧ s.absConverges := by
    sorry

end Chapter7

/--
  Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). This is similar to the Chapter 5 sequence, except that now the
  sequence is real-valued. As with Chapter 5, we start sequences from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h: n ≥ m) :
    (Sequence.mk' m a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℝ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by simp

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {m₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ m₁) :
  (a.from m₁) n = a n := by
  simp [hn]; intros; symm; solve_by_elim [a.vanish]

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/

  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Universal specification) -/
abbrev axiom_of_universal_specification : Prop :=
  ∀ P : Object → Prop, ∃ A : Set, ∀ x : Object, x ∈ A ↔ P x

/--
  Exercise 3.2.1. The spirit of the exercise is to establish these results without using either
  Russell's paradox, or the specify operation.
-/
theorem SetTheory.Set.specify_exists (h: axiom_of_universal_specification) (A:Set) (P: A → Prop):
    ∃ (Z:Set), ∀ z, z ∈ Z ↔ ∃ h : z ∈ A, P ⟨ z, h ⟩ := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

structure PreRat where
  numerator : ℤ
  denominator : ℤ
  nonzero : denominator ≠ 0

instance PreRat.instSetoid : Setoid PreRat where
  r a b := a.numerator * b.denominator = b.numerator * a.denominator
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by sorry
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreRat.eq (a b c d:ℤ) (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (⟨ a,b,hb ⟩: PreRat) ≈ ⟨ c,d,hd ⟩ ↔ a * d = c * b := by rfl

abbrev Rat := Quotient PreRat.instSetoid

abbrev Rat.formalDiv (a b:ℤ) : Rat :=
  Quotient.mk PreRat.instSetoid (if h:b ≠ 0 then ⟨ a,b,h ⟩ else ⟨ 0, 1, by decide ⟩)

infix:100 " // " => Rat.formalDiv

theorem Rat.eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0): a // b = c // d ↔ a * d = c * b := by
  simp [formalDiv, hb, hd, Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]

theorem Rat.eq_diff (n:Rat) : ∃ a b, b ≠ 0 ∧ n = a // b := by
  apply Quotient.ind _ n; intro ⟨ a, b, h ⟩
  refine ⟨ a, b, h, ?_ ⟩
  simp [formalDiv, h]

instance Rat.decidableEq : DecidableEq Rat := by
  sorry

instance Rat.add_inst : Add Rat where
  add := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*d+b*c) // (b*d)) (by
    intro ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ⟨ a', b', h1' ⟩ ⟨ c', d', h2' ⟩ h3 h4
    simp_all [Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]
    linear_combination d * d' * h3 + b * b' * h4
  )

theorem Rat.add_eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (a // b) + (c // d) = (a*d + b*c) // (b*d) := by
  convert Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _ <;> simp [hb, hd]

instance Rat.mul_inst : Mul Rat where
  mul := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*c) // (b*d)) (by sorry)

theorem Rat.mul_eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (a // b) * (c // d) = (a*c) // (b*d) := by
  convert Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _ <;> simp [hb, hd]

instance Rat.neg_inst : Neg Rat where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ↦ (-a) // b) (by sorry)

theorem Rat.neg_eq (a:ℤ) {b:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) : - (a // b) = (-a) // b := by
  convert Quotient.lift_mk _ _ _ <;> simp [hb]

instance Rat.instIntCast : IntCast Rat where
  intCast a := a // 1

instance Rat.instNatCast : NatCast Rat where
  natCast n := (n:ℤ) // 1

instance Rat.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Rat n where
  ofNat := (n:ℤ) // 1

theorem Rat.coe_Int_eq (a:ℤ) : (a:Rat) = a // 1 := rfl


theorem Rat.coe_Nat_eq (n:ℕ) : (n:Rat) = n // 1 := rfl


theorem Rat.of_Nat_eq (n:ℕ) : (ofNat(n):Rat) = (ofNat(n):Nat) // 1 := rfl

theorem Rat.natCast_succ (n: ℕ) : ((n + 1: ℕ): Rat) = (n: Rat) + 1 := by sorry

lemma Rat.intCast_add (a b:ℤ) : (a:Rat) + (b:Rat) = (a+b:ℤ) := by sorry

lemma Rat.intCast_mul (a b:ℤ) : (a:Rat) * (b:Rat) = (a*b:ℤ) := by sorry

lemma Rat.intCast_neg (a:ℤ) : - (a:Rat) = (-a:ℤ) := rfl


theorem Rat.coe_Int_inj : Function.Injective (fun n:ℤ ↦ (n:Rat)) := by sorry

instance Rat.instInv : Inv Rat where
  inv := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ↦ b // a) (by
    sorry -- hint: split into the `a=0` and `a≠0` cases
)

lemma Rat.inv_eq (a:ℤ) {b:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) : (a // b)⁻¹ = b // a := by
  convert Quotient.lift_mk _ _ _ <;> simp [hb]

@[simp]
theorem Rat.inv_zero : (0:Rat)⁻¹ = 0 := rfl

instance Rat.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Rat :=
AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by
  intro x y z
  obtain ⟨ a, b, hb, rfl ⟩ := eq_diff x
  obtain ⟨ c, d, hd, rfl ⟩ := eq_diff y
  obtain ⟨ e, f, hf, rfl ⟩ := eq_diff z
  have hbd : b*d ≠ 0 := Int.mul_ne_zero hb hd
  have hdf : d*f ≠ 0 := Int.mul_ne_zero hd hf
  have hbdf : b*d*f ≠ 0 := Int.mul_ne_zero hbd hf
  rw [add_eq _ _ hb hd, add_eq _ _ hbd hf, add_eq _ _ hd hf,
      add_eq _ _ hb hdf, ←mul_assoc b, eq _ _ hbdf hbdf]
  ring
)
 (by sorry) (by sorry)

  add_comm := by sorry

  mul_comm := by sorry

  one_mul := by sorry
  mul_one := by sorry

instance Rat.instCommRing : CommRing Rat where
  left_distrib := by sorry
  right_distrib := by sorry
  zero_mul := by sorry
  mul_zero := by sorry
  mul_assoc := by sorry
  natCast_succ := natCast_succ

instance Rat.instRatCast : RatCast Rat where
  ratCast q := q.num // q.den

 theorem Rat.ratCast_inj : Function.Injective (fun n:ℚ ↦ (n:Rat)) := by sorry

 theorem Rat.coe_Rat_eq (a:ℤ) {b:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) : (a/b:ℚ) = a // b := by
  set q := (a/b:ℚ)
  set num :ℤ := q.num
  set den :ℤ := (q.den:ℤ)
  have hden : den ≠ 0 := by simp [den, q.den_nz]
  change num // den = a // b
  rw [eq _ _ hden hb]
  qify
  have hq : num / den = q := Rat.num_div_den q
  rwa [div_eq_div_iff] at hq <;> simp [hden, hb]

instance Rat.instDivInvMonoid : DivInvMonoid Rat where

 theorem Rat.div_eq (q r:Rat) : q/r = q * r⁻¹ := by rfl

instance Rat.instField : Field Rat where
  exists_pair_ne := by sorry
  mul_inv_cancel := by sorry
  inv_zero := rfl
  ratCast_def := by
    intro q
    set num := q.num
    set den := q.den
    have hden : (den:ℤ) ≠ 0 := by simp [den, q.den_nz]
    rw [← Rat.num_div_den q]
    convert coe_Rat_eq _ hden
    rw [coe_Int_eq, coe_Nat_eq, div_eq, inv_eq, mul_eq, eq] <;> simp [num, den, q.den_nz]
  qsmul := _
  nnqsmul := _

example : (3//4) / (5//6) = 9 // 10 := by sorry

  toFun n := (n:Rat)
  map_zero' := rfl
  map_one' := rfl
  map_add' := by sorry
  map_mul' := by sorry

def Rat.isPos (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ a b:ℤ, a > 0 ∧ b > 0 ∧ q = a/b

def Rat.isNeg (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ r:Rat, r.isPos ∧ q = -r

 theorem Rat.trichotomous (x:Rat) : x = 0 ∨ x.isPos ∨ x.isNeg := by sorry

 theorem Rat.not_zero_and_pos (x:Rat) : ¬(x = 0 ∧ x.isPos) := by sorry

instance Rat.instLT : LT Rat where
  lt x y := (x-y).isNeg

instance Rat.instLE : LE Rat where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

 theorem Rat.lt_iff (x y:Rat) : x < y ↔ (x-y).isNeg := by rfl
 theorem Rat.le_iff (x y:Rat) : x ≤ y ↔ (x < y) ∨ (x = y) := by rfl

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

namespace Section_4_2

structure PreRat where
  numerator : ℤ
  denominator : ℤ
  nonzero : denominator ≠ 0

/-- Exercise 4.2.1 -/
instance PreRat.instSetoid : Setoid PreRat where
  r a b := a.numerator * b.denominator = b.numerator * a.denominator
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by sorry
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreRat.eq (a b c d:ℤ) (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (⟨ a,b,hb ⟩: PreRat) ≈ ⟨ c,d,hd ⟩ ↔ a * d = c * b := by rfl

abbrev Rat := Quotient PreRat.instSetoid

/-- We give division a "junk" value of 0//1 if the denominator is zero -/
abbrev Rat.formalDiv (a b:ℤ) : Rat :=
  Quotient.mk PreRat.instSetoid (if h:b ≠ 0 then ⟨ a,b,h ⟩ else ⟨ 0, 1, by decide ⟩)

infix:100 " // " => Rat.formalDiv

/-- Definition 4.2.1 (Rationals) -/
theorem Rat.eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0): a // b = c // d ↔ a * d = c * b := by
  simp [formalDiv, hb, hd, Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Negation well-defined) -/
instance Rat.neg_inst : Neg Rat where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ↦ (-a) // b) (by sorry)

/-- Embedding the integers in the rationals -/
instance Rat.instIntCast : IntCast Rat where
  intCast a := a // 1

/-- Provide a minimal division structure on Rat to parse `a / b` in `isPos`. -/
instance Aux_instDivRat : Div Rat where
  div a b := a

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (positivity) -/
def Rat.isPos (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ a b:ℤ, a > 0 ∧ b > 0 ∧ q = a/b

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (negativity) -/
def Rat.isNeg (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ r:Rat, r.isPos ∧ q = -r

/-- Lemma 4.2.7 (trichotomy of rationals) / Exercise 4.2.4 -/
theorem Rat.not_pos_and_neg (x:Rat) : ¬(x.isPos ∧ x.isNeg) := by sorry

end Section_4_2

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 6 sequences and the target theorem from Section 6.5.
We avoid importing any Analysis.Section_* modules from the textbook project.
-/

/- Definition 6.1.1 (Distance). Here we use the Mathlib distance. -/
abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

/-- Definition 6.1.2 (ε-close). -/
theorem Real.close_def (ε x y : ℝ) : ε.Close x y ↔ dist x y ≤ ε := by rfl

namespace Chapter6

/--
  Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). Real-valued sequences indexed by ℤ with a starting index m,
  and extended by zero to the left of m.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be coerced to sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h: n ≥ m) :
    (Sequence.mk' m a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℝ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by simp

/--
  a.from m₁ starts `a : Sequence` from `m₁`. It is intended for use when `m₁ ≥ a.m`,
  but returns the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {m₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ m₁) :
  (a.from m₁) n = a n := by
  simp [hn]; intros; symm; solve_by_elim [a.vanish]

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
abbrev Real.CloseSeq (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
theorem Real.closeSeq_def (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  ε.CloseSeq a L ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.m, dist (a n) L ≤ ε := by rfl

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
abbrev Real.EventuallyClose (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
theorem Real.eventuallyClose_def (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  ε.EventuallyClose a L ↔ ∃ N, (N ≥ a.m) ∧ ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L := by rfl

namespace Chapter6

abbrev Sequence.TendsTo (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

/-- Unfolding lemma for TendsTo. -/
theorem Sequence.tendsTo_def (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  a.TendsTo L ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L := by rfl

/-- Lemma 6.5.3 / Exercise 6.5.3 -/
theorem Sequence.lim_of_roots {x:ℝ} (hx: x > 0) :
    ((fun (n:ℕ) ↦ x^(1/(n+1:ℝ))):Sequence).TendsTo 1 := by
  sorry

end Chapter6

/-!
Self-contained extraction for Section 4.2 minimal context to typecheck the target theorem.
-/

namespace Section_4_2

structure PreRat where
  numerator : ℤ
  denominator : ℤ
  nonzero : denominator ≠ 0

/-- Exercise 4.2.1 -/
instance PreRat.instSetoid : Setoid PreRat where
  r a b := a.numerator * b.denominator = b.numerator * a.denominator
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by sorry
  }

@[simp]
theorem PreRat.eq (a b c d:ℤ) (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (⟨ a,b,hb ⟩: PreRat) ≈ ⟨ c,d,hd ⟩ ↔ a * d = c * b := by rfl

abbrev Rat := Quotient PreRat.instSetoid

/-- We give division a "junk" value of 0//1 if the denominator is zero -/
abbrev Rat.formalDiv (a b:ℤ) : Rat :=
  Quotient.mk PreRat.instSetoid (if h:b ≠ 0 then ⟨ a,b,h ⟩ else ⟨ 0, 1, by decide ⟩)

infix:100 " // " => Rat.formalDiv

/-- Definition 4.2.1 (Rationals) -/
theorem Rat.eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0): a // b = c // d ↔ a * d = c * b := by
  simp [formalDiv, hb, hd, Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]

/-- Definition 4.2.1 (Rationals) -/
theorem Rat.eq_diff (n:Rat) : ∃ a b, b ≠ 0 ∧ n = a // b := by
  apply Quotient.ind _ n; intro ⟨ a, b, h ⟩
  refine ⟨ a, b, h, ?_ ⟩
  simp [formalDiv, h]

/--
  Decidability of equality. Hint: modify the proof of `DecidableEq Int` from the previous
  section. However, because formal division handles the case of zero denominator separately, it
  may be more convenient to avoid that operation and work directly with the `Quotient` API.
-/
instance Rat.decidableEq : DecidableEq Rat := by
  sorry

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Addition well-defined) -/
instance Rat.add_inst : Add Rat where
  add := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*d+b*c) // (b*d)) (by
    intro ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ⟨ a', b', h1' ⟩ ⟨ c', d', h2' ⟩ h3 h4
    simp_all [Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]
    linear_combination d * d' * h3 + b * b' * h4
  )

/-- Definition 4.2.2 (Addition of rationals) -/
theorem Rat.add_eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (a // b) + (c // d) = (a*d + b*c) // (b*d) := by
  convert Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _ <;> simp [hb, hd]

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Multiplication well-defined) -/
instance Rat.mul_inst : Mul Rat where
  mul := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*c) // (b*d)) (by sorry)

/-- Definition 4.2.2 (Multiplication of rationals) -/
theorem Rat.mul_eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (a // b) * (c // d) = (a*c) // (b*d) := by
  convert Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _ <;> simp [hb, hd]

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Negation well-defined) -/
instance Rat.neg_inst : Neg Rat where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ↦ (-a) // b) (by sorry)

/-- Definition 4.2.2 (Negation of rationals) -/
theorem Rat.neg_eq (a:ℤ) {b:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) : - (a // b) = (-a) // b := by
  convert Quotient.lift_mk _ _ _ <;> simp [hb]

/-- Embedding the integers in the rationals -/
instance Rat.instIntCast : IntCast Rat where
  intCast a := a // 1

instance Rat.instNatCast : NatCast Rat where
  natCast n := (n:ℤ) // 1

instance Rat.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Rat n where
  ofNat := (n:ℤ) // 1

theorem Rat.coe_Int_eq (a:ℤ) : (a:Rat) = a // 1 := rfl

/-- Proposition 4.2.4 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.2.3 -/
instance Rat.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Rat :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms
    (by
      -- this proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
      intro x y z
      obtain ⟨ a, b, hb, rfl ⟩ := eq_diff x
      obtain ⟨ c, d, hd, rfl ⟩ := eq_diff y
      obtain ⟨ e, f, hf, rfl ⟩ := eq_diff z
      have hbd : b*d ≠ 0 := Int.mul_ne_zero hb hd     -- can also use `observe hbd : b*d ≠ 0` here
      have hdf : d*f ≠ 0 := Int.mul_ne_zero hd hf     -- can also use `observe hdf : d*f ≠ 0` here
      have hbdf : b*d*f ≠ 0 := Int.mul_ne_zero hbd hf -- can also use `observe hbdf : b*d*f ≠ 0` here
      rw [add_eq _ _ hb hd, add_eq _ _ hbd hf, add_eq _ _ hd hf,
          add_eq _ _ hb hdf, ←mul_assoc b, eq _ _ hbdf hbdf]
      ring
    )
    (by sorry)
    (by sorry)

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (positivity) -/
def Rat.isPos (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ a b:ℤ, a > 0 ∧ b > 0 ∧ q = a/b

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (negativity) -/
def Rat.isNeg (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ r:Rat, r.isPos ∧ q = -r

/-- Definition 4.2.8 (Ordering of the rationals) -/
instance Rat.instLT : LT Rat where
  lt x y := (x-y).isNeg

/-- Proposition 4.2.9(a) (order trichotomy) / Exercise 4.2.5 -/
@[simp] theorem Rat.lt_iff (x y:Rat) : x < y ↔ (x-y).isNeg := by rfl

/-- Proposition 4.2.9(a) (order trichotomy) / Exercise 4.2.5 -/
 theorem Rat.not_gt_and_lt (x y:Rat) : ¬ (x > y ∧ x < y):= by sorry

end Section_4_2

macro_rules
  | `(tactic| grind) => `(tactic| first | intros; simp [*] | simp [*])

namespace Chapter7

open BigOperators Filter

/--
  Definition 7.2.1 (Formal infinite series). This is similar to Chapter 6 sequence, but is
  manipulated differently. As with Chapter 5, we will start series from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as series. -/
instance Series.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Series where
  coe := fun a ↦ {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind
  }

@[simp]
theorem Series.eval_coe (a: ℕ → ℝ) (n: ℕ) : (a: Series).seq n = a n := by simp

abbrev Series.mk' {m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Series where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

 theorem Series.eval_mk' {m:ℤ} (a : { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) {n : ℤ} (h:n ≥ m) :
    (Series.mk' a).seq n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

/-- Definition 7.2.2 (Convergence of series) -/
abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop := ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

/-- Exercise 7.5.2 -/
 theorem Series.poly_mul_geom_converges {x:ℝ} (hx: |x|<1) (q:ℝ) : (fun n:ℕ ↦ (n:ℝ)^q * x^n : Series).converges
  ∧ atTop.Tendsto (fun n:ℕ ↦ (n:ℝ)^q * x^n) (nhds 0) := by
  sorry

end Chapter7

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Universal specification) -/
abbrev axiom_of_universal_specification : Prop :=
  ∀ P : Object → Prop, ∃ A : Set, ∀ x : Object, x ∈ A ↔ P x

/--
  Exercise 3.2.1. The spirit of the exercise is to establish these results without using either
  Russell's paradox, or the union operation.
-/
theorem SetTheory.Set.union_exists (h: axiom_of_universal_specification) (A B:Set):
    ∃ (Z:Set), ∀ z, z ∈ Z ↔ z ∈ A ∨ z ∈ B := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/

 {

  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {m₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ m₁) :
  (a.from m₁) n = a n := by
  simp [hn]; intros; symm; solve_by_elim [a.vanish]

  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ

  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {

 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as sequences. -/

  m := m

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h: n ≥ m) :
    (Sequence.mk' m a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℝ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by simp

  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.

  (a.from m₁) n = a n := by
  simp [hn]; intros; symm; solve_by_elim [a.vanish]

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/

theorem Real.closeSeq_def (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  ε.CloseSeq a L ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.m, dist (a n) L ≤ ε := by rfl

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
theorem Real.eventuallyClose_def (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  ε.EventuallyClose a L ↔ ∃ N, (N ≥ a.m) ∧ ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L := by rfl

namespace Chapter6

  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

namespace Chapter3

/- The ability to work in multiple universe is not relevant immediately, but
becomes relevant when constructing models of set theory in the Chapter 3 epilogue. -/
universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Universal specification) -/
abbrev axiom_of_universal_specification : Prop :=
  ∀ P : Object → Prop, ∃ A : Set, ∀ x : Object, x ∈ A ↔ P x

/--
  Exercise 3.2.1.  The spirit of the exercise is to establish these results without using either
  Russell's paradox, or the singleton set.
-/
theorem SetTheory.Set.singleton_exists (h: axiom_of_universal_specification) (x:Object):
    ∃ (X:Set), ∀ y, y ∈ X ↔ y = x := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

/- Definition 6.1.1 (Distance).  Here we use the Mathlib distance. -/
abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

  sorry

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/

  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8

  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :

    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/

namespace Chapter6

abbrev Sequence.TendsTo (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

/-- Definition 6.1.8 -/
abbrev Sequence.Convergent (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∃ L, a.TendsTo L

open Classical in
/-- Definition 6.1.8. We give the limit of a sequence the junk value 0 if not convergent. -/
noncomputable abbrev lim (a:Sequence) : ℝ := if h: a.Convergent then h.choose else 0

/-- Provide a very small version of the `grind` tactic used in the textbook proofs. -/
macro "grind" : tactic =>
  `(tactic|
    first
    | apply lt_of_lt_of_le; first | assumption; first | exact min_le_left _ _
    | apply lt_of_lt_of_le; first | assumption; first | exact min_le_right _ _
    | try simp
    | try decide)

noncomputable instance Sequence.inst_div : Div Sequence where
  div a b := {
    m := min a.m b.m
    seq n := a n / b n
    vanish n hn := by
      simp [a.vanish n (by grind), b.vanish n (by grind)]
  }

@[simp]
theorem Sequence.div_eval {a b: Sequence} (n:ℤ) : (a / b) n = a n / b n := rfl

/-- Exercise 6.1.9 -/
theorem Sequence.lim_div_fail :
    ∃ a b, a.Convergent
    ∧ b.Convergent
    ∧ lim b = 0
    ∧ ¬ ((a / b).Convergent ∧ lim (a / b) = lim a / lim b) := by
  sorry

end Chapter6

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

namespace Section_4_2

structure PreRat where
  numerator : ℤ
  denominator : ℤ
  nonzero : denominator ≠ 0

/-- Exercise 4.2.1 -/
instance PreRat.instSetoid : Setoid PreRat where
  r a b := a.numerator * b.denominator = b.numerator * a.denominator
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by sorry
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreRat.eq (a b c d:ℤ) (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0) :
    (⟨ a,b,hb ⟩: PreRat) ≈ ⟨ c,d,hd ⟩ ↔ a * d = c * b := by rfl

abbrev Rat := Quotient PreRat.instSetoid

/-- We give division a "junk" value of 0//1 if the denominator is zero -/
abbrev Rat.formalDiv (a b:ℤ) : Rat :=
  Quotient.mk PreRat.instSetoid (if h:b ≠ 0 then ⟨ a,b,h ⟩ else ⟨ 0, 1, by decide ⟩)

infix:100 " // " => Rat.formalDiv

/-- Definition 4.2.1 (Rationals) -/
theorem Rat.eq (a c:ℤ) {b d:ℤ} (hb: b ≠ 0) (hd: d ≠ 0): a // b = c // d ↔ a * d = c * b := by
  simp [formalDiv, hb, hd, Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Addition well-defined) -/
instance Rat.add_inst : Add Rat where
  add := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ↦ (a*d+b*c) // (b*d)) (by
    intro ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ⟨ c, d, h2 ⟩ ⟨ a', b', h1' ⟩ ⟨ c', d', h2' ⟩ h3 h4
    simp_all [Quotient.eq, PreRat.instSetoid]
    linear_combination d * d' * h3 + b * b' * h4
  )

/-- Lemma 4.2.3 (Negation well-defined) -/
instance Rat.neg_inst : Neg Rat where
  neg := Quotient.lift (fun ⟨ a, b, h1 ⟩ ↦ (-a) // b) (by sorry)

/-- Auxiliary: define subtraction directly from addition and negation (for this snippet). -/
instance Aux_instHSubRat : HSub Rat Rat Rat where
  hSub x y := x + (-y)

/-- Embedding the integers in the rationals -/
instance Rat.instIntCast : IntCast Rat where
  intCast a := a // 1

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (positivity) -/
def Rat.isPos (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ a b:ℤ, a > 0 ∧ b > 0 ∧ q = a/b

/-- Definition 4.2.6 (negativity) -/
def Rat.isNeg (q:Rat) : Prop := ∃ r:Rat, r.isPos ∧ q = -r

/-- Definition 4.2.8 (Ordering of the rationals) -/
instance Rat.instLT : LT Rat where
  lt x y := (x-y).isNeg

/-- Definition 4.2.8 (Ordering of the rationals) -/
instance Rat.instLE : LE Rat where
  le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

/-- Definition of subtraction -/
@[simp] theorem Rat.sub_eq (a b:Rat) : a - b = a + (-b) := by rfl

/-- Proposition 4.2.9(a) (order trichotomy) / Exercise 4.2.5 -/
theorem Rat.not_lt_and_eq (x y:Rat) : ¬ (x < y ∧ x = y):= by sorry

end Section_4_2

  iseqv := {

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Universal specification) -/
abbrev axiom_of_universal_specification : Prop :=
  ∀ P : Object → Prop, ∃ A : Set, ∀ x : Object, x ∈ A ↔ P x

/--
  Exercise 3.2.1.  The spirit of the exercise is to establish these results without using either
  Russell's paradox, or the pair set.
-/
 theorem SetTheory.Set.pair_exists (h: axiom_of_universal_specification) (x₁ x₂:Object):
    ∃ (X:Set), ∀ y, y ∈ X ↔ y = x₁ ∨ y = x₂ := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

  mem X x := mem x X

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := emptyset

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := singleton

/-- Definition 3.1.14.   -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

  Note that the strict subset operation in Mathlib is denoted `⊂` rather than `⊊`.
-/

  SSubset X Y := X ⊆ Y ∧ X ≠ Y

import Mathlib.Tactic

import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Pow

/-- We use Mathlib's real distance. -/
abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

namespace Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence of reals). -/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be evaluated as functions ℤ → ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
  { m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all }

/-- Functions from ℕ → ℝ coerce to sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m : ℤ) (a : { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence :=
  { m := m
    seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
    vanish := by simp_all }

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m : ℤ} (a : { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h : n ≥ m) :
    (Sequence.mk' m a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n : ℕ) (a : ℕ → ℝ) : (a : Sequence) n = a n := by simp

/-- Start a sequence from a later index. -/
abbrev Sequence.from (a : Sequence) (m₁ : ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a : Sequence) {m₁ n : ℤ} (hn : n ≥ m₁) :
  (a.from m₁) n = a n := by
  simp [hn]; intros; symm; solve_by_elim [a.vanish]

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.3 (ε-steady, eventually steady) helpers for limits. -/
abbrev Real.CloseSeq (ε : ℝ) (a : Chapter6.Sequence) (L : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) L

abbrev Real.EventuallyClose (ε : ℝ) (a : Chapter6.Sequence) (L : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

namespace Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.3 (TendsTo). -/
abbrev Sequence.TendsTo (a : Sequence) (L : ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0 : ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

/-- Convergent sequence. -/
abbrev Sequence.Convergent (a : Sequence) : Prop := ∃ L, a.TendsTo L

open Classical in
/-- Limit of a sequence (junk value 0 if divergent). -/
noncomputable abbrev lim (a : Sequence) : ℝ :=
  if h : a.Convergent then h.choose else 0

end Chapter6

namespace Chapter6
open Sequence Real

namespace Real

/-- From Mathlib reals, every real is a limit of rationals (as a sequence). -/
-- We use the exact statement from the textbook; the proof is omitted here.
theorem eq_lim_of_rat (α : ℝ) : ∃ q : ℕ → ℚ, ((fun n ↦ (q n : ℝ)) : Chapter6.Sequence).TendsTo α := by
  sorry

/-- Definition 6.7.2 (Exponentiation to a real exponent). -/
noncomputable abbrev rpow (x : ℝ) (α : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  Chapter6.lim ((fun n ↦ x ^ ((eq_lim_of_rat α).choose n : ℝ)) : Chapter6.Sequence)

end Real

/-- Proposition 6.7.3(a) / Exercise 6.7.1. -/
-- We keep the exact statement; the proof is omitted.
theorem Real.ratPow_nonneg {x : ℝ} (hx : x > 0) (q : ℝ) : rpow x q ≥ 0 := by
  sorry

end Chapter6
