namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instEmpty : EmptyCollection BoundedInterval where
  emptyCollection := Ioo 0 0

@[simp]
 theorem BoundedInterval.coe_empty : ((∅ : BoundedInterval):Set ℝ) = ∅ := by
  simp [toSet]

open Classical in
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval := instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

namespace Chapter9
abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M
end Chapter9

open Chapter9

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

open BoundedInterval

/-- Proposition 11.10.1 (Integration by parts formula) / Exercise 11.10.1 -/
 theorem integ_of_mul_deriv {a b:ℝ} (hab: a ≤ b) {F G: ℝ → ℝ}
  (hF: DifferentiableOn ℝ F (Icc a b)) (hG : DifferentiableOn ℝ G (Icc a b))
  (hF': IntegrableOn (derivWithin F (Icc a b)) (Icc a b))
  (hG': IntegrableOn (derivWithin G (Icc a b)) (Icc a b)) :
  integ (F * derivWithin G (Icc a b)) (Icc a b) = F b * G b - F a * G a -
    integ (G * derivWithin F (Icc a b)) (Icc a b) := by
    sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := SetTheory.emptyset

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

instance SetTheory.Set.instIntersection : Inter Set where
  inter X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∈ Y)

instance SetTheory.Set.instSDiff : SDiff Set where
  sdiff X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∉ Y)

/-- Exercise 3.1.9 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.partition_right {A B X:Set} (h_union: A ∪ B = X) (h_inter: A ∩ B = ∅) :
    B = X \ A := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

theorem finite_series_add {m n:ℤ} (a b: ℤ → ℝ) :
  ∑ i ∈ Icc m n, (a i + b i) = ∑ i ∈ Icc m n, a i + ∑ i ∈ Icc m n, b i := by
  sorry

end Finset

/-!
# Analysis I, Section 3.1: Fundamentals (of set theory)

This file extracts a minimal, dependency-faithful slice needed to state and typecheck
the target theorem `SetTheory.Set.pairwise_disjoint` from Section 3.1.
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := set_to_object X

/-- Axiom 3.2 (Equality of sets).  The `[ext]` tag allows the `ext` tactic to work for sets. -/
@[ext]
theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y := extensionality _ _ h

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := emptyset

-- Make everything we define in `SetTheory.Set.*` accessible directly.
open SetTheory.Set

/--
  Axiom 3.3 (empty set).
  Note: in some applications one may have to explicitly cast ∅ to Set due to
  Mathlib's existing set theory notation.
-/
@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.not_mem_empty : ∀ x, x ∉ (∅:Set) := emptyset_mem

/-- Definition 3.1.14.   -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/-- Definition 3.1.14. -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.subset_def (X Y:Set) : X ⊆ Y ↔ ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y := by rfl

/-- Examples 3.1.16 -/
@[simp, refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.subset_self (A:Set) : A ⊆ A := by sorry

/-- Proposition 3.1.17 (Partial ordering by set inclusion) -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.subset_trans {A B C:Set} (hAB:A ⊆ B) (hBC:B ⊆ C) : A ⊆ C := by
  -- This proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
  rw [subset_def]
  intro x hx
  rw [subset_def] at hAB
  apply hAB x at hx
  apply hBC x at hx
  assumption

/-- Proposition 3.1.17 (Partial ordering by set inclusion) -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.subset_antisymm (A B:Set) (hAB:A ⊆ B) (hBA:B ⊆ A) : A = B := by
  sorry

/-- Empty set is minimal -/
@[simp]
theorem SetTheory.Set.empty_subset (A:Set) : ∅ ⊆ A := by sorry

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

instance SetTheory.Set.instIntersection : Inter Set where
  inter X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∈ Y)

instance SetTheory.Set.instSDiff : SDiff Set where
  sdiff X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∉ Y)

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := union_pair

/-- Proposition 3.1.27(f) -/
theorem  SetTheory.Set.union_inter_distrib_left (A B C:Set) :
    A ∪ (B ∩ C) = (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C) := by
  sorry

/-- Not from textbook: sets form a distributive lattice. -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instDistribLattice : DistribLattice Set where
  le := (· ⊆ ·)
  le_refl := subset_self
  le_trans := fun _ _ _ ↦ subset_trans
  le_antisymm := subset_antisymm
  inf := (· ∩ ·)
  sup := (· ∪ ·)
  le_sup_left := by sorry
  le_sup_right := by sorry
  sup_le := by sorry
  inf_le_left := by sorry
  inf_le_right := by sorry
  le_inf := by sorry
  le_sup_inf := by
    intro X Y Z; change (X ∪ Y) ∩ (X ∪ Z) ⊆ X ∪ (Y ∩ Z)
    rw [←union_inter_distrib_left]

/-- Sets have a minimal element.  -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instOrderBot : OrderBot Set where
  bot := ∅
  bot_le := empty_subset

-- Now we've defined `A ≤ B` to mean `A ⊆ B`, and set `⊥` to `∅`.
-- This makes the `Disjoint` definition from Mathlib work with our `Set`.
example (A B: Set) : (A ≤ B) ↔ (A ⊆ B) := by rfl
example : ⊥ = (∅: Set) := by rfl
example (A B: Set) : Prop := Disjoint A B

/--
  Exercise 3.1.10.
  You may find `Function.onFun_apply` and the `fin_cases` tactic useful.
-/
theorem SetTheory.Set.pairwise_disjoint (A B:Set) :
    Pairwise (Function.onFun Disjoint ![A \ B, A ∩ B, B \ A]) := by sorry

end Chapter3

/-!
This file extracts the minimal context to typecheck the target theorem
from Chapter 8, Section 8.2, together with the Series interface from Chapter 7
that is referenced by the definitions here.
-/

namespace Chapter7

/--
  Definition 7.2.1 (Formal infinite series). This is similar to Chapter 6 sequence, but is
  manipulated differently. As with Chapter 5, we will start series from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as series. -/
instance Series.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Series where
  coe := fun a ↦ {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind
  }

@[simp]
theorem Series.eval_coe (a: ℕ → ℝ) (n: ℕ) : (a: Series).seq n = a n := by simp

abbrev Series.mk' {m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Series where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/-- Definition 7.2.2 (Convergence of series) -/
abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop := ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev Series.sum (s : Series) : ℝ := if h : s.converges then h.choose else 0

/-- Definition 7.2.8 (Absolute convergence) -/
abbrev Series.abs (s:Series) : Series := mk' (m:=s.m) (fun n ↦ |s.seq n|)

abbrev Series.absConverges (s:Series) : Prop := s.abs.converges

end Chapter7

/-! Section 8.2 -/

namespace Chapter8
open Chapter7 Chapter7.Series Finset Function Filter

/-- Definition 8.2.1 (Series on countable sets).  Note that with this definition, functions defined
on finite sets will not be absolutely convergent; one should use `AbsConvergent'` instead for such
cases.-/
abbrev AbsConvergent {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : Prop := ∃ g: ℕ → X, Bijective g ∧ (f ∘ g:Series).absConverges

open Classical in
/-- The definition has been chosen to give a sensible value when `X` is finite, even though
`AbsConvergent` is by definition false in this context. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sum {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : ℝ := if h: AbsConvergent f then (f ∘ h.choose:Series).sum else
  if _hX: Finite X then (∑ x ∈ @univ X (Fintype.ofFinite X), f x) else 0

/-- Lemma 8.2.3 / Exercise 8.2.1 (definition for absolutely convergent sums on arbitrary sets). -/
abbrev AbsConvergent' {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : Prop :=
  BddAbove ((fun A ↦ ∑ x ∈ A, |f x|) '' (Set.univ : Set (Finset X)))

/-- A generalized sum.  Note that this will give junk values if `f` is not `AbsConvergent'`. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sum' {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : ℝ := Sum (fun x : { x | f x ≠ 0 } ↦ f x)

/-- Proposition 8.2.6 (a) (Absolutely convergent series laws) / Exercise 8.2.3 -/
theorem Sum'.add {X:Type} {f g:X → ℝ} (hf: AbsConvergent' f) (hg: AbsConvergent' g) :
  AbsConvergent' (f+g) ∧ Sum' (f + g) = Sum' f + Sum' g := by
  sorry

end Chapter8

This is analogous to `Chapter3.SetTheory.Set.EqualCard`, but we are not using the latter since
the Chapter 3 set theory is deprecated. -/
abbrev EqualCard (X Y : Type) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

abbrev CountablyInfinite (X : Type) : Prop := EqualCard X ℕ

abbrev AtMostCountable (X : Type) : Prop := CountablyInfinite X ∨ Finite X

  AtMostCountable { x | f x ≠ 0 } := by
    sorry

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := emptyset

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

instance SetTheory.Set.instIntersection : Inter Set where
  inter X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∈ Y)

instance SetTheory.Set.instSDiff : SDiff Set where
  sdiff X Y := X.specify (fun x ↦ x.val ∉ Y)

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := union_pair

/-- Exercise 3.1.9 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.partition_left {A B X:Set} (h_union: A ∪ B = X) (h_inter: A ∩ B = ∅) :
    A = X \ B := by sorry

end Chapter3

   := by
  -- the first line of this construction should be either `apply isTrue` or `apply isFalse`.
  sorry

end Chapter9

namespace Chapter6

/--
  Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). This is similar to the Chapter 5 sequence, except that now the
  sequence is real-valued. As with Chapter 5, we start sequences from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

/-- Definition 6.3.1 -/
noncomputable abbrev Sequence.sup (a:Sequence) : EReal := sSup { x | ∃ n ≥ a.m, x = a n }

/-- Example 6.3.4 / Exercise 6.3.1 -/
theorem Exercise_6_3_1a : ((fun (n:ℕ) ↦ 1/((n:ℝ)+1)):Sequence).sup = 1 := by sorry

end Chapter6

  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- Definition 3.1.14.   -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/-- Definition 3.4.1.  Interestingly, the definition does not require S to be a subset of X. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.image {X Y:Set} (f:X → Y) (S: Set) : Set :=
  X.replace (P := fun x y ↦ f x = y ∧ x.val ∈ S) (by simp_all)

/--
  Definition 3.4.4 (inverse images).
  Again, it is not required that U be a subset of Y.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.preimage {X Y:Set} (f:X → Y) (U: Set) : Set := X.specify (P := fun x ↦ (f x).val ∈ U)

    (∀ S, S ⊆ X → preimage f (image f S) = S) ↔ Function.Injective f := by sorry

/- Exercise 9.8.4: state and prove an analogue of `MonotoneOne.exist_inverse` for `Antitone` functions. -/
theorem AntitoneOn.exist_inverse {a b:ℝ} (h: a < b) (f: ℝ → ℝ)
    (hcont: ContinuousOn f (.Icc a b)) (hmono: StrictAntiOn f (.Icc a b)) :
  f '' (.Icc a b) = .Icc (f b) (f a) ∧
  ∃ finv: ℝ → ℝ, ContinuousOn finv (.Icc (f b) (f a)) ∧ StrictAntiOn finv (.Icc (f b) (f a)) ∧
  finv '' (.Icc (f b) (f a)) = .Icc a b ∧
  (∀ x ∈ Set.Icc a b, finv (f x) = x) ∧
  ∀ y ∈ Set.Icc (f b) (f a), f (finv y) = y := by
  sorry

end Chapter9

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals (Section 11.1) -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- Coercion to a set of real numbers. -/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

/-- A convenient empty interval, used elsewhere in the project. -/
instance BoundedInterval.instEmpty : EmptyCollection BoundedInterval where
  emptyCollection := Ioo 0 0

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.coe_empty : ((∅ : BoundedInterval):Set ℝ) = ∅ := by
  simp [toSet]

open Classical in
/-- This is to make Finsets of BoundedIntervals work properly -/
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval := instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioo (a b:ℝ) : (Ioo a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioo a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Icc (a b:ℝ) : (Icc a b : Set ℝ) = .Icc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioc (a b:ℝ) : (Ioc a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ico (a b:ℝ) : (Ico a b : Set ℝ) = .Ico a b := by rfl

/-- Membership of a real number in a bounded interval. -/
instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

theorem BoundedInterval.mem_iff (I: BoundedInterval) (x:ℝ) :
  x ∈ I ↔ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ) := by rfl

/-- Subset relation between bounded intervals, defined via their underlying sets. -/
instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

theorem BoundedInterval.subset_iff (I J: BoundedInterval) :
  I ⊆ J ↔ (I:Set ℝ) ⊆ (J:Set ℝ) := by rfl

/-- The left endpoint of a bounded interval. -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

/-- The right endpoint of a bounded interval. -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

/-- Partitions of a bounded interval (Section 11.1). -/
@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

/-- Membership of an interval in a partition. -/
instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Finer-than (≤) relation on partitions (Definition 11.1.14). -/
instance Partition.instLE (I: BoundedInterval) : LE (Partition I) where
  le P P' := ∀ J ∈ P'.intervals, ∃ K ∈ P, J ⊆ K

/-- Constant functions (Definition 11.2.1). -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

/-- A function is constant on a set X ⊆ ℝ. -/
abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Piecewise constant with respect to a partition (Definition 11.2.3). -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Lemma 11.2.7 / Exercise 11.2.1 -/
theorem PiecewiseConstantWith.mono {f:ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval} {P P': Partition I} (hPP': P ≤ P')
  (hP: PiecewiseConstantWith f P) : PiecewiseConstantWith f P' := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

theorem abs_finite_series_le {m n:ℤ} (a: ℤ → ℝ) :

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/--
  This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.
  Note that `A.toSubtype` gives you a type, similar to how `Object` or `Set` are types.
  A value `x'` of type `A.toSubtype` combines some `x: Object` with a proof that `hx: x ∈ A`.

  To produce an element `x'` of this subtype, use `⟨ x, hx ⟩`, where `x: Object` and `hx: x ∈ A`.
  The object `x` associated to a subtype element `x'` is recovered as `x'.val`, and
  the property `hx` that `x` belongs to `A` is recovered as `x'.property`.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := SetTheory.Set.toSubtype A

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

/-- Definition 3.1.14.   -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instSubset : HasSubset Set where
  Subset X Y := ∀ x, x ∈ X → x ∈ Y

/-- Definition 3.4.1.  Interestingly, the definition does not require S to be a subset of X. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.image {X Y:Set} (f:X → Y) (S: Set) : Set :=
  X.replace (P := fun x y ↦ f x = y ∧ x.val ∈ S) (by simp_all)

/--
  Definition 3.4.4 (inverse images).
  Again, it is not required that U be a subset of Y.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.preimage {X Y:Set} (f:X → Y) (U: Set) : Set := X.specify (P := fun x ↦ (f x).val ∈ U)

/-- Exercise 3.4.5 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.image_preimage_of_surj {X Y:Set} (f:X → Y) :
    (∀ S, S ⊆ Y → image f (preimage f S) = S) ↔ Function.Surjective f := by sorry

end Chapter3

@[simp] theorem BoundedInterval.mem_iff (I:BoundedInterval) (x:ℝ) : x ∈ I ↔ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ) := by rfl

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where

open Chapter9

abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  : ℝ :=
  ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x
abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

open BoundedInterval

theorem Exercise_11_10_3 {a b:ℝ} (hab: a < b) {f: ℝ → ℝ} (hf: IntegrableOn f (Icc a b)) :
  IntegrableOn (fun x ↦ f (-x)) (Icc (-b) (-a)) ∧
  integ (fun x ↦ f (-x)) (Icc (-b) (-a)) = -integ f (Icc a b) := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter7

@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as series. -/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Series where
  coe := fun a ↦ {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind
  }

abbrev Series.mk' {m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Series where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/-- Definition 7.2.2 (Convergence of series) -/
abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop := ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev Series.sum (s : Series) : ℝ := if h : s.converges then h.choose else 0

abbrev Series.abs (s:Series) : Series := mk' (m:=s.m) (fun n ↦ |s.seq n|)

abbrev Series.absConverges (s:Series) : Prop := s.abs.converges

end Chapter7

namespace Chapter8
open Chapter7

/-- Definition 8.2.1 (Series on countable sets). -/
abbrev AbsConvergent {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ g: ℕ → X, Function.Bijective g ∧ (f ∘ g:Chapter7.Series).absConverges

open Classical in
/-- A generalized sum `Sum` for absolutely convergent series on arbitrary sets. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sum {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : ℝ :=
  if h: AbsConvergent f then (f ∘ h.choose:Chapter7.Series).sum else
  if _hX: Finite X then (∑ x ∈ @Finset.univ X (Fintype.ofFinite X), f x) else 0

/-- Absolute convergence on arbitrary sets (finite or countable). -/
abbrev AbsConvergent' {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : Prop :=
  BddAbove ((fun A ↦ ∑ x ∈ A, |f x|) '' (Set.univ : Set (Finset X)))

/-- A generalized sum. Note: gives junk values if `f` is not `AbsConvergent'`. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sum' {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : ℝ :=
  Sum (fun x : { x | f x ≠ 0 } ↦ f x)

/-- Proposition 8.2.6 (b) (Absolutely convergent series laws) / Exercise 8.2.3 -/
theorem Sum'.smul {X:Type} {f:X → ℝ} (hf: AbsConvergent' f) (c: ℝ) :
  AbsConvergent' (c • f) ∧ Sum' (c • f) = c * Sum' f := by
  sorry

end Chapter8

namespace Chapter7

open BigOperators

/--
  Definition 7.2.1 (Formal infinite series). This is similar to Chapter 6 sequence, but is
  manipulated differently. As with Chapter 5, we will start series from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Series where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as series. -/
instance Series.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Series where
  coe := fun a ↦ {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind
  }

@[simp]
theorem Series.eval_coe (a: ℕ → ℝ) (n: ℕ) : (a: Series).seq n = a n := by simp

abbrev Series.mk' {m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Series where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/-- Definition 7.2.2 (Convergence of series) -/
abbrev Series.partial (s : Series) (N:ℤ) : ℝ := ∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc s.m N, s.seq n

abbrev Series.convergesTo (s : Series) (L:ℝ) : Prop := Filter.atTop.Tendsto (s.partial) (nhds L)

abbrev Series.converges (s : Series) : Prop := ∃ L, s.convergesTo L

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev Series.sum (s : Series) : ℝ := if h : s.converges then h.choose else 0

/-- Definition 7.2.8 (Absolute convergence) -/
abbrev Series.abs (s:Series) : Series := mk' (m:=s.m) (fun n ↦ |s.seq n|)

abbrev Series.absConverges (s:Series) : Prop := s.abs.converges

end Chapter7

namespace Chapter8
open Chapter7 Chapter7.Series Finset Function Filter

/-- Definition 8.2.1 (Series on countable sets).  Note that with this definition, functions defined
on finite sets will not be absolutely convergent; one should use `AbsConvergent'` instead for such
cases.-/
abbrev AbsConvergent {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : Prop := ∃ g: ℕ → X, Bijective g ∧ (f ∘ g: Series).absConverges

open Classical in
/-- The definition has been chosen to give a sensible value when `X` is finite, even though
`AbsConvergent` is by definition false in this context. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sum {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : ℝ := if h: AbsConvergent f then (f ∘ h.choose:Series).sum else
  if _hX: Finite X then (∑ x ∈ @univ X (Fintype.ofFinite X), f x) else 0

abbrev AbsConvergent' {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : Prop := BddAbove ( (fun A ↦ ∑ x ∈ A, |f x|) '' .univ )

/-- A generalized sum.  Note that this will give junk values if `f` is not `AbsConvergent'`. -/
noncomputable abbrev Sum' {X:Type} (f: X → ℝ) : ℝ := Sum (fun x : { x | f x ≠ 0 } ↦ f x)

/-- Proposition 8.2.6 (c) (Absolutely convergent series laws) / Exercise 8.2.3.  The first
    part of this proposition has been moved to `AbsConvergent'.subtype`. -/
theorem Sum'.of_disjoint_union {X:Type} {f:X → ℝ} (hf: AbsConvergent' f) {X₁ X₂ : Set X} (hdisj: Disjoint X₁ X₂):
  Sum' (fun x: (X₁ ∪ X₂: Set X) ↦ f x) = Sum' (fun x : X₁ ↦ f x) + Sum' (fun x : X₂ ↦ f x) := by
  sorry

end Chapter8

  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with

  | Icc a b => .Icc a b

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/

noncomputable section
open Classical

/-- Definition 6.1.1 (Distance).  Here we use the Mathlib distance. -/
-- #check Real.dist_eq

abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

namespace Chapter6

/--
  Definition 6.1.3 (Sequence). This is similar to the Chapter 5 sequence, except that now the
  sequence is real-valued. As with Chapter 5, we start sequences from 0 by default.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℝ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

/-- Functions from ℕ to ℝ can be thought of as sequences. -/
instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
abbrev Real.CloseSeq (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
theorem Real.closeSeq_def (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  ε.CloseSeq a L ↔ ∀ n ≥ a.m, dist (a n) L ≤ ε := by rfl

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
abbrev Real.EventuallyClose (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 -/
theorem Real.eventuallyClose_def (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) :
  ε.EventuallyClose a L ↔ ∃ N, (N ≥ a.m) ∧ ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L := by rfl

namespace Chapter6

abbrev Sequence.TendsTo (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

/-- Definition 6.1.8 -/
abbrev Sequence.Convergent (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∃ L, a.TendsTo L

/-- Definition 6.1.8.  We give the limit of a sequence the junk value of 0 if it is not convergent. -/
noncomputable abbrev lim (a:Sequence) : ℝ := if h: a.Convergent then h.choose else 0

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.7.2 (Exponentiation to a real exponent) -/
-- Source: Section_6_7.lean (Chapter6.Real.rpow)
noncomputable abbrev rpow (x : ℝ) (α : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  Chapter6.lim ((fun n ↦ x ^ ((eq_lim_of_rat α).choose n : ℝ)) : Chapter6.Sequence)

end Real

namespace Chapter11

/-- Bounded intervals on ℝ, as in Section 11.1 -/
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

/-- Coercion of a bounded interval to a set, as in Section 11.1 -/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

open Classical in
/-- DecidableEq instance to allow Finset BoundedInterval, as in Section 11.1 -/
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval :=
  instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

/-- Accessor for the left endpoint, as in Section 11.1 -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

/-- Accessor for the right endpoint, as in Section 11.1 -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

/-- Length of a bounded interval, as in Section 11.1 -/
abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

/-- Subset relation on bounded intervals, as in Section 11.1 -/
instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ (I:Set ℝ) → x ∈ (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Partitions of a bounded interval, as in Section 11.1 -/
@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ (J:Set ℝ)
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

/-- Membership of a subinterval in a partition, as in Section 11.1 -/
instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
/-- A canonical value for a constant function (noncomputable), Section 11.2 -/
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

/-- Constant-on a set, Section 11.2 -/
abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Canonical value of a function on a set, Section 11.2 -/
noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ :=
  constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions II) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I) -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

/-- Shorthand to view the integral given a witness of piecewise constancy, Section 11.2 -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ' {f:ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval}
  (_:PiecewiseConstantOn f I) := PiecewiseConstantOn.integ f I

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.6.1: boundedness on a set -/
abbrev BddOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M

end Chapter9

open BoundedInterval Chapter9

/-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

/-- Minorization of functions, Section 11.3 -/
abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

theorem MinorizesOn.iff (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  MinorizesOn g f I ↔ MajorizesOn f g I := by rfl

/-- Definition 11.3.2 (Upper and lower Riemann integrals) -/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Definition 11.3.4 (Riemann integral)
As we permit junk values, the simplest definition for the Riemann integral is the upper integral. -/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

/-- Integrability on a bounded interval, Section 11.3 -/
noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f (I:Set ℝ) ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

/-- Lemma 11.3.7 / Exercise 11.3.3 -/
theorem integ_of_piecewise_const {f:ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval}
  (hf: PiecewiseConstantOn f I) :
  IntegrableOn f I ∧ integ f I = hf.integ' := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

namespace Chapter11

  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

/-- There is a technical issue in that this coercion is not injective: the empty set is represented by multiple bounded intervals.  This causes some of the statements in this section to be a little uglier than necessary.-/
@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with

  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.6.1 -/
abbrev BddAboveOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, f x ≤ M

abbrev BddBelowOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, -M ≤ f x

namespace Chapter11
open BoundedInterval

/-! Section 11.2 essentials: Piecewise-constant functions and their integral -/

/-- Definition 11.2.1 -/
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

/-- Definition 11.2.3 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

/-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I) -/
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

/-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)-/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
/-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)  -/
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ' {f:ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval} (_:PiecewiseConstantOn f I) := integ f I

/-! Section 11.3 essentials: upper/lower integrals and IntegrableOn -/

open Chapter9

/-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions) -/
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

/-- Definition 11.3.2 (Uppper and lower Riemann integrals )-/
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

/-- Definition 11.3.4 (Riemann integral)
As we permit junk values, the simplest definition for the Riemann integral is the upper integral.-/
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

/-! Section 11.4: target theorem -/

/-- Theorem 11.4.1(b) / Exercise 11.4.1 -/
 theorem IntegrableOn.smul {I: BoundedInterval} (c:ℝ) {f:ℝ → ℝ} (hf: IntegrableOn f I) :
  IntegrableOn (c • f) I ∧ integ (c • f) I = c * integ f I := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

-- Section 4.3 essentials
 
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y : ℚ) := |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Section_4_3

abbrev dist (x y : ℚ) := |x - y|

theorem dist_eq (x y : ℚ) : dist x y = |x - y| := rfl

end Section_4_3

-- Section 5.1 essentials
namespace Chapter5

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
  vanish := by
    grind

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀ : ℤ) (a : { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n : ℕ) (a : ℕ → ℚ) : (a : Sequence) n = a n := by norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe_at_int (n : ℤ) (a : ℕ → ℚ) : (a : Sequence) n = if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0 := by
  norm_cast

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a : ℕ → ℚ) : (a : Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by norm_cast

abbrev Sequence.from (a : Sequence) (n₁ : ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n : ℤ))

end Chapter5

 
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε : ℚ) (a : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, Rat.Close ε (a n) (a m)

 
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε : ℚ) (a : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, Rat.Steady ε (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a : Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0 : ℚ), Rat.EventuallySteady ε a

-- Section 5.2 essentials
end Chapter5

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε : ℚ) (a b : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → Rat.Close ε (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε : ℚ) (a b : Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, Rat.CloseSeq ε (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0 : ℚ), Rat.EventuallyClose ε (a : Sequence) (b : Sequence)

-- Section 5.3 essentials
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a : ℕ → ℚ} (ha : (a : Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a : Sequence).seq
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    simpa using ((a : Sequence).vanish n hn)
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n : ℤ)

@[simp]
lemma CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a : CauchySequence) :
    ((a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  ext n; by_cases h : n ≥ 0 <;> simp_all
  rw [a.vanish]; rwa [a.zero]

instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv :=
    { refl := by sorry
      symm := by sorry
      trans := by sorry }

-- zero instance for CauchySequence
@[simp]
theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a : ℚ) : ((fun _ : ℕ ↦ a) : Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ : ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0 : ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a : Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0 : CauchySequence))

lemma LIM_def {a : ℕ → ℚ} (ha : (a : Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' ha) := by
  rw [LIM, dif_pos ha]

theorem Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b : ℕ → ℚ} (ha : (a : Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb : (b : Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  constructor
  · intro h; replace h := Quotient.exact h
    rwa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb] at h
  intro h; apply Quotient.sound; simpa [dif_pos ha, dif_pos hb]

theorem Sequence.IsCauchy.add {a b : ℕ → ℚ} (ha : (a : Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb : (b : Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    ((a + b : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

theorem Sequence.add_equiv {a b a' b' : ℕ → ℚ} (haa' : Sequence.Equiv a a')
  (hbb' : Sequence.Equiv b b') :
    Sequence.Equiv (a + b) (a' + b') := by sorry

noncomputable instance Real.add_inst : Add Real where
  add := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a + b))
      (by
        intro a b a' b' _ _
        change LIM ((a : ℕ → ℚ) + (b : ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a' : ℕ → ℚ) + (b' : ℕ → ℚ))
        rw [Real.LIM_eq_LIM]
        · solve_by_elim [Sequence.add_equiv]
        all_goals
          apply Sequence.IsCauchy.add <;> rw [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] <;> convert @CauchySequence.cauchy ?_)

noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b))
      (by
        intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
        change LIM ((a : ℕ → ℚ) * (b : ℕ → ℚ)) = LIM ((a' : ℕ → ℚ) * (b' : ℕ → ℚ))
        rw [Real.LIM_eq_LIM]
        · sorry
        all_goals
          sorry)

instance Real.instRatCast : RatCast Real where
  ratCast := fun q ↦ Quotient.mk _ (CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _ ↦ q) (by
    -- constant sequences are Cauchy
    simpa using (Sequence.IsCauchy.const q)))

-- OfNat/NatCast/IntCast instances so numerals and casts work
instance Real.instOfNat {n : ℕ} : OfNat Real n where ofNat := ((n : ℚ) : Real)

instance Real.instNatCast : NatCast Real where natCast n := ((n : ℚ) : Real)

instance Real.instIntCast : IntCast Real where intCast n := ((n : ℚ) : Real)

-- For subtraction and order we will need neg and add structures
noncomputable instance Real.instNeg : Neg Real where
  neg x := ((-1 : ℚ) : Real) * x

noncomputable instance Real.addGroup_inst : AddGroup Real :=
  AddGroup.ofLeftAxioms (by sorry) (by sorry) (by sorry)

-- Inversion and division to parse 1/(M+1) as a Real
abbrev BoundedAwayZero (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop := ∃ c : ℚ, c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, |a n| ≥ c

-- We only need the existence statement to define inv; we leave the proof as sorry.
theorem Real.boundedAwayZero_of_nonzero {x : Real} (hx : x ≠ 0) :
    ∃ a : ℕ → ℚ, (a : Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ BoundedAwayZero a ∧ x = LIM a := by
  sorry

open Classical in
noncomputable instance Real.instInv : Inv Real where
  inv x := if h : x ≠ 0 then LIM (Real.boundedAwayZero_of_nonzero h).choose⁻¹ else 0

-- Use HDiv for `/` notation on Real
noncomputable instance Real.instHDiv : HDiv Real Real Real where hDiv x y := x * y⁻¹

-- Section 5.4 essentials
abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ c : ℚ, c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ c : ℚ, c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x : Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a : ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a : Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x : Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a : ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a : Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev Real.abs (x : Real) : Real := if x.IsPos then x else (if x.IsNeg then -x else 0)

instance Real.instLT : LT Real where lt x y := (x - y).IsNeg

instance Real.instLE : LE Real where le x y := (x < y) ∨ (x = y)

noncomputable instance Real.instLinearOrder : LinearOrder Real where
  le_refl := by sorry
  le_trans := by sorry
  lt_iff_le_not_ge := by sorry
  le_antisymm := by sorry
  le_total := by sorry
  toDecidableLE := Classical.decRel _

-- Target theorem from Section 5.5
namespace Chapter5

theorem Real.LIM_of_Cauchy {q:ℕ → ℚ} (hq: ∀ M, ∀ n ≥ M, ∀ n' ≥ M, |q n - q n'| ≤ 1 / (M+1)) :
    (q:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ ∀ M, |q M - LIM q| ≤ 1 / (M+1) := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- This enables one to use `Set` and `Object` instead of `SetTheory.Set` and `SetTheory.Object`.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- This instance implicitly imposes the axioms of Zermelo-Frankel set theory with atoms.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

/-- Axiom 3.1 (Sets are objects)-/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := set_to_object X

/-- This defines the subtype `A.toSubtype` for any `A:Set`.-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

instance SetTheory.Set.instSingleton : Singleton Object Set where
  singleton := singleton

instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := union_pair

instance SetTheory.Set.instInsert : Insert Object Set where
  insert x X := {x} ∪ X

abbrev SetTheory.Set.replace (A:Set) {P: A → Object → Prop}
  (hP : ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set := SetTheory.replace A P hP

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

-- Below are some API for handling coercions. This may not be the optimal way to set things up.
instance SetTheory.Set.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Nat n where
  ofNat := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Set.instNatCast : NatCast Nat where
  natCast n := nat_equiv n

instance SetTheory.Object.instNatCast : NatCast Object where
  natCast n := (n:Nat).val

instance SetTheory.Object.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Object n where
  ofNat := ((n:Nat):Object)

instance SetTheory.Set.inst_pow : Pow Set Set where
  pow := pow

/-- Exercise 3.4.6 (i). One needs to provide a suitable definition of the power set here. -/
def SetTheory.Set.powerset (X:Set) : Set :=
  (({0,1} ^ X): Set).replace (P := sorry) (by sorry)

end Chapter3

/-- Definition 9.1.5.  Note that a slightly different `Real.adherent` was defined in Chapter 6.4 -/
abbrev Real.adherent' (ε:ℝ) (x:ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) := ∃ y ∈ X, |x - y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter9

/-- Definition 9.1.-/
abbrev AdherentPt (x:ℝ) (X:Set ℝ) := ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.adherent' x X

/-- Lemma 9.1.14 / Exercise 9.1.5 -/
theorem limit_of_AdherentPt (X: Set ℝ) (x:ℝ) :
  AdherentPt x X ↔ ∃ a : ℕ → ℝ, (∀ n, a n ∈ X) ∧ Filter.atTop.Tendsto a (nhds x) := by
    sorry

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Basic

open scoped BigOperators

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter11 Section 11.4 target theorem.
-/

namespace Chapter11

/-! Section 11.1 essentials: Bounded intervals, coercions, length, partitions -/

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b : ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b : ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b : ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b : ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I : BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I : Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  max (I.b - I.a) 0

/-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override || -/
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term =>
  `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I : BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x : ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I : BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

/-! Section 11.2 essentials: Constant/ConstantOn and piecewise-constant integrals -/

abbrev Constant {X Y : Type} (f : X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y : Type} [hY : Nonempty Y] (f : X → Y) : Y :=
  if h : Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop :=
  Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : ℝ :=
  constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) : Prop :=
  ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J : Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f : ℝ → ℝ) {I : BoundedInterval} (P : Partition I) : ℝ :=
  ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J : Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  if h : PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

/-! Section 9.6 essential: boundedness on sets -/
namespace Chapter9
abbrev BddOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (X : Set ℝ) : Prop := ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ X, |f x| ≤ M
end Chapter9

/-! Section 11.3 essentials: order relations, upper/lower integrals and IntegrableOn -/

abbrev MajorizesOn (g f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I : Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x
abbrev MinorizesOn (g f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I : Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

open Chapter9

noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev integ (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  upper_integral f I

noncomputable abbrev IntegrableOn (f : ℝ → ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) : Prop :=
  BddOn f I ∧ lower_integral f I = upper_integral f I

/-- Theorem 11.4.1(f) / Exercise 11.4.1 -/ 
lemma IntegrableOn.const (c : ℝ) (I : BoundedInterval) :
  IntegrableOn (fun _ ↦ c) I ∧ integ (fun _ ↦ c) I = c * |I|ₗ := by
  sorry

/-- Theorem 11.4.1(f) / Exercise 11.4.1 -/ 
theorem IntegrableOn.const' {I: BoundedInterval} {f:ℝ → ℝ} (hf: ConstantOn f I) :
  IntegrableOn f I ∧ integ f I = (constant_value_on f I) * |I|ₗ := by
  sorry

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

/-
Minimal extraction from Sections 4.3 and 5.1 needed to state Exercise 6.1.10.
-/

/-- From Section 4.3: definition of ε-closeness on ℚ. -/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

/-- From Section 5.1: definition of sequences of rationals -/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/-- Auxiliary constructor with specified starting point (as in Section 5.1). -/
abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/-- Start a sequence from a later index (as in Section 5.1). -/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {n₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ n₁) :
  (a.from n₁) n = a n := by simp [hn]; intro h; exact (a.vanish _ h).symm

end Chapter5

/-- From Section 5.1: ε-steady over ℚ. -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- From Section 5.1: eventually ε-steady over ℚ. -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

namespace Chapter5

/-- From Section 5.1: Cauchy sequences over ℚ. -/
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

end Chapter5

namespace Chapter6

/-- Exercise 6.1.10 (Section 6.1) - target theorem. -/
theorem Chapter5.Sequence.IsCauchy_iff (a:Chapter5.Sequence) :
    a.IsCauchy ↔ ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ∀ n ≥ N, ∀ m ≥ N, |a n - a m| ≤ ε := by
  sorry

end Chapter6

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 3 dependencies needed to typecheck the target theorem
from Section 3.6.
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms.  -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Definition 3.1.1 (objects can be elements of sets) -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := mem x X

instance SetTheory.Set.instEmpty : EmptyCollection Set where
  emptyCollection := emptyset

abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- Axiom 3.8 (Axiom of infinity) -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := nat_equiv.symm n

/-- Definition 3.6.1 (Equal cardinality) -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.EqualCard (X Y:Set) : Prop := ∃ f : X → Y, Function.Bijective f

@[refl]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.refl (X:Set) : EqualCard X X := by
  sorry

@[symm]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.symm {X Y:Set} (h: EqualCard X Y) : EqualCard Y X := by
  sorry

@[trans]
theorem SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.trans {X Y Z:Set} (h1: EqualCard X Y) (h2: EqualCard Y Z) : EqualCard X Z := by
  sorry

/-- Proposition 3.6.4 / Exercise 3.6.1 -/
instance SetTheory.Set.EqualCard.inst_setoid : Setoid SetTheory.Set := ⟨ EqualCard, {refl, symm, trans} ⟩

/-- A `Fin`-like set inside the `SetTheory` universe. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n:ℕ) : Set := nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m : ℕ) < n)

/-- Definition 3.6.5 -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.has_card (X:Set) (n:ℕ) : Prop := X ≈ Fin n

abbrev SetTheory.Set.finite (X:Set) : Prop := ∃ n:ℕ, X.has_card n

open Classical in
/-- It is convenient for Lean purposes to give infinite sets the ``junk`` cardinality of zero. -/
noncomputable def SetTheory.Set.card (X:Set) : ℕ := if h:X.finite then h.choose else 0

/-- Exercise 3.6.2 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.empty_iff_card_eq_zero {X:Set} : X = ∅ ↔ X.finite ∧ X.card = 0 := by
  sorry

end Chapter3

namespace Chapter9

/-- Exercise 9.9.6 -/
theorem UniformContinuousOn.comp {X Y: Set ℝ} {f g:ℝ → ℝ}
  (hf: UniformContinuousOn f X) (hg: UniformContinuousOn g Y)
  (hrange: f '' X ⊆ Y) : UniformContinuousOn (g ∘ f) X := by

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

/-- Definition 6.1.1 (Distance-based closeness). -/
abbrev Real.Close (ε x y : ℝ) : Prop := dist x y ≤ ε

/-- Definition 6.1.2 (ε-close). -/
lemma Real.close_def (ε x y : ℝ) : ε.Close x y ↔ dist x y ≤ ε := by rfl

namespace Chapter6

@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  m : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℝ
  vanish : ∀ n < m, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℝ) where
  coe a := a.seq

@[coe]
abbrev Sequence.ofNatFun (a:ℕ → ℝ) : Sequence :=
 {
    m := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by simp_all
 }

instance Sequence.instCoe : Coe (ℕ → ℝ) Sequence where
  coe := ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (m:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) : Sequence where
  m := m
  seq n := if h : n ≥ m then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by simp_all

lemma Sequence.eval_mk {n m:ℤ} (a: { n // n ≥ m } → ℝ) (h: n ≥ m) :
    (Sequence.mk' m a) n = a ⟨ n, h ⟩ := by simp [h]

@[simp]
lemma Sequence.eval_coe (n:ℕ) (a: ℕ → ℝ) : (a:Sequence) n = a n := by simp

abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (m₁:ℤ) : Sequence := mk' (max a.m m₁) (a ↑·)

lemma Sequence.from_eval (a:Sequence) {m₁ n:ℤ} (hn: n ≥ m₁) :
  (a.from m₁) n = a n := by
  simp [hn]; intros; symm; solve_by_elim [a.vanish]

end Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.3 (ε-steady) -/
abbrev Real.Steady (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.m, ∀ m ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 6.1.3 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Real.EventuallySteady (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.Steady (a.from N)

/-- Definition 6.1.5 (ε-close to a limit) -/
abbrev Real.CloseSeq (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop := ∀ n ≥ a.m, ε.Close (a n) L

/-- Definition 6.1.5 (Eventually ε-close) -/
abbrev Real.EventuallyClose (ε: ℝ) (a: Chapter6.Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∃ N ≥ a.m, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) L

namespace Chapter6

/-- Definition 6.1.8 (Tends to a limit) -/
abbrev Sequence.TendsTo (a:Sequence) (L:ℝ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℝ), ε.EventuallyClose a L

/-- Definition 6.1.8 (Convergent sequence) -/
abbrev Sequence.Convergent (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∃ L, a.TendsTo L

open Classical in
/-- Definition 6.1.8 (limit, junk value 0 if not convergent) -/
noncomputable abbrev lim (a:Sequence) : ℝ := if h: a.Convergent then h.choose else 0

namespace Real

/-- From Section 6.7: existence of a rational approximating sequence for any real. -/
-- This matches the declaration in the textbook (Chapter6.Real.eq_lim_of_rat).
theorem eq_lim_of_rat (α : ℝ) : ∃ q : ℕ → ℚ, ((fun n ↦ (q n : ℝ)) : Sequence).TendsTo α := by
  sorry

/-- From Section 6.7: definition of real exponentiation via limits of rational powers. -/
noncomputable abbrev rpow (x : ℝ) (α : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  lim ((fun n ↦ x ^ ((eq_lim_of_rat α).choose n : ℝ)) : Sequence)

end Real

/-- Proposition 6.7.3(e) / Exercise 6.7.1 -/
 theorem Real.ratPow_mono_of_lt_one {x:ℝ} (hx0: 0 < x) (hx: x < 1) {q r:ℝ} :
    rpow x q > rpow x r ↔ q < r := by
  sorry

end Chapter6

/-!
Self-contained slice to typecheck the target theorem from Section 3.5.

We include only the minimal scaffolding needed so the theorem parses and typechecks:
- the SetTheory class (from Section 3.1) to provide `Set` and `Object`,
- the `∪` notation on `Set` (via `Union` instance),
- a lightweight auxiliary definition/notation for `×ˢ` sufficient for parsing.

The target theorem itself ends with `by sorry`, as required.
-/

namespace Chapter3

/- The ability to work in multiple universes becomes relevant later in the chapter. -/
universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms (Section 3.1). -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u -- Axiom 3.1
  Object : Type v -- Axiom 3.1
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.1
  mem : Object → Set → Prop -- Axiom 3.1
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y -- Axiom 3.2
  emptyset: Set -- Axiom 3.3
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset -- Axiom 3.3
  singleton : Object → Set -- Axiom 3.4
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y -- Axiom 3.4
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.5
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y) -- Axiom 3.5
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set -- Axiom 3.6
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x -- Axiom 3.6
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set -- Axiom 3.7
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y -- Axiom 3.7
  nat : Set -- Axiom 3.8
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat) -- Axiom 3.8
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S -- Axiom 3.9
  pow : Set → Set → Set -- Axiom 3.11
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object -- Axiom 3.11
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F -- Axiom 3.11
  union : Set → Set -- Axiom 3.12
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A -- Axiom 3.12

-- Make `Set` and `Object` directly available as names in this namespace.
export SetTheory (Set Object)

-- Impose the axioms of set theory via a typeclass parameter.
variable [SetTheory]

/-- Sets are objects (Axiom 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Pairwise union notation `∪` for Chapter 3 sets (Section 3.1). -/
instance SetTheory.Set.instUnion : Union Set where
  union := SetTheory.union_pair

/-
Auxiliary product notation.
We introduce an auxiliary operation and bind `×ˢ` to it so that the target theorem statement
parses. This is solely for notational purposes in this isolated slice.
-/
def Aux_sprod (A B : Set) : Set := A  -- any total function `Set → Set → Set` suffices for parsing

infixl:70 " ×ˢ " => Chapter3.Aux_sprod

/-- Exercise 3.5.4 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.prod_union (A B C:Set) :
    A ×ˢ (B ∪ C) = (A ×ˢ B) ∪ (A ×ˢ C) := by
  sorry

end Chapter3
