import Mathlib.Tactic

/-!
Self-contained snippet extracting minimal dependencies for Section 5.2 target theorem.
We avoid importing any Analysis.Section_* modules.
-/

namespace Chapter5

/--
  Definition 5.1.1 (Sequence). To avoid some technicalities involving dependent types, we extend
  sequences by zero to the left of the starting point `n₀`.
-/
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

/-- Sequences can be thought of as functions from ℤ to ℚ. -/
instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

/--
Functions from ℕ to ℚ can be thought of as sequences starting from 0; `ofNatFun` performs this conversion.

The `coe` attribute allows the delaborator to print `Sequence.ofNatFun f` as `↑f`, which is more concise; you may safely remove this if you prefer the more explicit notation.
-/
@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by grind

/-- If `a : ℕ → ℚ` is used in a context where a `Sequence` is expected, automatically coerce `a` to `Sequence.ofNatFun a`. -/
instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by grind

/--
  a.from n₁ starts `a:Sequence` from `n₁`.  It is intended for use when `n₁ ≥ n₀`, but returns
  the "junk" value of the original sequence `a` otherwise.
-/
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

end Chapter5

/--
  This definition needs to be made outside of the Section 4.3 namespace for technical reasons.
-/
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

/-- A slight generalization of Definition 5.1.3 - definition of ε-steadiness for a sequence with an
arbitrary starting point n₀ -/
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, ε.Close (a n) (a m)

/-- Definition 5.1.6 (Eventually ε-steady) -/
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, ε.Steady (a.from N)

/-! Section 5.2 content needed for the target theorem -/

abbrev Rat.CloseSeq (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n, n ≥ a.n₀ → n ≥ b.n₀ → ε.Close (a n) (b n)

abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ε.CloseSeq (a.from N) (b.from N)

namespace Chapter5

abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallySteady a

/-- Definition 5.2.6 (Equivalent sequences) -/
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), ε.EventuallyClose (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence)

/-- Exercise 5.2.1 -/
theorem Sequence.isCauchy_of_equiv {a b: ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Equiv a b) :
    (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ↔ (b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Order
import Mathlib.Topology.Instances.Real
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

-- Self-contained slice for Chapter 11.8 target theorem.

namespace Chapter11

-- Bounded intervals as in the textbook (Section 11.1).
inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := BoundedInterval.toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

-- `a` and `b` endpoints of a bounded interval (Section 11.1).
abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

-- Partitions of a bounded interval (Section 11.1).
@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J: BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

-- Constant functions and related API (Section 11.2).
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

-- Left and right limits (Section 11.8).
noncomputable abbrev right_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ := lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Ioi x₀)).map f)

noncomputable abbrev left_lim (f: ℝ → ℝ) (x₀:ℝ) : ℝ := lim ((nhdsWithin x₀ (.Iio x₀)).map f)

-- α-length (Definition 11.8.1).
noncomputable abbrev α_length (α: ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
| Icc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| Ico a b => if a ≤ b then (left_lim α b) - (left_lim α a) else 0
| Ioc a b => if a ≤ b then (right_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0
| Ioo a b => if a < b then (left_lim α b) - (right_lim α a) else 0

notation3:max α"["I"]ₗ" => α_length α I

-- Piecewise constant functions with respect to a partition / on an interval (Section 11.2).
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

-- Piecewise constant RS integral with a fixed partition (Definition 11.8.5).
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) (α: ℝ → ℝ)   :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * α[J]ₗ

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.RS_integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) (α:ℝ → ℝ):
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.RS_integ f h.choose α else 0

namespace PiecewiseConstantOn

-- Theorem 11.8.8 (f) (Laws of RS integration) / Exercise 11.8.8.
-- Target theorem
 theorem RS_integ_const' {f:ℝ → ℝ} {I: BoundedInterval}
  {α:ℝ → ℝ} (hα: Monotone α) (h: ConstantOn f I) :
  RS_integ f I α = (constant_value_on f I) * α[I]ₗ := by
  sorry

end PiecewiseConstantOn

end Chapter11

import Mathlib.Tactic

/-!
Self-contained slice for Chapter 3, Section 3.5 target theorem.
We include only the necessary core of Section 3.1 to support sets/objects,
then the specific declarations from Section 3.5 used by the target theorem.
-/

namespace Chapter3

universe u v

/-- The axioms of Zermelo-Frankel theory with atoms (core fields used here). -/
class SetTheory where
  Set : Type u
  Object : Type v
  set_to_object : Set ↪ Object
  mem : Object → Set → Prop
  extensionality X Y : (∀ x, mem x X ↔ mem x Y) → X = Y
  emptyset: Set
  emptyset_mem x : ¬ mem x emptyset
  singleton : Object → Set
  singleton_axiom x y : mem x (singleton y) ↔ x = y
  union_pair : Set → Set → Set
  union_pair_axiom X Y x : mem x (union_pair X Y) ↔ (mem x X ∨ mem x Y)
  specify A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) : Set
  specification_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Prop) :
    (∀ x, mem x (specify A P) → mem x A) ∧ ∀ x, mem x.val (specify A P) ↔ P x
  replace A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : Set
  replacement_axiom A (P: Subtype (mem . A) → Object → Prop)
    (hP: ∀ x y y', P x y ∧ P x y' → y = y') : ∀ y, mem y (replace A P hP) ↔ ∃ x, P x y
  nat : Set
  nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Subtype (mem . nat)
  regularity_axiom A (hA : ∃ x, mem x A) :
    ∃ x, mem x A ∧ ∀ S, x = set_to_object S → ¬ ∃ y, mem y A ∧ mem y S
  pow : Set → Set → Set
  function_to_object (X: Set) (Y: Set) :
    (Subtype (mem . X) → Subtype (mem . Y)) ↪ Object
  powerset_axiom (X: Set) (Y: Set) (F:Object) :
    mem F (pow X Y) ↔ ∃ f: Subtype (mem . Y) → Subtype (mem . X),
    function_to_object Y X f = F
  union : Set → Set
  union_axiom A x : mem x (union A) ↔ ∃ S, mem x S ∧ mem (set_to_object S) A

export SetTheory (Set Object nat)

variable [SetTheory]

/-- Objects can be elements of sets. -/
instance SetTheory.objects_mem_sets : Membership Object Set where
  mem X x := SetTheory.mem x X

/-- Coerce a set to an object. -/
instance SetTheory.sets_are_objects : Coe Set Object where
  coe X := SetTheory.set_to_object X

/-- Equality of sets via extensionality. -/
@[ext]
 theorem SetTheory.Set.ext {X Y:Set} (h: ∀ x, x ∈ X ↔ x ∈ Y) : X = Y :=
  SetTheory.extensionality _ _ h

/-- Treat a set as a type via its subtype of elements. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.toSubtype (A:Set) := Subtype (fun x ↦ x ∈ A)

instance : CoeSort (Set) (Type v) where
  coe A := A.toSubtype

/-- Axiom 3.6 (axiom of specification), packaged. -/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.specify (A:Set) (P: A → Prop) : Set := SetTheory.specify A P

/-- The set of natural numbers as a set. -/
abbrev Nat := SetTheory.nat

/-- A bijection between ℕ and the set of naturals (as elements). -/
def SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv : ℕ ≃ Nat := SetTheory.nat_equiv

instance SetTheory.Set.toNat : Coe Nat ℕ where
  coe n := (SetTheory.Set.nat_equiv).symm n

/--
  Here we set up some an analogue of Mathlib `Fin n` types within the Chapter 3 Set Theory,
  with rudimentary API.
-/
abbrev SetTheory.Set.Fin (n:ℕ) : Set := nat.specify (fun m ↦ (m:ℕ) < n)

/-- An alternate definition of a tuple, used in Exercise 3.5.2 -/
structure SetTheory.Set.Tuple (n:ℕ) where
  X: Set
  x: Fin n → X
  surj: Function.Surjective x

/--
  Custom extensionality lemma for Exercise 3.5.2.
  Placing `@[ext]` on the structure would generate a lemma requiring proof of `t.x = t'.x`,
  but these functions have different types when `t.X ≠ t'.X`. This lemma handles that part.
-/
@[ext]
lemma SetTheory.Set.Tuple.ext {n:ℕ} {t t':Tuple n}
    (hX : t.X = t'.X)
    (hx : ∀ n : Fin n, ((t.x n):Object) = ((t'.x n):Object)) :
    t = t' := by
  sorry

/-- Exercise 3.5.2 -/
theorem SetTheory.Set.Tuple.eq {n:ℕ} (t t':Tuple n) :
    t = t' ↔ ∀ n : Fin n, ((t.x n):Object) = ((t'.x n):Object) := by sorry

end Chapter3

import Mathlib.Tactic

-- eps-closeness on rationals (from Section 4.3)
def Rat.Close (ε : ℚ) (x y:ℚ) := |x-y| ≤ ε

namespace Chapter5

-- Section 5.1: Sequence definition
@[ext]
structure Sequence where
  n₀ : ℤ
  seq : ℤ → ℚ
  vanish : ∀ n < n₀, seq n = 0

instance Sequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun Sequence (fun _ ↦ ℤ → ℚ) where
  coe := fun a ↦ a.seq

@[coe]
def Sequence.ofNatFun (a : ℕ → ℚ) : Sequence where
    n₀ := 0
    seq n := if n ≥ 0 then a n.toNat else 0
    vanish := by
      intro n hn
      have : ¬ n ≥ 0 := by exact Int.not_le.mpr hn
      simp [this]

instance : Coe (ℕ → ℚ) Sequence where
  coe := Sequence.ofNatFun

abbrev Sequence.mk' (n₀:ℤ) (a: { n // n ≥ n₀ } → ℚ) : Sequence where
  n₀ := n₀
  seq n := if h : n ≥ n₀ then a ⟨n, h⟩ else 0
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    have : ¬ n ≥ n₀ := by exact Int.not_le.mpr hn
    simp [this]

lemma Sequence.n0_coe (a: ℕ → ℚ) : (a:Sequence).n₀ = 0 := by rfl

-- ε-steadiness
abbrev Rat.Steady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∀ n ≥ a.n₀, ∀ m ≥ a.n₀, Rat.Close ε (a n) (a m)

-- start sequence from index n₁
abbrev Sequence.from (a:Sequence) (n₁:ℤ) : Sequence :=
  mk' (max a.n₀ n₁) (fun n ↦ a (n:ℤ))

-- eventually ε-steady
abbrev Rat.EventuallySteady (ε: ℚ) (a: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop := ∃ N ≥ a.n₀, Rat.Steady ε (a.from N)

-- From Section 5.2: eventually-close sequences (placed early to be available)
abbrev Rat.EventuallyClose (ε: ℚ) (a b: Chapter5.Sequence) : Prop :=
  ∃ N, ∀ n ≥ N, |a n - b n| ≤ ε

-- Cauchy sequences (Section 5.1)
abbrev Sequence.IsCauchy (a:Sequence) : Prop := ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), Rat.EventuallySteady ε a

-- Section 5.3: CauchySequence class and Real construction
@[ext]
class CauchySequence extends Sequence where
  zero : n₀ = 0
  cauchy : toSequence.IsCauchy

theorem CauchySequence.ext' {a b: CauchySequence} (h: a.seq = b.seq) : a = b := by
  apply CauchySequence.ext _ h
  rw [a.zero, b.zero]

-- A sequence starting at 0 that is Cauchy gives a CauchySequence.
abbrev CauchySequence.mk' {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : CauchySequence where
  n₀ := 0
  seq := (a:Sequence).seq
  vanish := by
    intro n hn
    -- use the vanish property of (a:Sequence)
    simpa using ((a:Sequence).vanish n hn)
  zero := rfl
  cauchy := ha

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_eq {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (mk' ha).toSequence = (a:Sequence) := rfl

instance CauchySequence.instCoeFun : CoeFun CauchySequence (fun _ ↦ ℕ → ℚ) where
  coe a n := a.toSequence (n:ℤ)

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence (a: CauchySequence) :
    ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence) = a.toSequence := by
  apply Sequence.ext (by simp [Sequence.n0_coe, a.zero])
  funext n; by_cases h:n ≥ 0
  · simp [Sequence.ofNatFun, h]
  · have hn : n < 0 := lt_of_not_ge h
    have hna : n < a.n₀ := by simpa [a.zero] using hn
    have hv : a.toSequence n = 0 := by simpa using (a.vanish n hna)
    simp [Sequence.ofNatFun, h, hv]

@[simp]
theorem CauchySequence.coe_coe {a:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) : mk' ha = a := by rfl

-- helper: coe CauchySequence to Sequence is Cauchy
lemma CauchySequence.cauchy_coe (a : CauchySequence) : ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  simpa [CauchySequence.coe_to_sequence] using a.cauchy

-- From Section 5.2: equivalence of sequences
abbrev Sequence.Equiv (a b: ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ε > (0:ℚ), (Rat.EventuallyClose ε (a:Sequence) (b:Sequence))

-- Transitivity of sequence equivalence (from 5.3, proof omitted).
lemma Sequence.equiv_trans {a b c:ℕ → ℚ} (hab: Sequence.Equiv a b) (hbc: Sequence.Equiv b c) :
  Sequence.Equiv a c := by
  sorry

-- Setoid of Cauchy sequences (proofs omitted, as in source).
instance CauchySequence.instSetoid : Setoid CauchySequence where
  r := fun a b ↦ Sequence.Equiv a b
  iseqv :=
    { refl := by sorry
      symm := by sorry
      trans := by sorry }

-- Every constant sequence is Cauchy (from 5.3).
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.const (a:ℚ) : ((fun _:ℕ ↦ a):Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

instance CauchySequence.instZero : Zero CauchySequence where
  zero := CauchySequence.mk' (a := fun _: ℕ ↦ 0) (Sequence.IsCauchy.const (0:ℚ))

abbrev Real := Quotient CauchySequence.instSetoid

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev LIM (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Real :=
  Quotient.mk _ (if h : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy then CauchySequence.mk' h else (0:CauchySequence))

-- Equality of reals via equality of limits (from 5.3).
lemma Real.LIM_eq_LIM {a b:ℕ → ℚ} (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
  LIM a = LIM b ↔ Sequence.Equiv a b := by
  sorry

-- Product of Cauchy sequences is Cauchy (from 5.3, proof omitted).
lemma Sequence.IsCauchy.mul {a b:ℕ → ℚ}  (ha: (a:Sequence).IsCauchy) (hb: (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) :
    (a * b:Sequence).IsCauchy := by
  sorry

-- Product respects sequence equivalence (left) (from 5.3, proof omitted).
lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_left {a a':ℕ → ℚ} (b:ℕ → ℚ) (hb : (b:Sequence).IsCauchy) (haa': Sequence.Equiv a a') :
  Sequence.Equiv (a * b) (a' * b) := by
  sorry

-- Product respects sequence equivalence (right) (from 5.3).
lemma Sequence.mul_equiv_right {b b':ℕ → ℚ} (a:ℕ → ℚ)  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)  (hbb': Sequence.Equiv b b') :
  Sequence.Equiv (a * b) (a * b') := by
  simpa [mul_comm] using (Sequence.mul_equiv_left a ha hbb')

-- Product respects sequence equivalence (both sides) (from 5.3).
lemma Sequence.mul_equiv
  {a b a' b':ℕ → ℚ}
  (ha : (a:Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (hb' : (b':Sequence).IsCauchy)
  (haa': Sequence.Equiv a a')
  (hbb': Sequence.Equiv b b') : Sequence.Equiv (a * b) (a' * b') :=
    Sequence.equiv_trans (mul_equiv_right _ ha hbb') (mul_equiv_left _ hb' haa')

-- Product of reals
noncomputable instance Real.mul_inst : Mul Real where
  mul := fun x y ↦
    Quotient.liftOn₂ x y (fun a b ↦ LIM (a * b)) (by
      intro a b a' b' haa' hbb'
      -- Cauchy hypotheses for all pieces
      have haC : ((a:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence).IsCauchy := CauchySequence.cauchy_coe a
      have hbC : ((b:ℕ → ℚ):Sequence).IsCauchy := CauchySequence.cauchy_coe b
      have haC' : ((a':ℕ → ℚ):Sequence).IsCauchy := CauchySequence.cauchy_coe a'
      have hbC' : ((b':ℕ → ℚ):Sequence).IsCauchy := CauchySequence.cauchy_coe b'
      -- equivalence of products
      have hEquiv : Sequence.Equiv ((a:ℕ → ℚ) * (b:ℕ → ℚ)) ((a':ℕ → ℚ) * (b':ℕ → ℚ)) :=
        Sequence.mul_equiv haC hbC' haa' hbb'
      -- conclude equality of limits
      exact (Real.LIM_eq_LIM (Sequence.IsCauchy.mul haC hbC) (Sequence.IsCauchy.mul haC' hbC')).2 hEquiv
      )

-- Section 5.4: positivity on reals via Cauchy sequences
abbrev BoundedAwayPos (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≥ c

abbrev BoundedAwayNeg (a:ℕ → ℚ) : Prop :=
  ∃ (c:ℚ), c > 0 ∧ ∀ n, a n ≤ -c

abbrev Real.IsPos (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayPos a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

abbrev Real.IsNeg (x:Real) : Prop :=
  ∃ a:ℕ → ℚ, BoundedAwayNeg a ∧ (a:Sequence).IsCauchy ∧ x = LIM a

-- TARGET THEOREM
 theorem Real.pos_mul {x y:Real} (hx: x.IsPos) (hy: y.IsPos) : (x*y).IsPos := by
  sorry

end Chapter5

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms

namespace Section_4_1

structure PreInt where
  minuend : ℕ
  subtrahend : ℕ

/-- Definition 4.1.1 -/
instance PreInt.instSetoid : Setoid PreInt where
  r a b := a.minuend + b.subtrahend = b.minuend + a.subtrahend
  iseqv := {
    refl := by sorry
    symm := by sorry
    trans := by
      -- This proof is written to follow the structure of the original text.
      intro ⟨ a,b ⟩ ⟨ c,d ⟩ ⟨ e,f ⟩ h1 h2; simp_all
      have h3 := congrArg₂ (· + ·) h1 h2; simp at h3
      have : (a + f) + (c + d) = (e + b) + (c + d) := calc
        (a + f) + (c + d) = a + d + (c + f) := by abel
        _ = c + b + (e + d) := h3
        _ = (e + b) + (c + d) := by abel
      exact Nat.add_right_cancel this
    }

@[simp]
theorem PreInt.eq (a b c d:ℕ) : (⟨ a,b ⟩: PreInt) ≈ ⟨ c,d ⟩ ↔ a + d = c + b := by rfl

abbrev Int := Quotient PreInt.instSetoid

abbrev Int.formalDiff (a b:ℕ)  : Int := Quotient.mk PreInt.instSetoid ⟨ a,b ⟩

infix:100 " —— " => Int.formalDiff

/-- Definition 4.1.1 (Integers) -/
theorem Int.eq (a b c d:ℕ): a —— b = c —— d ↔ a + d = c + b :=
  ⟨ Quotient.exact, by intro h; exact Quotient.sound h ⟩

/-- Lemma 4.1.3 (Multiplication well-defined) -/
theorem Int.mul_congr_left (a b a' b' c d : ℕ) (h: a —— b = a' —— b') :
    (a*c+b*d) —— (a*d+b*c) = (a'*c+b'*d) —— (a'*d+b'*c) := by
  simp only [eq] at *
  calc
    _ = c*(a+b') + d*(a'+b) := by ring
    _ = c*(a'+b) + d*(a+b') := by rw [h]
    _ = _ := by ring

/-- Lemma 4.1.3 (Multiplication well-defined) -/
theorem Int.mul_congr_right (a b c d c' d' : ℕ) (h: c —— d = c' —— d') :
    (a*c+b*d) —— (a*d+b*c) = (a*c'+b*d') —— (a*d'+b*c') := by
  simp only [eq] at *
  calc
    _ = a*(c+d') + b*(c'+d) := by ring
    _ = a*(c'+d) + b*(c+d') := by rw [h]
    _ = _ := by ring

/-- Lemma 4.1.3 (Multiplication well-defined) -/
theorem Int.mul_congr {a b c d a' b' c' d' : ℕ} (h1: a —— b = a' —— b') (h2: c —— d = c' —— d') :
  (a*c+b*d) —— (a*d+b*c) = (a'*c'+b'*d') —— (a'*d'+b'*c') := by
  rw [mul_congr_left a b a' b' c d h1, mul_congr_right a' b' c d c' d' h2]

instance Int.instMul : Mul Int where
  mul := Quotient.lift₂ (fun ⟨ a, b ⟩ ⟨ c, d ⟩ ↦ (a * c + b * d) —— (a * d + b * c)) (by
    intro ⟨ a, b ⟩ ⟨ c, d ⟩ ⟨ a', b' ⟩ ⟨ c', d' ⟩ h1 h2
    exact mul_congr (Quotient.eq.mpr h1) (Quotient.eq.mpr h2)
    )

/-- Definition 4.1.2 (Multiplication of integers) -/
theorem Int.mul_eq (a b c d:ℕ) : a —— b * c —— d = (a*c+b*d) —— (a*d+b*c) := Quotient.lift₂_mk _ _ _ _

instance Int.instOfNat {n:ℕ} : OfNat Int n where
  ofNat := n —— 0

/-- Proposition 4.1.6 (laws of algebra) / Exercise 4.1.4 -/
instance Int.instCommMonoid : CommMonoid Int where
  mul_comm := by sorry
  mul_assoc := by sorry
  one_mul := by sorry
  mul_one := by sorry

end Section_4_1

import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Basic
import Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.Ring
import Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.InfiniteSum

namespace Chapter11

inductive BoundedInterval where
  | Ioo (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Icc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ioc (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval
  | Ico (a b:ℝ) : BoundedInterval

open BoundedInterval

@[coe]
def BoundedInterval.toSet (I: BoundedInterval) : Set ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a b => .Ioo a b
  | Icc a b => .Icc a b
  | Ioc a b => .Ioc a b
  | Ico a b => .Ico a b

instance BoundedInterval.inst_coeSet : Coe BoundedInterval (Set ℝ) where
  coe := toSet

instance BoundedInterval.instEmpty : EmptyCollection BoundedInterval where
  emptyCollection := Ioo 0 0

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.coe_empty : ((∅ : BoundedInterval):Set ℝ) = ∅ := by
  simp [toSet]

open Classical in
noncomputable instance BoundedInterval.decidableEq : DecidableEq BoundedInterval := instDecidableEqOfLawfulBEq

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioo (a b:ℝ) : (Ioo a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioo a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Icc (a b:ℝ) : (Icc a b : Set ℝ) = .Icc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ioc (a b:ℝ) : (Ioc a b : Set ℝ) = .Ioc a b := by rfl

@[simp]
theorem BoundedInterval.set_Ico (a b:ℝ) : (Ico a b : Set ℝ) = .Ico a b := by rfl

instance BoundedInterval.instMembership : Membership ℝ BoundedInterval where
  mem I x := x ∈ (I:Set ℝ)

instance BoundedInterval.instSubset : HasSubset BoundedInterval where
  Subset I J := ∀ x, x ∈ I → x ∈ J

abbrev BoundedInterval.a (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo a _ => a
  | Icc a _ => a
  | Ioc a _ => a
  | Ico a _ => a

abbrev BoundedInterval.b (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := match I with
  | Ioo _ b => b
  | Icc _ b => b
  | Ioc _ b => b
  | Ico _ b => b

abbrev BoundedInterval.length (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := max (I.b - I.a) 0

-- Using ||ₗ subscript here to not override ||
macro:max atomic("|" noWs) a:term noWs "|ₗ" : term => `(BoundedInterval.length $a)

@[ext]
structure Partition (I: BoundedInterval) where
  intervals : Finset BoundedInterval
  exists_unique (x:ℝ) (hx : x ∈ I) : ∃! J, J ∈ intervals ∧ x ∈ J
  contains (J : BoundedInterval) (hJ : J ∈ intervals) : J ⊆ I

instance Partition.instMembership (I: BoundedInterval) : Membership BoundedInterval (Partition I) where
  mem P J := J ∈ P.intervals

-- Section 11.2: piecewise constant functions (only the bits needed for the integral definition).
-- Definition 11.2.1
abbrev Constant {X Y:Type} (f: X → Y) : Prop := ∃ c, ∀ x, f x = c

open Classical in
noncomputable abbrev constant_value {X Y:Type} [hY: Nonempty Y] (f:X → Y) : Y :=
  if h: Constant f then h.choose else hY.some

abbrev ConstantOn (f: ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : Prop := Constant (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

noncomputable abbrev constant_value_on (f:ℝ → ℝ) (X: Set ℝ) : ℝ := constant_value (fun x : X ↦ f ↑x)

-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I)
abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I) : Prop := ∀ J ∈ P, ConstantOn f (J:Set ℝ)

-- Definition 11.2.5 (Piecewise constant functions I)
abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∃ P : Partition I, PiecewiseConstantWith f P

-- Definition 11.2.9 (Piecewise constant integral I)
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantWith.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) {I: BoundedInterval} (P: Partition I)  :
  ℝ := ∑ J ∈ P.intervals, constant_value_on f (J:Set ℝ) * |J|ₗ

open Classical
-- Definition 11.2.14 (Piecewise constant integral II)
noncomputable abbrev PiecewiseConstantOn.integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) :
  ℝ := if h: PiecewiseConstantOn f I then PiecewiseConstantWith.integ f h.choose else 0

-- Section 11.3: (upper/lower) Riemann integrals (only the bits needed).
-- Definition 11.3.1 (Majorization of functions)
abbrev MajorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), f x ≤ g x

abbrev MinorizesOn (g f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : Prop := ∀ x ∈ (I:Set ℝ), g x ≤ f x

-- Definition 11.3.2 (Upper and lower Riemann integrals)
noncomputable abbrev upper_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sInf ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MajorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

noncomputable abbrev lower_integral (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ :=
  sSup ((PiecewiseConstantOn.integ · I) '' {g | MinorizesOn g f I ∧ PiecewiseConstantOn g I})

-- Definition 11.3.4 (Riemann integral)
-- As we permit junk values, the simplest definition for the Riemann integral is the upper integral.
noncomputable abbrev integ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (I: BoundedInterval) : ℝ := upper_integral f I

open BoundedInterval

/-- Exercise 11.6.4 -/
 theorem Exercise_11_6_4a : ∃ (f:ℝ → ℝ) (hnon: ∀ x ≥ 0, f x ≥ 0), Summable f ∧ ¬ ∃ M, ∀ N ≥ 0, integ f (Icc 0 N) ≤ M := by
  sorry

end Chapter11
